"Matilda, does that basket with dice and number cards go there?" The educator reminds her that she needs to look at the labels to know where each material goes; the girl observes and stores it in the correct place

"Matilda, look at the label under the basket you just stored, does it correspond to the material you are storing?" The educator shows her where the shadow is; the girl stores the material in the indicated place

"Matilda, I noticed you stored the materials as I requested, but what should you look at to know where they go? Next time, pay close attention to where to store them by observing how your classmates are doing it."

question: A 2nd year secondary teacher, in the unit of Solutions, is explaining colloids. She points out that in this type of mixture there are suspended particles. She complements her explanation by showing images of examples, such as mayonnaise and gelatin. At the end of the class, a student raises his hand and comments: "I don’t understand why mayonnaise and gelatin are colloids if those suspended particles cannot be seen. To me, they are homogeneous mixtures."  What activity would allow the teacher to work on the characteristics of a colloid to answer the student's doubt?
options: Apply the Tyndall effect in a common colloid
Mix a sample of mayonnaise and water and note the observations
Draw the microscopic organization of a common colloid
Compare the molecular structure of a colloid and a solution

question: Consider the following situation: An educator is covering the content about the ocean in the unit on Living Beings and Their Environment. For this, she shows a video in which the sea is seen during a day of swells. One of the children asks why swells occur. Which of the following phenomena should the educator mention as the main cause of the swells?
options: The wind
The gravitational force of the Moon
The differences in atmospheric temperatures
The change in ocean temperatures

question: A teacher in a Grade 6 class aims to teach their students the views of elevation, profile, and plan, so that they can graphically represent the objects they are designing. Which of the following teacher actions would help students understand the process of creating the views?
options: Invite students to imagine a chair "floating" in the corner of the room and illuminate it perpendicularly to the walls and floor. Point out that the shadows seen correspond to each of the views. Then, ask them to answer the following questions: how many views are there? what is the relationship between them?

Ask students to visualize the main view of an object or piece and identify the orthogonal projection on each of the planes, like those found in the classroom. Then, point out that these planes are called the elevation plane, plan plane, and profile plane, with the images projected on each one being the elevation view

Explain to students that the projections of an object are called views, because each projected image approximately matches the view we would have of the object if we looked at it from very far away, with a powerful telephoto lens, in the direction of the projecting rays

Ask students: how many views does an object have? how many of these do we need to represent an object? Then, explain that it all depends on the position of the object in relation to the projection planes, and that in practice the object should always be positioned so that most of its faces are parallel or perpendicular to them

question: A teacher in 1st year of secondary education asks his students to prepare a Gantt chart to plan the service they intend to carry out at the institution. During its preparation, the teacher notices that the students are not correctly calculating the time it will take them to carry out each of the activities and, therefore, they will not be able to complete the preparations for the execution of the service on time. Which of the following interventions by the teacher will allow the students to reorient their work to accomplish the requested task?
options: Show the students an example of a daily activity in which it is required to consider the necessary time to carry out the next one
Ask the students to visualize their project objective and from it derive all the steps to follow to carry out the proposed activity
Present the students with a hypothetical situation where it is observed that the protagonists cannot carry out an activity because they have not done another that is required beforehand
Together with the students, carry out an analysis of the nature of the established activities, relate them to the assigned times, and evaluate whether the latter are appropriate

question: Responding to the needs of their environment, the 8th-grade students have built benches for the playground of the elementary children at the school. Considering the situation described, what evaluation criteria should the teacher consider to assess the product created by the students, according to the recipients?
options: Using low-cost materials or materials that have previously been used, thus reducing resource investment
Employing recyclable materials or those that have a lower environmental impact after the benches' useful life
Selecting durable and resistant materials that allow for construction details that give the benches firmness and stability
Using materials that allow for a harmonious design in line with the school's characteristics

question: Read the following situation: Mateo, a 2nd grade student who has a learning disability, attends a school with an Individualized Education Program. His special education teacher mentions that the student is capable of segmenting syllables, but despite the supports provided, he is unable to identify them within the word. On the other hand, his classroom teacher states that Mateo faces challenges in tasks that involve following a sequence of steps.  According to the information provided, in which aspect does the student require support?
options: Verbal memory
Auditory association
Auditory comprehension
Grammatical integration

question:
Read the following excerpt from an essay submitted by a second-year high school student:
In the novel, one can see the lack of freedom experienced by the characters, especially the protagonist, to do what they want or act according to their true desires. For example, at one point the narrator says that no one can marry someone they truly love because if they are caught they could be imprisoned or executed. This seems very bad to me because one cannot be free even to be with the person they want and, sometimes, in today's society, this can be seen, for example, when people are pressured or pressure themselves to be with someone out of convenience or for appearances or money and not for love.
Which of the following possible comments from a teacher on the student's essay would be appropriate feedback for the use of citations in writing?
options: If you do not include textual citations, your arguments will lose validity
Extend the paragraph with another aspect of the problem and support it with a textual citation from the novel
If instead of referencing the novel you add a textual citation, the point you want to discuss would be more explicit
Re-read the guide that explains what citations consist of and carefully look at the examples presented

question: In an 8th grade class, during the contemporary art unit, a teacher is addressing the topic of installation. To do this, the students have watched videos of different installations, and together they have determined the characteristics of this artistic genre. As a final activity, the students, organized in groups, created an ephemeral installation inside the classroom, using materials brought from their homes. While monitoring the activity, the teacher notices that one of the groups is making random shapes with yarns stuck to the classroom wall.  What misunderstanding regarding the content of installation is evident in the work presented by the students?
options: They do not relate the shapes used to the space chosen for the installation
They do not consider the type of support that the installation should have
They do not use the space applying the diversity of points of view that an installation should have
They do not consider the properties of the material that should be used in an installation

question: A 4th grade basic education teacher is working with her students on understanding the grammatical agreement between the subject and the verb. For this purpose, she has decided to incorporate some activities during the reading of a short text that presents a single protagonist. Which of the following activities would be appropriate for the teacher to incorporate so that students work on the grammatical agreement between the subject and the verb?
options: Underline the actions performed by the protagonist and rewrite them by changing the number of the subject
Select in the text the sentences where the protagonist appears and distinguish the subject from the predicate in each one
Highlight with different colors in the text the actions performed by the protagonist and conjugate them in a different tense
Enclose with a color the subjects of the sentences where the protagonist appears and replace them with the corresponding personal pronoun

question: Read the following situation:  The 2nd grade classroom team is designing a work plan for Sofia, a student who has a diagnosis of intellectual disability and a low capacity to retain information (auditory and/or visual). The student needs to continue progressing in her reading acquisition, so they agree to enhance the student's memory transversally in activities.  According to the above situation, which of the following activities is suitable for achieving the objective proposed by the classroom team?
options: Conduct a game where the student must complete a circuit. If she makes a mistake, she must return to the start
Conduct a game where the student has to repeat sequences dictated by the teacher: as per the model and in reverse
Conduct a syllable lottery where the student identifies on her board the syllable that her teacher dictates and writes on the board
Conduct a game where the student must turn over cards that are face down and make pairs based on the initial sound of the words

question: At the end of a class on the Doppler effect for a first-year secondary class, a teacher conducts an assessment to verify the students' learning. One of the items in this assessment states the following:  In the Doppler effect with sound waves, did the speed of sound propagation change? Explain.  A student responds as follows: "The speed does change because it depends on frequency and wavelength, and both vary in the Doppler effect."  What feedback from the teacher is most appropriate to guide the student's learning so that they can identify and overcome their error?
options: It is not entirely correct to say that the frequency and wavelength of sound change, as this effect only occurs for the observer. Additionally, both change in opposite proportion, thus the product of both magnitudes remains constant

What causes lead to a change in the frequency and wavelength of a sound? Is there any in this case? The speed of sound propagation in the Doppler effect does not vary. Therefore, if the perceived frequency of the sound increases, its wavelength decreases

It is incorrect to say that the speed of propagation changes, as it is constant. In the Doppler effect, the wavelength will vary for the observer, but in the opposite proportion to the change in frequency for the observer. Therefore, the product of both magnitudes is constant

Remember what causes make the speed of sound change. Is there any in this case? Even though for the observer the frequency and wavelength may vary, the speed of propagation does not change. How then does the frequency and wavelength of sound change?

question: A teacher is working on the following objective with 8th-grade students: "Understand the multiplicative combinatorial principle", and she will design an activity to start her class. Which of the following starting activities would allow students to deduce this principle?
options: Determine the total combinations when tossing two coins
Determine the probability of an event involving the toss of a coin and a die
Determine the sample space obtained when tossing two coins, using ordered pairs
Determine the sample space obtained when tossing a coin and a die, using a tree diagram

question: A fourth grade teacher is reading with his students the following excerpt from the story *The Discussion of the Hares,* by Alicia Morel:  Simon the rabbit had a ferocious appetite. He also had a wife, Clarita, and a large family of eight children, because they were "multilittered." On a sunny winter day, one of those where you feel like running and flying across fields and mountains, Simon called his wife to take the family out for fresh air. "Clarita, take advantage of the good weather because it's going to rain tomorrow!" "Are you crazy? Dogs or the fox that lives in the nearby forest could come," she replied, barely poking her nose out of the burrow. "What dogs or foxes! Today is truce day." "Are you sure?" "I'm not brave enough to be up here if today wasn't the day when no hunter can hunt," Simon affirmed, lying back to soak up the sun.  Which of the following questions can the teacher ask for students to make an inference from the text?
options: What was the weather like the day Simon called his wife?
What did you think of Simon's attitude towards Clarita?
Why did Simon want his wife to take the kids out that day?
What feeling does Clarita convey when she replies to her husband?

question: A second grade teacher sets the following class objective: “Predict what happens to a certain volume of water when it is transferred to a different container”. Which of the following activities would be appropriate for students to perform in order to achieve the stated objective?
options: Record what happens to a certain volume of water when it is transferred to a different container, and draw the results in their notebook
Indicate the results they might obtain by transferring a volume of water to three different types of containers, and then verify them
Plan a simple experiment in which they have to measure a volume of water and place it in different containers, recording the observations
Determine that the volume of water remains the same after transferring a certain volume to different containers, and then share their results in plenary

question: A teacher of a kindergarten class has organized the classroom into play zones and has incorporated new sensory materials such as water tables, light, sand, and seeds. The children showed evident enthusiasm for playing with the new resources; however, there were conflicts between them when using them.  Considering the educational level of the children, which of the following strategies fosters harmonious coexistence among the children?
options: The teacher joins the children's play by intervening in the resolution of emerging conflicts and modeling the appropriate use of the materials
The teacher establishes a dialogue space with the children to build agreements on the use of the materials and to resolve conflicts peacefully
The teacher establishes specific rules for the use of the new materials during playtime and constantly reminds them to promote coexistence
The teacher sets up a rotating system by organizing the level into small play groups to prevent conflicts and promote peaceful coexistence among peers

question: A teacher is planning to conduct a sequence of three activities, using sources, so that their second-year high school students can understand the antagonistic nature of the ideologies that guided the superpowers during the Cold War; in this process, they have designed various possible activities.  Which of the following sequences of activities is appropriate for students to achieve the stated goal?
options:
1: On a historical map of the Cold War period, they locate the scenarios where there were indirect military conflicts between the superpowers.

2: Grouped together, they choose one of the identified conflicts and describe the geopolitical changes it caused.

3: They create a synthesis table of the conflicts, highlighting the successes and failures of each of the superpowers in them

1: They observe propaganda posters from the US and the USSR, contrasting the context of creation, purpose, etc.

2: Grouped together, they choose one of the superpowers and create a poster that represents it.

3: They set up a presentation with the posters under the title “USA vs USSR,” to determine through voting which superpower was more powerful

1: They analyze documents from the main intelligence agencies of each of the powers (KGB and CIA), recognizing their purposes.

2: Grouped together, they create a diagram that graphically presents the justifications given by each of the agencies regarding their modes of operation.

3: They create a comparative table on each of the political projects that the intelligence agencies of the powers attempt to defend

1: They consult internet sites seeking sources on the main military strategies of the powers during the Cold War.

2: Grouped together, they read the sources and conclude the main ideas of each.

3: They present their conclusions to the class, highlighting the differences and similarities of the strategies adopted by each of the powers

question: A teacher is designing a written summative assessment for 7th grade in order to collect evidence of the achievement level of the following learning objective: "Explain the behavior of ideal gases in everyday situations, considering the laws that model it." Which of the following indicators accounts for the achievement of the objective?
options: They qualitatively apply Boyle's, Gay-Lussac's, and Charles's laws in everyday examples
They interpret graphs, establishing the relationship between the pressure and temperature variables of a gas
They identify in everyday life the magnitudes of volume and temperature of gases
They calculate the value of the temperature, volume, and pressure of a gas, applying the formula of the ideal gas laws in everyday contexts

question: In a senior High School class on the History of Philosophy, a teacher and her students are working on the philosophical tenets of R. Descartes. Which of the following statements corresponds to a difficulty that students might encounter, related to the understanding of the phrase “I think, therefore I am”?
options: Deduce the existence of the Cartesian cogito
Associate existence with the exercise of methodical doubt
Infer that existence is subject to the activity of the cogito
Relate the existence of the res cogitans with the existence of the res extensa

question: A 7th grade teacher will evaluate if their students "Explain the changes derived from the Neolithic revolution in the human-environment relationship through the analysis of sources."  Which of the following activities would allow the teacher to gather evidence of achieving the indicated objective?
options: Ask the students to individually select online sources about the characteristics of the Neolithic and explain them
Ask the students to work in pairs to analyze different sources about the Neolithic revolution and then present the central ideas of each one
Ask the students to work in pairs to complete a chart about the differences and similarities between two sources related to the political-social organization established during the Neolithic
Ask the students to individually write an argumentative text about the transformations brought about by the productive activities of the Neolithic based on different sources

question: In an 8th grade class, in the first lesson of the unit on Efficient and Sustainable Technological Products, a teacher starts by explaining the concept of sustainability. To help students understand, the teacher decides to illustrate it through an analogy. What analogy could the teacher present to make the concept of sustainability more comprehensible to the students?
options: In a family, they cook a pack of noodles for dinner for four members. Only three of them sit down to eat and, being very hungry, they eat all the noodles. When the missing member arrives, the family uses energy and water again to prepare more noodles for the person who was missing

A farmer of a small garden alternates planting different vegetables with different nutritional needs, such as tomatoes, carrots, and corn one year, and peas, onions, and beets the next, in order to keep the land fertile for future crops

A student has a phone with low battery and starts charging it. Meanwhile, they start playing a game, but the game consumes battery faster than the charger can replenish it. As a result, the phone will turn off after some time, so the student switches to a faster charger

A table manufacturer designs and produces tables in such a way that when marking and cutting the parts from a wooden board, the manufacturer uses the board to its fullest and gives the scraps other uses, reducing the amount of raw material used and promoting this way of production as a brand feature to boost sales

question: A teacher will be working with sixth-grade students on argumentative texts. To exemplify their characteristics, she shows the following advertisement:  LET'S TAKE CARE OF WATER!  LET'S BE RESPONSIBLE IN TIMES OF SCARCITY.  www.inae.gob.h  A student raises their hand and asks why the above advertisement is classified as an argumentative text and not as an informative text, since it provides objective information about the amount of water that is wasted by opening a faucet for 1 minute.  Which of the following interventions by the teacher is a response to the student's inquiry?
options: Because providing information in an advertisement is key for the consumer to know the characteristics of the product being offered to them
Because advertising, like publicity, has the function of convincing, with the intention that the receiver adheres to an idea or consumes a product
Because advertising can provide information as a means of persuasion, that is, as a way of convincing people to adhere to the point of view it presents
Because textual classifications do not always reflect the linguistic complexity of texts, as in this advertisement that serves both to inform and to convince

question: During the unit "The Process of National Emancipation," the teacher has emphasized to his 8th grade students the ability to work with sources so that they can distinguish the background that explains the development of this process. Which of the following activities will allow addressing the objective set for the class?
options: Infer from the Constitution of Spain in 1812, what were the changes resulting from the liberation of King Ferdinand VII
Learn the impressions of the Creoles regarding the formation of the Central Junta of Cádiz
Infer in the biographies of the independence leaders, the milestones that structured the process
Extract in the speeches of the First National Government Junta, the political principles that supported the process

question: A 5th-grade teacher whose class goal is to work with students on applying the concept of a semantic field, proposes reading the following fragment: The vegetation landscape of the Metropolitan Region is characterized by being predominantly mountainous, although in the valleys and basins the acacia steppe develops. In this region, there is a strong retreat of native vegetation due to high urbanization. Due to the high degree of population that the region presents, the landscape has been altered, significantly reducing the natural vegetation, remaining only on the slopes and mountain foothills. Agriculture and forest plantations of exotic species, such as eucalyptus, poplars, and pines, have transformed the region's agricultural landscape. Above 500 meters of altitude, there are sclerophyllous forests characterized by their hard, evergreen foliage that requires greater precipitation (between 400 and 1000 mm) to develop. (National Library of Congress). Using the above fragment, which of the following activities carried out by students would be the most appropriate to work on the above objective?
options: Recognize examples of synonymy in the text
Investigate the meaning of unknown terms
Classify words from the text based on a hyperonym
Group words from the text according to their grammatical category

question: Which of the following mathematical learnings is achieved through serialization?
options: Solving numerical addition and subtraction problems
Recognizing that numbers have a logical order through magnitude differences
Understanding that when graphing a number, this symbol includes all previous numbers
Confirming that despite the order transformations in counting, it will always give the same result

question: Read the following situation: During a class where a teacher explains the different time signatures, they notice that the vast majority of their students cannot audibly distinguish the time signatures that share the same number of beats but differ in their division (2/4 and 6/8, 3/4 and 9/8, 4/4 and 12/8). What activity could the teacher do to help students distinguish these differences?
options: Review sheet music that demonstrates these differences
Modify the time signature of simple melodies using a sheet music editor
Perform a melody in 6/8 with the students, and immediately after, modify its accents to perform it in 3/4
Explain the binary and ternary division of the beat with the help of the mathematical knowledge students have regarding fractions

question: A teacher is standing in front of a class delivering a printmaking unit, and one of her students asks: What type of printmaking is called intaglio and why? Which of the following options contains an answer the teacher might give to clarify the student's question?
options: Woodcut, because the wood block is carved with gouges and parting tools that make deep incisions into the surface of the woodblock matrix
Screen printing, because the ink passes through the fabric matrix in depth to create a print on surfaces like paper, fabric, or others
Etching and aquatint, because the ink is embedded into the grooves created in the metal plate, and therefore, the print is the result of the grooves of the matrix and not its surface
Lithography, because the weight of the stone matrix, combined with the pressure exerted by the printing press, creates an impression where the ink is visible with a slight relief

question: A teacher, in a 1st grade secondary class, conducts a group oral reading of the story El padre, by Olegario Lazo, with the aim of verifying, during the reading, whether the students understand what they are reading.  What reading comprehension strategy would be suitable to address the proposed learning objective?
options: Students summarize what they are reading
Students identify the most important ideas of the story
Students formulate questions about what they read and answer them
Students relate the information from the story to their experiences

question: Read the following situation: Upon entering preschool, Camila has a diagnosis of an intellectual disability, and the classroom team must create an Individual Education Programme to develop pre-reading or initial skills in the reading process, specifically visual perception and discrimination, considering the student's interests. Based on the global reading method, which of the following actions promotes the development of reading skills in its initial stage?
options: Create a personal book with meaningful vocabulary for the student
Associate images of her close relatives, through memory games and lotto
Use photo-word cards to associate the image with the name of the corresponding relative
Globally recognize words, using the names of her close relatives as vocabulary

question: What action represents a greater technical difficulty for a student learning to play an electric keyboard?
options: Playing melody and bass
Playing with dynamic nuances
Playing rallentando
Playing an arpeggio with the left hand

question: Consider the following situation: A teacher in a 7th-grade class wants to develop learning related to expressive resources, especially dynamics and articulation. What prior knowledge should students handle to achieve such learning?
options: Rhythmic reading
Melodic reading
Sound qualities
Instrumental performance

question: Read the following situation:  A sixteen-year-old young man with moderate intellectual disability, severe scoliosis, and low hearing lives with his parents and his older sister, with whom he shares a love for video games and accompanies her to school. He is friendly and sociable, participates in the theater group. He develops eating, hygiene, and clothing habits independently. He recognizes numerals, counts, and uses some coins and bills. He writes his name and transcribes sentences. He works on simple tasks, which he starts and finishes, but he has difficulties solving more abstract problems.  According to the Multidimensional Approach, which of these skills requires the most support for the young man?
options: Adaptive skills
Intellectual skills
Psychomotor skills
Communicative skills

question: An educator makes different figures with popsicle sticks on a cardboard, which he sticks on the board. Then he asks the boys and girls to reproduce the same figures from the board, using the popsicle sticks they have on their tables. What logical-mathematical skill does the educator promote with the described activity?
options: Patterns
Seriation
Spatiality
Conservation

question: A preschool educational team aims in the short term to strengthen effective communication between the establishment and families so that they are informed about the teaching-learning process of the children. Which of the following strategies is appropriate to achieve the stated objective?
options: Invite families to various exhibitions and shows of the projects carried out by the children, showcasing the educational process
Frequently provide families with evaluative reports on the children's learning, difficulties, and support needs
Implement workshops open to the community, addressing various topics, to enhance understanding of the children's development process
Hold regular meetings with families to timely exchange relevant information about the children's learning process

question: At the start of the unit Painting in Contemporary Art, which addresses the symbolic value of works in cultural contexts, a teacher will conduct a diagnostic assessment to determine if their 6th grade students recognize the expressive purpose conveyed by a series of works. Upon reviewing the responses, he notices that most students point out characteristics of line, shape, and color of the paintings, without referencing the ideas they convey. This alerts him that he must work with them on the integration of the symbolic understanding of the images in these works. Which of the following activities helps students understand how to analyze the expressive purpose of a work?
options: The teacher asks students to observe different works of contemporary art and identify the common themes that are repeated in them
The teacher presents a series of works of contemporary art to the students and asks them to recognize the expressive forms and the procedures used by the artists
The teacher presents different works of contemporary art to the students and asks them to write down the sensations that the colors or shapes represented in them produce

The teacher shows students a selection of representative works of contemporary art and asks them to indicate what worldview the artists had based on the analysis of the elements they observed in the works

question: Read the following situation:  A special education teacher uses Piagetian tests to assess a 2nd-grade student who possibly shows learning disabilities in math. Based on the application of the subtest "use of quantifiers", the teacher concludes that the student shows needs for support in handling the concepts involved in that test.  Which of the following strategies addresses the need for support of the student in the described situation?
options: Provide cards with illustrations of successive actions for the student to arrange according to different criteria
Facilitate a numerical series with the support of images for the student to identify similarities and differences
Use cards for the student to make estimates of quantities and use the criteria "more than what" and "less than what"
Use cards with numerals and popsicle sticks so that the student can establish "one-to-one" correspondence according to indicated quantities

question: Read the following situation: After explaining to the students the importance of memory in people's lives, the teacher sets the learning objective of structuring the elements of memory. For this purpose, an investigation activity on the topic is proposed, to be conducted during a two-hour class, in pairs, in the computer room. Class work: 1. Find out what are the components of memory that intervene in the memorization process. 2. Create a diagram in which these components are organized according to their relationships in the process, clearly differentiating each one of them. 3. Accompany your diagram with an explanation, as brief as possible, to clarify how memory is constituted. Which of the following options accurately expresses the evaluation indicator consistent with the activity proposed by the teacher?
options: The students distinguish and describe the components of memory
The students distinguish the components of memory and evaluate their mutual relationships
The students distinguish the components of memory and analyze their mutual relationships
The students distinguish the components of memory and explain each one of them

question: A 5th grade teacher aims for his students to identify, based on a compilation, music that is present in their everyday environment.  What activity is appropriate for the students to achieve the above objective?
options: Create a log of songs based on information extracted from interviews
Research a topic that addresses urban music and its influence on modern society
Recreate or interpret popular songs from a diverse youth repertoire that are representative for them
Conduct a survey of their neighbors and gather information about the musical tastes that exist in their neighborhoods

question: In a class on chemical bonds, the teacher aims for students to identify different types of intermolecular interactions and develop scientific skills. Which of the following activities would allow the teacher to verify the achievement of the goal?
options: Ask students to classify different molecules based on their intramolecular interactions
Ask students to construct a concept map based on reading the intermolecular interactions unit from the textbook
Ask students to analyze the presence of intramolecular interactions in natural substances
Ask students to design an experimental procedure to identify the presence of different types of intermolecular interactions

question: Which of the following activities will allow students to investigate the main cause of global warming?
options: Summarize a publication in a scientific journal about the effects of chlorofluorocarbons (CFCs)
Analyze a scientific article with information about the concentration of CO2 in different seasons of the year in different cities
Summarize the results and conclusions of a thesis work on the increase of CO2 concentration in the atmosphere
Analyze scientific reviews containing information related to the effects of thermal inversion on the concentration of pollutant gases

question: A preschool teacher, during zone play, notices that there is a conflict between Simón and Javiera in the construction zone. She approaches to find out what is happening and realizes that Simón is very angry because Javiera dismantled his construction and Javiera is upset because Simón took the material she was going to use. Taking advantage of this situation, the teacher decides to enhance the following cross-curricular learning objective: "Apply peaceful strategies in resolving everyday conflicts with other children."  What intervention by the teacher would be most appropriate to promote the proposed learning objective?
options: Ask both what happened, how they felt, and whether their actions were peaceful or how they could be. Then, invite them to propose solutions and agreements to resolve the conflict
Ask both what happened and comment on the importance of sharing and resolving conflicts by talking. Then, wait for them to calm down and ask them to apologize and agree on the use of materials
Tell them that their behavior was wrong. Then, ask both how they felt about each other's reaction and invite them to resolve the conflict peacefully, by talking and sharing
Ask both to comment on what happened individually, promoting recognition of the mistake made, and then ask them to apologize and continue working by sharing materials

question: Read the following text, which describes the postulate of an author regarding the functioning of language: Among the different scientific theories that explain the functioning of human language is generative grammar [...], also currently known as biolinguistics. This theory postulates the existence of an innate mental structure that allows the production and comprehension of any statement in any natural language, also making it possible that the process of acquiring and mastering spoken language requires very little linguistic input for its correct functioning and develops almost automatically. To which of the following authors does the postulate described above belong?
options: Chomsky
Skinner
Piaget
Vygotsky

question: Read the following definition:  "They are characterized by significant limitations in both cognitive functioning and adaptive behavior, as manifested in conceptual, social, and practical adaptive skills. This originates before the age of 18" (AAIDD, 2011, p. 25).  AAIDD (2011). Intellectual Disability. Definition, Classification, and Systems of Social Support. Alianza Editorial. Which of the following options corresponds to the previous definition?
options: Functional diversity
Intellectual disability
Concept of intelligence
Autism Spectrum Disorder

question: A teacher in 2nd year of high school is planning the classes in which she will address the objective "Order irrational numbers and represent them on the number line".  Which of the following sequences of activities is relevant for students to work on in class and achieve the objective?
options:
1) Identify problems in mathematical contexts that have no solution in rational numbers.  
2) Determine the square root of prime numbers, analyzing the obtained values.  
3) Approximate to the thousandth the previous roots, by defect and by excess

1) Determine the square root of prime numbers, approximately, using a calculator.  
2) Locate the previous roots on the number line, considering their approximate value.  
3) Locate the previous roots on the number line, using a ruler and compass

1) Construct a square with a side of 1 cm and mark its diagonal.  
2) Construct a new square on the diagonal of the previous square.  
3) Propose strategies to determine the area of the new square and locate its value on the number line

1) Determine the square root of prime numbers with an algorithm, approximating its value.  
2) Check the inequality relationships between the previous roots, considering that the square root function is increasing.  
3) Locate the previous roots on the number line, using a geometric program

question: In a 1st Grade (high school) class, to develop content related to photography, a teacher presents images that exemplify different photographic angles and frames. After observing and analyzing the images, she asks the students to work on the content by taking photographs in different places around the school. This activity will be formatively evaluated using a rating scale. Considering the task given, which of the following indicators is appropriate to include in the instrument?
options: They apply various angles and frames in relation to their expressive purposes
They identify the angles and frames in the school's infrastructure
They apply the different angles and frames in their photographs
They identify types of angles and frames to be used in the composition of their photographs

question: Maria is a student with Autism Spectrum disorder who attends 6th grade in a school with inclusive education. Generally, the student has adequate relationships with her peers, interacting with them during recess and at the end of the day, but during classes she appears shy and withdrawn. She responds to communicative roles and introduces conversational topics, but is unable to maintain them due to a lack of coherence and cohesion in her statements. Gradually, she has managed to strengthen her reading and writing processes, showing greater fluency. In the subject of History, Geography, and Social Sciences, the teacher observes low comprehension from Maria regarding the activities planned for the learning objective: Distinguishing some actors of Chile's political and democratic organization, referring to its presidents and periods. Therefore, she decides to formulate a diversified strategy, considering the challenges that Maria presents. Which of the following strategies addresses, in a diversified manner, the learning objective that the teacher is working on in this case?
options: The teacher will request the class to create a timeline, indicating the presidents and the most significant events of each period, and she will support Maria by providing images related to the figures and events presented in that timeline

The teacher will show a timeline to introduce the presidents and their events, through a data show presentation: students will have to complete a guide with the information presented and share their answers with their peers

The teacher will request each student to make a presentation regarding a chosen president, according to their tastes and interests; in these presentations they must comment on characteristics and represent some of the main events that occurred during his governance with images

The teacher will adapt the classroom and organize groups to create a personification of each president, along with the representation of the main events in their periods; then, she will invite them to choose a president and create a brief biography, song, or poem representing them, according to their contribution to the country

question:
Read the following situation:
Camila is in 5th grade and receives support from the integration department, due to the special educational needs derived from her diagnosis of intellectual disability. Currently, Camila decodes medium-length texts, however, she has difficulties at the comprehension level, especially in extracting the main idea. In the subject of Language and Communication, the teacher is working on the goal: "independently reading and comprehending non-literary texts", in this case, the news. Considering the stated objective, which of the following activities is the most appropriate for developing information processing skills in Camila?
options:
Read the news and then make a summary considering the most significant events
and the most relevant actors in the text

Read the news using the "highlight with marker" strategy, highlighting the main concepts and
events addressed in the text

Read the news with the guidance of "guiding questions" that facilitate data collection,
identification of the problem, relationships with experiences, among others

Read the news and create a conceptual map from the relationships established between the
most relevant concepts and the main events described in the text

question:
Read the following situation:
In a 5th/6th elementary class, the Music teacher invites the students to use the space of the room to move freely while listening to a march. They are asked to mark the pulse and accents they can perceive with their bodies. Then, in groups, they agree on a movement to highlight the accents of the music: it can be bending down, raising an arm, etc. They listen to the music again and each group performs their movement one by one so that everyone can observe each other. What evaluative tool should the teacher use to verify that the students are discriminating the elements of musical language?
options: Rubric
Checklist
Anecdotal record
Rating scale

question: Read the following situation:  A 4th-grade teacher at a special school serving students with intellectual disabilities has noticed that several of her students face significant challenges when it comes to expressing their ideas and recounting everyday situations, as their vocabulary is limited. Therefore, she decides to implement strategies to address the needs identified in her students.  Based on the above situation, which of the following strategies is relevant to respond to the support needs presented by the students?
options: Intend to describe objects, pictures, places, characters, and others that are of interest to the class group
Propose the representation of texts such as tongue twisters, rhymes, and poems related to significant elements
Assign the preparation of presentations on significant topics for the students, such as animals and hobbies
Request the reproduction of stories, micro-stories, legends, and other literary texts that are of interest to the students

question: A teacher of a 3rd grade has been working with her students on the objective "Observe and describe, through experimental research, the needs of plants." To assess this objective, she proposes the following experimental research: In three transparent cups with drainage holes that contain the same quantity and type of garden soil, they sow the same quantity of seeds per cup. Then, they place the cups on the classroom window and water them with a syringe of 10 mL of water with the following frequency: Plant 1: once a day. Plant 2: once a week. Plant 3: twice a week. Finally, she asks them to record the observations and conclusions obtained. Which of the following performances indicates that the students were able to obtain the relevant conclusions from the experiment carried out according to the variable that changes?
options: They relate light with the water plants need to survive
They identify the nutrients that water provides to the growth of plants
They determine the amount of water a plant needs for its development
They explain the function of the roots in absorbing the water that the plant needs

question: Read the following situation:  The 2nd grade classroom team, in the subject of Mathematics, observes that a student diagnosed with a learning disability in calculation shows difficulty in finding two numbers that, when added together, result in a specified amount. For example, when asked to find two numbers that sum to twelve. The student also faces challenges in learning when given a quantity and asked how much more is needed to reach another quantity, for example, when asked to determine how many candies are needed to reach ten, if they only have three.  Which of the following strategies is appropriate to address the challenges faced by the student?
options: Work on ascending series through the representation of numbers on a number line
Work on the classification of numerical operations based on recognizing their characteristics
Develop the reversibility of operations through a results-checking table
Develop the unique correspondence of numbers by pairing related elements from the environment

question: The Learning Resource Center of a school has various didactic materials to promote language development. A teacher of preschool must select a resource to work on syntactic awareness with her group of boys and girls. Considering the previous situation, what is the most appropriate resource to directly work on the development of syntactic awareness?
options: Sheets with verbal opposites
Domino of initial vowel sound
Cards for image-word association
Cards to build sentences with pictograms

question: In which of the following options is the schematic stage of children's drawing characterized?
options: There is a more defined concept of shape, object-color relationship, and actions are included in the drawings
The human figure appears in the shape of a tadpole, details begin to be included, and there is juxtaposition
There is a main figure and the space is everything that surrounds this figure, other elements float around it
The strokes are controlled, there is representational intent, but little object-color relationship

question: A teacher in the 4th year of secondary education is working on the unit "Technological resources in our musical environment". One of the objectives defined in their planning is for students to know and identify different sound effects applied to voice and musical instruments (reverb, delay, etc.). Which of the following options presents the resource with the greatest educational possibilities according to the learning objective designed by the teacher?
options: A sustain pedal
A MIDI instrument
Audio editing software
Selected song audio tracks

question:
In the unit Efficient Use of Resources, from the 2nd year of high school, a group of students proposes the creation of a manual with different solutions to help selected families from the educational community reduce the cost and consumption of resources such as water, gas, and electricity, and then check if these proposed solutions were useful.
Which set of actions carried out by the group of students indicates that they are capable of evaluating the economic impact of their solution?
options: The students send an online survey to each family one month after delivering the manual of solutions, asking which of the proposals were implemented in their homes, what difficulties they encountered, and which could be adopted as habits

The students ask the families for their consumption bills, and then they deliver the manual. After a month, they request another bill to compare the results. With this data, they analyze the impact of their solutions

One month after distributing the manual, the students conduct interviews with the families, asking about each member's experience with the application of the proposed solutions. From there, they draw conclusions about the economic impact generated by the solution

Before delivering the manual, the students ask the families: What practices might be causing higher resource consumption in your homes? One month after delivering the manual, they ask again: Which of the proposed solutions helped reduce the expenses?

question: Consider the following situation: In the unit of "Music and sound environment", a music teacher asks his students, as homework, to inform themselves about therapeutic and harmful uses of sound, ultrasound, and infrasound. What objective does the teacher pursue with this task?
options: To explore qualities and forms of sound production
To bring students closer to the sound environment from an aesthetic perspective
To identify the audible spectrum that humans can perceive
To make his students aware of the scope of the sound reality

question:
A teacher designs an instrument to evaluate the production of an informative article about the historical context of an epic.
Which of the following indicators allows for evaluating the writing process, specifically the coherence of the informative article?
options: Fulfill the purpose of informing about the historical context of the epic
Present the information in independent paragraphs that address the historical context of the epic
Establish meaningful connections between the ideas that address the historical context of the epic
Provide descriptions that include relevant information about the historical context of the epic

question: A group of 8th-grade students must conduct research related to the history of music, with a requirement to work digitally. For this, they request support from the Technology teacher. Which of the following digital tools could the teacher present as the most suitable for editing the information of the research?
options: Canva
Instagram
WordPress
Google Chrome

question: During a history, geography, and social sciences class for 4th grade students, aimed at understanding the technological and scientific legacy of the Inca culture, the classroom team decides to review with the students a historical source associated with this topic. While monitoring the activity, the special education teacher observes that a group of students faces challenges in understanding the written document due to lack of context.  In light of this, which of the following strategies is the most appropriate to work on before reviewing the historical source?
options: Project a video related to the topic that complements the content of the historical source
Work on key words present in the historical source to enhance the students' vocabulary
Model strategies for inferring information from explicit elements of the historical source
Provide information from another Latin American culture to determine the differences with the historical source read

question: A teacher is working with their 3rd-year high school students on the concept of specific heat, in the context of the unit Heat and temperature. Which of the following everyday examples allows introducing this concept?
options: Upon taking out a can of drink that has been in the refrigerator for a long time, water droplets form on its surface
No matter how strong the applied flame is, the temperature of a pot with boiling water will not increase as long as there is water in the pot
After some time of serving a stew, the potato is still very hot to eat, while the meat can be eaten without problems
When leaving a bottle painted black and another similar one painted white in the sun, both with water inside, the black one reaches a higher temperature than the white one

question: Read the following situation: The teacher of a 2nd grade class in a school with Integrated Educational Programs wants to work on the following learning objective: "Identify the role that services such as transportation, communication, and the market play in benefiting the community." To this end, it must be considered that there are two students in the class with intellectual disabilities, who are characterized by performing their work functionally. Considering the characteristics of the class, which of the following activities respond to differentiated teaching?
options: The teacher shows a video about some community services and asks the students to choose which one they would like to tackle first; in the chosen order, the teacher provides information with slides so that they can later produce a poster using various materials

The teacher shows cards with different images of transportation, communication, and market services, asking the students to form groups and choose one to gather more information about it. Once the information is collected, they should give a brief presentation using models and posters

The teacher explains what some services such as transportation, market, and communication are and invites the students to watch a video about them. Afterwards, they write a summary of what they mean in their notebooks. Finally, they give a presentation about the reviewed services using audiovisual materials

The teacher presents different everyday problems such as transportation or information issues and asks the children to choose the one they have experienced the most. The students must investigate possible solutions by consulting with their families or community, then generate proposals with specific, realistic solutions

question:
A 5th-grade teacher shows her students Chilean paintings from different periods, explaining the multiple ways in which artists apply color, form, and lighting. Then, she challenges them to choose a painting, different from the ones observed, to individually analyze the same elements of visual language taught. Finally, the students present their analyses to the class. The teacher evaluates the activity using a rubric.
Why is using this strategy suitable to evaluate the objective stated in the previous activity?
options: Because it involves a performance with different dimensions that should be distinguished to provide feedback to the student
Because the rubric allows for multiple combinations of scores and weightings
Because the rubric allows evaluation without grading or making a judgment about the student's product, but rather about the process they carried out
Because the rubric allows selecting the best performance from the analyses conducted by the students

question: An educator in a preschool setting observes that Ana decides to work with a set of farm animal figures and separates them into three groups that she calls "large farm animals", "medium farm animals" and "small farm animals". On the other hand, Pedro selects building blocks and arranges them by placing small red block, large blue block, small red block, large blue block. Considering the performance of these children, what skill are they developing, respectively?
options: Ana classifies by one criterion and Pedro creates a pattern with two variable attributes
Ana classifies by two criteria and Pedro creates a pattern with one stable and one variable attribute
Ana establishes correspondence between two groups and Pedro creates a pattern with two stable attributes
Ana establishes correspondence between two groups and Pedro creates a pattern with one stable and one variable attribute

question: In a 7th grade classroom, during the unit on Myths and creation stories, a teacher is working on the following learning objective: "Analyze the role of characters in creation myths." To achieve this, he has chosen a selection of stories created by different cultures.  Which of the following activities is suitable for students to address this objective?
options: Create cards mentioning the characters of each myth and the culture to which they belong
Create diagrams distinguishing main, secondary, and incidental characters from the selected myths
Create tables in which they relate some characters from each myth with the realities explained in these narratives
Write brief texts in which they inform about the characteristics of the main characters of the selected myths

question: A teacher has planned to work on the objective of graphically representing the variation of pH in a controlled neutralization in their class. Therefore, the teacher asks the senior year students to construct the titration curve of a weak acid with a strong base, for which the concentrations and volumes of both substances are provided. As the exercise progresses, the teacher observes that most students have only managed to construct the curve up to the equivalence point, because they obtain negative pH values after that point. Which of the following remedial activities would allow the teacher to redirect the students to complete the construction of the titration curve?
options: Take the students to the laboratory so they can experimentally identify the equivalence point
Explain to them, using a pH scale, that it is constructed based on positive values of acid or base concentration
Explain to them, with an example, how to determine the number of excess moles of each substance at different points during the titration
Use software where the student enters the volumes of added base, and the system provides the points to construct the curve

question: A teacher of an 8th-grade class will work with students on the following objective: "Develop investigations by adequately evaluating the consulted sources of information." To do this, a project is proposed which consists of choosing an author of their interest to research about their work and then present the work to their classmates.  Which of the following activities promotes in students the appropriate search for information for the realization of the indicated projects?
options: Ask them to review the information they have in relation to their objectives to find quotes that support their conclusions
Guide the search for information based on the formulation of questions about any of their books, for example: how is the author's particular style reflected in this work?
Look for books by the chosen authors or, if they have not chosen, suggest one based on a genre of their interest to have a broader view of the theme

Propose the use of a checklist in the texts found, based on indicators such as: "Is it written clearly?", "Does it identify its author or responsible institution?", "Does it present technical language about the topic?"

question: Read the following situation:  In Prekindergarten, a student with severe hearing impairment who does not use technical assistance participates. He comes from a family of hearing individuals and therefore does not have command of sign language; he only uses some gestures and communicates through unintelligible speech. The special education teacher proposes to the father and mother of the child to participate in a workshop to learn sign language, but they reject this invitation, arguing that their child will develop oral language through constant interaction with other boys and girls throughout his school life.  According to the situation presented, in which areas should support for the student be prioritized?
options: Communicative and health
Communicative and social
Communicative and family
Communicative and cognitive

question: One of the objectives worked on by the 2nd year secondary students is to "Organize and interpret data related to the physicochemical properties of organic compounds." What of the following activities allows for the evaluation of the aforementioned objective?
options: Deliver to the students data on the boiling points of hydrocarbons of different chain lengths and ask them to analyze whether they are in a solid, liquid or gaseous state at room temperature

Ask the students to investigate the boiling points of hydrocarbons of different chain lengths, organize them in a table, and analyze whether they are in a solid, liquid, or gaseous state at room temperature

Provide the students with data on the boiling points of hydrocarbons of different chain lengths arranged in a table and have them interpret the behavior according to the interactions presented by the molecules

Provide the students with data on the boiling points of hydrocarbons of different chain lengths, which they should organize in a table according to the number of carbons in the chain and then interpret this behavior according to the magnitude of the interactions presented by the molecules

question: In the context of the unit "Evaluation and functioning of the solution", the teacher of an 8th grade class plans one of his lessons, with the purpose of redesigning the selected technological product, based on the information gathered in user tests. Which of the following skills should the teacher reinforce beforehand to address the stated objective?
options: Apply graphic expression and representation methods
Identify basic elements and resources of graphic design
Know data analysis techniques and draw conclusions
Know information collection instruments and their characteristics

question: In the process of developing a service, the teacher decides to introduce the use of the Gantt chart through an activity. Which of the following activities would help students understand the purpose and use of the Gantt chart in their service projects?
options: Provide students with a definition of a Gantt chart along with examples of its use in different projects
Together with the students, model the ordering of activities for the creation of an object or service, given in disorder, in a Gantt chart
Present a service to be developed and ask students to establish actions, the deadlines involved, and organize them in a Gantt chart
Show students examples of the use of different types of Gantt charts in various goods and services projects, pointing out the advantages of each

question: A 2nd year secondary teacher is planning the classes for the Kinematics unit. In one of them, he will conduct an activity that will address the content of the class, responding to the curriculum emphasis on the Nature of Science. Which of the following activities to be carried out by students most appropriately responds to this curriculum emphasis?
options: They explain how the concept of gravitational acceleration applies to solving everyday problems
They explain how the concept of gravitational acceleration has evolved throughout human history
They explain how advances in technology have allowed for greater accuracy in measuring the value of gravitational acceleration
They explain how free fall movement of an object originates and how this movement depends on gravitational acceleration

question: In a 7th grade, in the instrumental ensemble workshop, the teacher has been working on the interpretation of an arrangement for two voices and instrumental accompaniment. The students, in groups, presented their interpretation of the piece considering the criteria of rhythmic precision, harmonic and melodic accuracy, and correct use of expressive resources and instrumental technique. At the end of the presentations, the teacher detects that one group of students makes duration errors in some figures, executing gallops instead of syncopations when these appear in the score.  What formative feedback from the teacher would enable the students to overcome the error and meet the established criteria for the interpretation of the piece?
options: It is undeniable that they have markedly improved the execution of the piece; however, there is always room to perfect musical reading. What aspect of the interpretation of the piece do you find weakest?

I congratulate you on the correct execution of the musical piece. Next, I invite you to listen to an audio of the piece, focusing on the metric and tempo aspects, which are the ones that still need improvement

During the interpretation, you have reached a great capacity for concentration, congratulations! Regarding rhythmic aspects, it is necessary to correct the execution of the gallops when interpreting them together

The harmonic accompaniment and melodic precision have reached a good interpretative level. Regarding the rhythmic aspect, could you distinguish which subdivisions of the bar appear in the score and which one you executed in your interpretation?

question: A 1st year secondary teacher, in a class about the factors that influence photosynthesis, will address the formulation of hypotheses with his students. Which of the following pedagogical actions would directly promote achieving this goal?
options: Provide the results of a research study on the effect of light intensity along with the respective hypothesis, so that students can validate or reject it
Deliver an experimental design where a plant is illuminated with different wavelengths and, based on the analysis of the experiment, students identify the variables under study
Pose the following research question: “How does temperature and humidity influence photosynthesis?” so that students identify the variables and the relationship between them
Provide different hypotheses for the following research question: “How does the concentration of CO2 affect the photosynthetic rate?” so that students can select the most appropriate one

question:
A teacher presents to her 8th-grade students, as the class topic, the consequences of excessive screen use on school performance. After introducing the topic, she asks the students to search for information on it on the Internet.
What suggestion can she give to the students so they can obtain precise information about the topic posed on different digital search engines?
options: Search for preliminary sources and other databases, opting for those belonging to recognized or representative organizations, trying first to find the main websites by their domain name: .org (organization), .gov (government and official agencies) and .edu (education). Use a phrase that correctly delimits what is being searched for, or specify words in the search engine, in order to restrict the results

Start with a broad search of results, using conjugated words, for example: using screens, without quotes. Then narrow down the search by writing more detailed phrases, for example: using screen for more than 8 straight hours. And configuring the country, language, keywords, and the topic's update period. Place the phrase in quotes and use the signs + and –, to add a keyword or remove those we don't want

Choose an efficient and fast search engine (remember that the more databases a search engine accesses, the slower it is). Then search for the information by selecting the country about which you want to find out and the category of the topic, for example: science, technology, entertainment, sports. For the search, use generic words, do not use accents or capitals; if you know how an expression should appear, type it as such enclosed in quotes, with all the words

First create a work plan that contains, in one of the stages of the process, the collection and gathering of references on the topic being investigated. Upon reaching this stage, go to the sources of information, such as books, magazines, documents, databases on the Internet, where the topic is seriously analyzed. Pay close attention to capitals and lowercase letters when writing the articles. Be concerned with accents in the word or phrase to be searched for, to specify the search and narrow the results

question: At the end of the topic of Boyle's Law in a 7th grade class, a teacher needs to evaluate the following learning indicator: "Identify the relationship established between variables in Boyle's Law". Which activity would allow this teacher to gather evidence about the achievement of this learning indicator?
options: Ask students to explain the working mechanism of a pressure cooker
Ask students to explain the working mechanism of a hot air balloon
Ask students to calculate the final volume of a gas contained in a container at different heights
Ask students to explain the volume variation experienced by a bubble as it rises from the bottom of a pool

question: As part of an activity in the unit "Heat and Temperature", a teacher proposes the following reading to her Second Year High School students: "In the 17th century, many mining operations had reached such depths that pumping the accumulated water at the bottom had become a serious problem and the pumps that extracted the water had to be powered by horses or people. At this time, scientists like Torricelli or von Guericke made important advances related to atmospheric pressure, the existence of a vacuum, and the so-called "motive power of fire". The first to attempt to build a machine to lift water based on these advances was Papin, although his design was never constructed. More success was had by Savery, who developed a machine where a vacuum was created as the steam condensed. In 1712, Newcomen built a more practical machine, which, by not requiring such high steam pressure, was much safer, and was used for seventy years without requiring substantial improvements. The next step was taken by Watt, who, knowing Black's discoveries about latent heat, had the idea of separating the condenser from the body of the apparatus, constructing the first machine with this design in 1776. Watt's machine, cheaper and more efficient, ended up being used, in addition, in heavy metallurgy and the textile industry. This machine was exported from England to countries aspiring to industrialization, such as France, Germany, and Russia. This technology was the starting point for a revolution that continues to this day and enabled the development of more recent technological applications." Which of the following tasks can students perform from the reading of the previous text?
options: Describe the operation of the steam engine designed by Watt
Critically analyze scientific models, seeking their advantages and limitations in describing natural phenomena
Develop examples, representations or analogies that allow them to link the contents of Physics with the understanding of everyday world phenomena
Indicate how a dynamic and evolutionary process of knowledge construction is accounted for, and whose development is linked to the socio-historical context

question: In the unit “Opportunities and challenges of technology today”, the Technology teacher assigns the class a task consisting of observing, in their immediate environment, examples of work automation and identifying its effects on people's quality of life. One of the groups has conducted a thorough investigation, with interviews and graphic records on site, covering the strike of the workers at a branch of a supermarket chain, whose demands focus on the loss of jobs due to the automation of cash registers and other roles related to distribution and logistics. The group makes a very complete and illustrative presentation, revealing a very bleak perception of the situation by the interviewees. Once the presentation is concluded, what comment from the teacher, to provide formative feedback on the group's work, would be more aligned with the expected learning outcomes of the course?
options: “I congratulate you on your commitment to the local environment and I invite you to appreciate the positive side of this situation: what advantages does automating these services bring to consumers?”
“Very good teamwork, you have vividly covered the focus on the consequences of automation. In what other productive area do you think similar situations occur?”

“You have clearly reflected a difficult and nearby reality that represents the negative aspects of technology. I propose that we link our next project with it: what productive activity can we develop to include the neighbors who need to retrain?”

“You have gathered very valuable information to reveal one of the unavoidable impacts of automation. Given this reality, what challenges are posed for society in terms of training current workers and educating future generations?”

question: Which of the following activities allows assessing whether students in the initial reading stage identify phonemes in words with direct syllables?
options: The teacher gives the word "mariposa" and the students must note down the number of syllables it has
The teacher gives a set of letters and the students must sort them and form words with them
The teacher plays "A letter has arrived" with the students using words that start with different phonemes
The teacher pronounces the sound of each letter of the word "pala" and the students must recognize which word it corresponds to

question: Read the following situation:  In the 2nd Year of Middle School, the Mathematics teacher works on the following learning objective: Interpret given information to graph functions. To develop this objective, the teacher suggests using different software programs. Among the students in the course, there is Felipe, who is blind and receives access accommodations.  Considering the above, which of the following resources is most suitable to work with Felipe on the proposed objective?
options: Use Braille
Use JAWS software
Use EDICO software
Use accessibility mode functions

question: Read the following situation:  Nicole is a 2nd-grade student, integrated into a school with an Inclusive Education Program due to a diagnosis of mixed Specific Language Impairment. She faces challenges in learning new concepts and following instructions, as well as challenges in understanding the statements and the structure of the operations of the problems in the subject of Mathematics. Despite the various strategies implemented by the classroom team, she requires more support to achieve the learning objectives.  Which of the following curricular adaptations responds to the support requirements presented by the student?
options: Supplement the activities with the use of visual schemes during their development
Flexibilize the monthly planning by providing more time to achieve the skills
Organize the contents to work on, progressively addressing the more complex ones
Decrease the number of exercises and gradual ones from the one that presents simpler statements to more complex

question:
In the framework of achieving better utilization of energy resources, a teacher of a 6th-grade class guides her students in creating different objects. One of the groups built a "magic pot", aiming to optimize the use of gas when cooking food. The teacher decides to create a specific evaluation indicator for the following objective: "Test and evaluate the effectiveness of their technological product".
Which of the following indicators would show that the students are achieving the stated objective?
options: They specify the functions and usage characteristics that the "magic pot" must meet in relation to energy savings
They analyze known systems (such as the "magic pot") that use unconventional energy and achieve efficient energy management
They experimentally determine the differences in energy expenditure between the "magic pot" and other similar cooking methods
They create a simple "magic pot" and relate its operation to the use of unconventional energy and efficient energy management

question: Which of the following questions is appropriate to promote the development of metacognition among students during a group reading with the class?
options: How can you discover the meaning of unknown words?
What is the main idea of the paragraph we just read?
What title would you give to the text we are reading?
What is the topic of what we just read?

question: A teacher will work with her 5th grade students on the skill of playing instruments and singing in harmony, for the group arrangement of a Latin American musical piece.  According to the previous situation, what exercise is relevant that the students should have done beforehand to start developing polyphonic work?
options: Reading of polyrhythmic exercises, tapping and pounding
Sung execution of a short two-part canon accompanied by recorders
Singing of harmonic sequences in choir, where each group sings one note of the chord
Group vocalization work with simple motifs and articulated instrumental execution

question: Read the following situation:  The head teacher of a 5th grade class in a school with an Inclusive Education Program wants to enrich the social relationships in his class. Among his students is Raol, who has congenital blindness. Before planning a strategy, the teacher asks the class to answer the following question: how do I feel with my classmates?  Raol responds: "I don't feel good in the class, it is very noisy. I don't know who is talking to me, they do it very quickly and I struggle to understand them. I feel alone."  Which of the following strategies promotes the enrichment of social relationships in this class and the inclusion of Raol?
options: Show the class, in a reflection day, testimonies from people with disabilities
Conduct role-playing games with the class, modeling the necessary interactions among students
Ask all class members to verbally express how they feel in their class
