Considering the children's age, which of the following stories indicates difficulties in oral expression?
options: “First we went to see my aunt Tantisca. Then to the piazza to play.”
“I watched a movie with my dad. We went to the cinema. Then I had ice cream with your sister.”
“I went to the beach, we made sand castles. There were people sunbathing and running.”
“I went with my mom to a super big park. There was a game to climb high.”

question: A teacher, for the unit "The Independence of Chile", is developing the following plan: Learning Objective: Explain the development of the Chilean independence process, considering actors and factions that faced each other, notable men and women, advances and setbacks of the patriot cause, and some significant events. Class Objective: Distinguish the positions of patriots and royalists in relation to the process of emancipation. Thought skill promoted: Critical thinking. Which of the following possible activities is consistent with the purposes of the class and could be included in the plan?
options: Complete a graphic organizer with the stages of Independence
Construct a mural journal with the description of the heroes of Independence
Create three memes highlighting the ideas of different social actors in the independence process
Make a two-minute video recreating one of the events that occurred during Independence

question: A teacher, in a 2nd year secondary class, in the unit about lyrical texts from the Golden Age, is working on the following objective: "Understand the lyrical motive in different poems." To do this, he reads together with the students the following sonnet:  Love begins with restlessness, request, fervors, and insomnia; grows with risks, lances, and claims; sustains itself with cries and pleas. Doctrinal lukewarmness and thaw, maintains being among deceitful veils, until with grievances or jealousy it extinguishes its fire with its tears. Its beginning, its middle and end is this: so why, AlciNo, do you feel the deviation of Celia, who once loved you well? What reason is there that pain costs you? For love did not deceive you, my AlciNo, but the precise term arrived.  (Sor Juana Inés de la Cruz).  Several students comment that they cannot understand the lyrical motive of the poem because they have a vocabulary that is unknown to them.  What activity should the teacher propose so that the students can understand the lyrical motive of the poem?
options: Students highlight the words that hinder understanding of the poem and discuss their possible meanings with their peers
Students assign possible meanings to the difficult words and verify them with the help of the teacher
Students reflect on the meanings they assign to the difficult words and write sentences with them
Students select the words that are difficult to understand and replace them with others that, according to their prior knowledge, have similar meanings

question: Read the following situation:  The second-grade teacher wants to develop reading comprehension skills in her students through stories read by an adult. In the class is Sofia, a student with Down syndrome. Sofia's language is intelligible; she has a good understanding of simple sentences but shows challenges in her working memory.  Which of the following strategies directly supports Sofia's working memory in relation to reading comprehension?
options: Listening to a familiar story with the story altered, so that students can correct it and provide real information about it
Answering questions about a brief text and responding through a drawing, so that students can express what they understood
Giving opinions about the characters and situations in images to relate the content of the picture to the students' own experiences
Drawing the cover of the complementary reading book read at home, to promote the class's ability to understand the text's content

question: A teacher is addressing the Immune System unit with a 7th grade class. In one of the classes, she plans to address the following objective: "Understand the mechanism of the tertiary barrier." What distinction is essential for the students to be able to make before addressing the objective set for the class?
options: Between antibody and antigen
Between functions of B and T lymphocytes
Between tolerance and immunological memory
Between adaptive and innate immunity

question: A teacher is developing the unit People and Land in an 8th grade class, considering the objective: "Create an autobiographical cartography based on perceptions, feelings, and ideas from their life." To create the cartography, the teacher proposes using the collage technique. What sequence of activities is most suitable to guide students in creating their autobiographical cartography?
options: Create an autobiographical collage, analyze its different areas, draw the cartography, and incorporate fragments of the collage
Incorporate photographs into a cartography, include painting and drawing, analyze the geographical places covered in it
Create an autobiographical text, analyze different types of cartographies, draw the cartography, and incorporate printed images and painting
Reflect on their autobiography, collect and paste documents onto a large sheet, draw the cartography, and incorporate painting

question: Read the following situation: A 2nd grade elementary teacher showed his/her students a map of the Northern, Central, and Southern zones of Chile. Then, he/she presented landscapes of these zones and asked them to circle in red the landscapes of the north; in green, those of the center; and in black, those of the south. For which of the following objectives is the previous activity most pertinent?
options: Classify landscapes according to geographical zone
Compare geographical zones according to their landscapes
Identify characteristics of the geographical zones
Locate landscapes according to the geographical zone

question:
A teacher aims for her class to recognize Hund's rule of maximum multiplicity.
Which of the following assessment tasks would best verify whether the students have achieved this objective?
options: To write global electronic configurations of different atoms in the ground state
To write abbreviated electronic configurations of different atoms in the ground state
To compare the electron distribution in orbital diagrams of the valence shell of different atoms
To compare external electronic configurations of different atoms in both ground and excited states

question: A teacher, in a 2nd year secondary class, will conduct research with the students about the literature of the Golden Age, focusing on the characteristics and historical context of that period. After a search on the internet, the students will compile an extensive bibliography, and then select the most appropriate texts according to the purpose of the research. Which of the following activities proposed by the teacher would be the most suitable for the selection of the researched texts?
options: Create bibliographic records based on the consulted sources and then determine which source will be worked on
Conduct a general reading of the texts to identify key ideas and then evaluate the relevance of the information according to the purpose of the research
Apply strategies to determine the topic and main idea of each consulted source and then group the information according to subtopics, according to the research purpose
Make summaries with the central information extracted from the sources and then create graphic organizers with the information that is considered most relevant for the research

question: Read the following situation: Students from a 6th grade class begin a unit aimed at "Developing the aesthetic appreciation of visual works." To do this, they work in pairs visiting different websites of contemporary art museums on the Internet, select a work and search for information about it. In order to guide the analysis, the teacher provides them with an ordered sequence of activities in three steps. Which of the following sequences allows achieving the stated goal?
options: Describe the image; identify elements of visual language; analyze its form and content
Reflect on its form and content; analyze the elements of visual language; describe the image
Explain the form and content of the image, reflect on the elements of visual language; know the biography of the artist
Know the biography of the artist; analyze its form and content; identify elements of visual language

question: In relation to play as an organized physical activity, at what age does one of the essential characteristics of play correspond to individual participation rather than cooperative?
options: 6 years old
8 years old
10 years old
12 years old

question: In a summative evaluation, the Biology teacher includes the following question: 'What physiological alterations would be observed in a woman who has been administered a drug that blocks the action of GnRH? Describe them.' Which of the following assessment indicators is being addressed with the designed item?
options: Describe the hormonal action responsible for pubertal changes
Describe the control of hypothalamic hormones over the ovarian and uterine cycle
Describe the sequence of events in the ovarian cycle with reference to the follicular phase, ovulation, and luteal phase
Describe the sequence of events in the uterine cycle in relation to menstruation, the proliferative phase, and the secretory phase

question:
A teacher for 2nd year high school is planning a learning unit with the objective "To project scenarios of possible positive or negative impacts of current technological innovations in personal, social, environmental, legal, economic, or other realms." For the first class, they will address the following learning objective: "To infer impacts from past technological innovations."
Which of the following activities would allow the teacher to address the objective they will work on in the first class?
options: Show students a video that displays the invention and advancement of different technological objects and some of the effects they have had on current society, and ask them to point out those that have had the most negative impact
Explain to students the positive and negative effects of the compass and then ask them to investigate other objects that have had similar impacts based on this
Show students a video about the steam engine and how it evolved and led to new inventions. Then ask them to mention the effects it has caused over its advancement through time

Present to students the development of a technological object, such as the telegraph, and its effects on current society. Then ask them to complete a chart in which they classify the positive and negative effects

question:
In the Technology class, 5th-grade students are building a scale model of a car designed by them, using various materials and tools, including some for cutting.
What is the first instruction the teacher should give to the students for them to use the cutting tools safely?
options: Organize the workplace before carrying out the cutting task
Select a solid and regular surface to make cuts on
Check that the tools are in good condition and have the appropriate edge
Choose the cutting tool based on the task and the material they will work with

question: A teacher in 6th grade has formulated the following objective for a diagnostic assessment: "To represent through drawing and the volume of the human body." As a central activity of the class, she has tasked the students with making three sketches of a human figure: one of a child, another of an adult, and another of an elderly adult. Upon receiving the works, the teacher observes that several students drew the bodies of people with "stick figures," and concludes that this group has difficulty representing figures in three dimensions. Consequently, she decides to start the next class by addressing this aspect of learning. Which of the following activities allows the teacher to specifically reinforce the aspect diagnosed as challenging for her students?
options: Observe front and side drawings of human figures in anatomy books and then make copies of the analyzed forms
Relate geometric bodies to the different parts of the body and then use them to inscribe sketches of the human figure
Measure the proportions of a classmate's body to establish the relationship between their parts and then create representative sketches of the classmate's figure
Draw the axes and directions of the body parts in photographs of people to synthesize the shapes and then make copies of the analyzed human figures

question: Read the following situation: In a 12th grade class, in the unit 'Exploring artistic languages of our time', a teacher wants to explain to her students, through an action, the procedure for creating a stencil. Which of the following options contains the action that allows explaining this procedure?
options: Make incisions on a coin with a burin and then stamp it
Paint a coin and stamp it on a sheet of paper
Rub a pencil on a sheet placed over a coin
Place a coin on a sheet and paint around it

question: A group of students needs to experimentally identify the presence of functional groups in organic compounds. To do this, using carbonyl compounds, like formaldehyde and acetone, and for their recognition, Tollens' reagent and chromic acid, they perform the following procedure: 1. They place 2 mL of formaldehyde in one test tube and 2 mL of acetone in another. 2. They add 2 mL of Tollens' reagent to each test tube. 3. They heat in a water bath the tube(s) where no changes are observed. 4. They repeat step 1 and add 2 mL of chromic acid to each test tube. According to the expected results in the experiment, to which of the following conclusions should the students arrive?
options: Chromic acid allows only the recognition of ketones
Tollens' reagent allows only the recognition of aldehydes
Chromic acid allows the recognition of any carbonyl compound
Tollens' reagent allows the recognition of any carbonyl compound

question: During the reading of expository texts, 4th grade elementary students present some difficulties in differentiating between the topic and the main idea. Which of the following explanations correctly establishes this difference?
options: The topic corresponds to the introduction of the text and is discovered by asking “What is intended to be presented in the text?” and the main ideas correspond to the conclusion of the topic
The topic is the conclusion that can be drawn about what the text suggests, and the main ideas are the information that support that conclusion
The topic is what the text is going to be about and is discovered because it is in the title, and the main idea is always contained in the first paragraph
The topic answers the question “What is the text about?” and the main idea corresponds to what is said about that

question: In an 8th grade class, during the unit on epic poetry, a teacher designs an instrument to evaluate the following learning objective: 'Understand the production context in which the epic is framed.' Which of the following assessment indicators shows that students are addressing the stated learning objective?
options: Physically and psychologically characterizes the hero of the epic
Describes the cultural traits of the people to which the epic refers
Describes the values that the epic exalts with importance to divine interventions
Recognizes the didactic sense of the epic, considering its moralizing significance for society

question: In relation to thinking styles as problem-solving processes, which of the following actions corresponds to the exercise of divergent thinking?
options: Evaluate various courses of action according to a goal
Explore unusual possibilities for response and action
Search for a correct answer within an unknown field
Practice a strategy to solve problems in challenging areas

question: A teacher is working with her 2nd-year Middle School students. As part of her planning, she has considered evaluating the achievement of the indicator "Apply the concept of solution concentration." Which of the following activities directly addresses the indicator?
options: They dissolve different solutes in water and describe the type of solution that forms according to the physical state of the solute and solvent
They dissolve different amounts of solute in a fixed volume of water and arrange each solution in increasing order of solute-solvent ratio
They dissolve different amounts of solute in a fixed volume of water at different temperatures and describe the behavior of the solute
They dissolve different solutes in various solvents and describe the behavior of each solute according to the studied solvent

question: A teacher is analyzing various excerpts from philosophical texts with 12th-grade students, among which are Nietzsche and Sartre. A student asks if there can be any relationship between Nietzsche's phrase: “God is dead” and existential angst according to Sartre. Which of the following answers would clarify the student's doubt with comprehensive language and conceptual precision?
options: Yes, there is a relationship, since Sartre's thesis derives from what philosophers like Nietzsche have previously pointed out
Yes, there is a relationship, since both correspond to philosophical perspectives that question what humans have always defined as correct

Yes, there is a relationship, since both the phrase “God is dead” and existential angst refer to the independence or moral autonomy of humans in relation to the divine, to make decisions and act rightly or wrongly

Yes, they are related, since both authors are thinking about the human condition in the face of existence, and the absolute lack and precariousness of moral guidance to direct one's own action, which becomes contingent without divine or teleological principles

question: A philosophy teacher for 4th level, at the end of the unit on Aristotle's Ethics, aims to assess the following learning objective: "Understanding the Aristotelian concept of virtue." Which of the following indicators accounts for the stated objective?
options: They compare different acts based on the notion of habit
They identify different acts that fit the notion of the golden mean
They distinguish voluntary and free acts from non-voluntary and non-free acts
They recognize the Aristotelian notion of good in different situations

question: In an introductory class of the unit 'The Cell', a teacher wants to apply a strategy that allows his students to understand that science is a dynamic process of knowledge construction which development has an impact on society. Which of the following strategies will allow the teacher to achieve the proposed objective?
options: Ask the students to create a model about protein exportation in the cell, and indicate which scientist was the first to describe this pathway
Show the students different images of cells obtained with different types of microscopes
Ask the students to write an essay about the contributions of different scientists to the cell theory
Design a practical experience so that students can visualize different types of cells and relate their structures to their function

question: A teacher, in a 1st year of Secondary Education, in the unit Evolution and biodiversity, is conducting an activity with his students in which they must compare the different amino acids of protein C from different animal species and infer evolutionary relationships among them. During the development of this activity, a student asks: “If what differentiates organisms is the information in their DNA, what is the point of comparing the amino acid sequences of a protein?”. Considering the student's educational level, which of the following explanations by the teacher resolves the student's question?
options: The same protein can be expressed in organisms of different species. The information to synthesize the proteins is encoded in the genes. Therefore, by determining the differences between the genes that code for the protein and the amino acid sequences of it, evolutionary relationships between the species can be established

Proteins are made up of a specific sequence of amino acids that is encoded in DNA. If there is a modification in this, it will change the order of the amino acids of that protein. Therefore, by considering the number of different amino acids of the same protein, one can determine how closely related two species are evolutionarily

Proteins are formed from the translation of a messenger RNA molecule, which comes from the transcription of a gene. This protein can be expressed with a modification in the final amino acid sequence. Therefore, by analyzing these sequences, evolutionary relationships among species can be established

The amino acid sequence of a protein is determined by the sequence of codons that the messenger RNA has from which it is synthesized. Since each codon corresponds to a sequence of three nucleotides, if this is altered, a protein with a different amino acid sequence than that of the original protein could be obtained. Therefore, knowing the number of different amino acids between two species, one can determine how closely they are evolutionarily

question: In a 3rd Year secondary class, a teacher is reinforcing the interpretive level of reading comprehension. To do this, she presents the following excerpt for analysis:  Self-Portrait   I have spent three years and three months abroad. I prefer to look to the left. A friend of mine enjoys betrayal. The end of a trip left me with the same sad aftertaste as the end of a novel. He forgot what I disliked. It is possible that I have unknowingly spoken to someone who has killed someone. I will look in the dead ends. What lies at the end of life does not scare me. In reality, I do not listen to what I am told. I am surprised that I am given a nickname without barely knowing me. It takes me time to realize that someone is behaving badly towards me; it seems so surprising that something like this could happen to me: evil is, in a way, immoral. Archive. At two years old, I spoke with Salvador Dalí. Competition does not motivate me. Describing my life accurately would take me more time than living it.  After the reading, some students mention that they do not understand how the different sentences are connected in the text.   What question from the teacher could guide the students in analyzing the previous excerpt?
options: How does the enumeration of incoherent facts relate to the title of the text?
What association can be established between the painter Salvador Dalí and the actions described?
What narrative device characteristic of contemporary literature is used in the text?
How does the narrator's mood relate to the facts they enumerate?

question:
Read the following situation:
A three-year-old boy cries when his mother does not come to pick him up at the usual time, even though the educator has told him that she called on the phone and will arrive soon.
According to the routine and daily life of the child, from which criterion would this be considered abnormal behavior?
options: Evaluative
Homeostatic
Sociocultural
Empirical-statistical

question: A secondary teacher in 2nd Grade, in the unit "Anthropic Environmental Impacts", has asked their students to collaboratively research the causes of atmospheric pollution associated with industrialization and mass consumption, and the consequences this has in the short and long term. At the end of the work, the teacher asks the students to synthesize the results of their research by establishing the causes and effects of pollution, and to propose short- and medium-term actions to mitigate these impacts.  What evaluation tool allows the teacher to assess the work process performed by the team of students?
options: Portfolio
Checklist
Rating scale
Student log

question:
A 7th-grade teacher is addressing the following objective in one of their classes: "Describe the environmental impact of the development of technological solutions." At the beginning of the class, they explain the relationship between environmental impacts and the technological developments that produce them, but notice that the students do not clearly understand this relationship and decide to include an example.
Which of the following options represents a good example for students to understand this relationship?
options: Mention the problems caused by plastic waste in the oceans, such as garbage islands, harm to marine life, etc
Present graphs showing the amount of CO2 released into the atmosphere by various factories established in the national territory
Present the case of the paper industry and data related to deforestation, habitat loss, and associated changes in the water cycle
Show background on how transportation and industrialization have influenced the increase in the planet's global temperature due to the accumulation of greenhouse gases

question: The children in early years are enthusiastically participating in an activity proposed by the educator. Upon hearing a song, they must recognize which musical instrument is playing and raise a card with an image of it. What element of musical language is the educator working on in the described learning experience?
options: Beat
Rhythm
Timbre
Intensity

question: Alicia, a 3rd-grade student, has faced challenges in the process of acquiring reading and writing skills. In response to this, the special education teacher has considered the explicit teaching of each letter, moving on to syllables, words, sentences, and texts; therefore, it is a gradual learning process that goes from mastering the code to understanding a text, emphasizing the motor aspects of the act of writing. Based on the previous case, what is the reading and writing model to which the strategy used by the special education teacher adheres?
options: Holistic model
Integrated model
Logographic model
Skill model

question: A 6th grade teacher assessed the indicator "Recognize that heat flows from an object with a higher temperature to another with a lower temperature" with the following question: Why do people use thick clothing to keep warm? Upon reading the students' responses, the teacher found that many answered: "Because the clothing reduces the flow of cold to the body." Which of the following activities would help students understand how heat flows between objects?
options: Conduct an experimental activity where students, inside the classroom, touch with their hands a piece of wood, a piece of metal, and a piece of plastic, and record which one feels colder or warmer. Then, they measure and record the temperature of these materials to verify the heat flow

Conduct an experimental activity where there is heat flow between two cups of water at different temperatures, demonstrating what happens to the flow when one of the cups is covered with a natural wool sock

Conduct an experimental activity by exposing three cups with water at 10°C: one in the sun, another one near a burner, and the last one next to a cup with water at 70°C. Then, they take the time to reach 15°C to verify the heat flow
Conduct an experimental activity where they expose a steel clip and an aluminum clip to the flame of a burner and then, using a thermometer, record the temperature of each to verify the heat flow

question: A preschool educator is working on the following learning objective: “Communicate the process developed in solving concrete problems, identifying the question, actions, and possible answers.” Considering the above information, which assessment instrument allows the description of the progression of this learning?
options: Checklist
Holistic rubric
Numerical scale
Rating scale

question: An educator will address the following learning objective in Social Interaction and Citizenship: "Show interest in interacting with boys and girls, recognizing the diversity of their characteristics." Which of the following evaluative strategies is appropriate to measure the achievement of this objective?
options: Use an observation checklist while they play in the yard with children from other levels
Apply a performance rubric while the children participate in photo exhibitions of their families
Apply a rating scale while the boys and girls conduct an interview with people from the community
Use pedagogical documentation while the children participate in a fair about various cultures of the country

question: In a 6th-grade class, a teacher is working with the students on understanding the moods conveyed by poems. To do this, together with the class, she performs the following reading:  Can we sit in the shade   and contemplate the garden?   Sad herbs are growing.   The trees enclose us   and in their long throats   the silence cries.    (Our blood   also cries,   dark river   that begins   and ends   in us).    Beyond us, heavy mountains   halt us   and the moon rises   as an eye.   ( The Water Cycle, Alicia Montal).  What strategy can the teacher propose to the students to enhance the understanding of the mood conveyed in the poem read?
options: Identify the rhetorical figures present in the poem
Answer the question: what other title would you give to the poem?
Relate the characteristics of the garden to what the speaker expresses
Name characteristics of gardens known to the students and the feelings they generate

question: A teacher of a 7th Grade class is working on the concept of absolute value in integers. During the correction of some exercises presented on the board, a student asks the teacher: "Why is the result negative when there is a negative sign in front of the absolute value? You said that the absolute value of any number is always positive, so I don’t understand why that has to be the case." Which of the following explanations answers the above question accurately, using language understandable for a 7th Grade student?
options: The negative sign before an expression means "the additive inverse of." Since absolute value is always greater than or equal to zero, its additive inverse will always be less than or equal to zero

The negative sign before an expression also means "change of sign." Since absolute value is always greater than or equal to zero, changing the sign always results in a value that is less than or equal to zero

The negative sign before an expression is equivalent to using the rule of signs in the multiplication of integers. Since absolute value is always greater than or equal to zero, given that "positive times negative is negative," the result will always be less than or equal to zero

The negative sign before an expression is equivalent to having a negative number before a parenthesis. Since the absolute value is always greater than or equal to zero and the negative sign changes the sign inside the parenthesis, its value will always be less than or equal to zero

question:
Read the following situation:
In the 4th grade Basic History axis, a teacher asks her students to compare the Maya, Aztec, and Inca civilizations and point out three similarities between them. One of the responses she reviewed noted the following:
“They were all farmers, and although they had defined social classes, they were all poor because they did not have technology like us and lived in stone houses that were very cold.”
What error is manifested in the student's response?
options: Does not recognize the intrinsic value of American civilizations
Makes presentist judgments about American civilizations
Ignores the legacy of American civilizations to current culture
Confuses characteristics of one American civilization with those of another

question: Read the following situation:  Agustan is in 5th grade and has a diagnosis of an intellectual disability. He is capable of understanding all conversations from adults and peers, and shows a clear intention to communicate. However, his main support needs relate to intelligible speech.  Considering Agusta­n's characteristics, which of the following educational actions is appropriate to address his support need?
options: Encourage verbal expression with the support of photographs
Complement verbal expression with the use of pictograms
Support verbal expression by incorporating object key
Enrich verbal expression with the help of a drawing of a real object

question: Read the following situation: A teacher wants their 7th grade students to develop interactive stories that they can share with classmates and parents. Which web service is most suitable for carrying out this activity?
options: Scratch
Excel
WordPress
Vimeo

question: A teacher evaluated the unit on epistemology in the 3rd year of high school, specifically focusing on Cartesian arguments about substance. In the evaluation, he notices that some students express arguments concerning the idea of perfection similar to the following: "According to Descartes, if all people have an idea of what perfection is, it is because we have all once had an experience of something perfect, and thus this idea was imprinted in our minds. And if we all have known something perfect, it is because God created these objects." In relation to Cartesian arguments, what difficulty in the learning process can be inferred from this response?
options: The students did not manage to synthesize the Cartesian problem regarding the perfect substance originating from our impressions
The students did not understand the Cartesian argument about the idea of perfection as an innate idea originating from reason
The students did not adequately elaborate their statements, confusingly explaining the Cartesian argument about the idea of God that derives from perfection
The students did not adequately organize the argumentative sequence to express the Cartesian argument that infers the existence of God from the idea of perfection

question: In the kindergarten of a special school for students with Autism Spectrum Disorder, there are two learning groups. In group 1, there are students who display exploratory behaviors in the environment and instrumentalization to achieve what they want. In group 2, there are students with limited verbal language, but a good receptive-comprehension level of it; they recognize family images, animals, and colors.  Both groups present challenges in the learning area, organizational issues, little awareness of danger, maladaptive behaviors, and short attention and concentration spans in school tasks.  The teacher decided to implement the use of baskets, task boards, coat racks, materials with their photographs, and individual workspaces. Each student will work with personal schedules: those in group 1 with photographs and those in group 2 with pictograms. In spaces like bathrooms and resource rooms, task sequences with images were provided; additionally, reading and play corners were created in the room. The work will be carried out with adapted concrete materials.  Why do the measures implemented by the teacher allow addressing the learning challenges of their students?
options: Because these measures promote the understanding of the school context for the students, enhance the exploration of their environment in a more harmonious way, and by adapting materials based on their level, stress is reduced and adjustment to the environment increases

Because these measures comprehensively address the challenges of the students related to language disruption, verbal and non-verbal communication, and also because they address their social difficulties with the protection of a controlled and safe environment

Because these measures allow establishing entry parameters for behaviors and taking actions regarding learning and their pedagogical objectives, responding to the maladaptive behaviors presented by the students who need greater specialized support

Because these comprehensive measures are consistent with the reinforcement and individual work that students with autism spectrum disorder should receive, one-on-one work, while also considering their difficulties at the social level and interpersonal relationships with peers in the play and reading corners in the classroom

question: A high school teacher is planning a class with the objective "To analyze and interpret data on collisions between objects". Which activity, consistent with the curricular emphasis on developing scientific skills, would allow students to address this objective?
options: Calculate the momentum of the objects involved in collisions in everyday situations
Conduct an investigation on the conceptual differences between elastic and inelastic collisions
Draw conclusions about the movement of some marbles, based on measurements of their speed in collisions
Describe the collisions that appear in videos of everyday situations, pointing out the factors that influence them

question:
A teacher in a 6th grade class asks the students to write short narrative texts to sequence events and evaluate the spelling of written production. Upon reviewing the progress of the writing, she notices that a student has written: The red-haired girl always helped her classmates with their tasks with the best intentions; however, her friends continuously told her that she was becoming invasive.
Which of the following interventions by the teacher would more directly enable the student to understand her mistake and improve her spelling?
options: Read examples and explain the rules for the use of 's'
Show the student how the word 'invasive' is spelled, so she can correct her mistake
Indicate to the student the graphemic correspondence between 'c' and 's' using examples
Point out that the word 'invasive' is related to 'invade', therefore, there is a mistake in the way she wrote the word

question: The classroom team of the Science subject for a 6th grade class seeks for its students to explain the importance of energy for objects to change and why living beings need it to carry out their vital processes. To achieve this purpose, they propose diversified teaching strategies, intending various forms of grouping and the active participation of students, as these are the prominent skills in the course.  In light of this, which of the following diversified strategies is relevant to implement considering the learning objective and the skills of the students?
options: Conduct a group reading about energy and its implications for the vital processes of living beings
Develop a group research project that exposes the contribution of energy from everyday appliances to living beings
Create a graphical organizer in a group that accounts for the different types of energy, their classification, and contribution
Carry out a group experiment that demonstrates the implications of energy in living beings and in various objects

question: A 10th grade teacher finished the unit on real numbers and has decided to assess whether the students understood the main difference between irrational and rational numbers. Which of the following activities for the students is the most appropriate for this purpose?
options: Explain and justify the steps required to transform a fraction into a decimal, and vice versa
Classify periodic, semiperiodic, and non-periodic decimal numbers, indicating the difference between them
Express some numbers from a given set of real numbers in decimal form, and explain and justify the procedure used
Express some numbers from a given set of real numbers in fractional form and explain under what conditions this transformation is possible

question: Martina is a first-year secondary student diagnosed with aniridia, associated with nystagmus and bilateral strabismus. She shows good socialization with her peers and establishes friendships in the class. She demonstrates adequate skills in daily living and orientation and mobility, and she is in the process of transitioning from large print to braille. For the Language and Literature subject, the classroom team decides to work on the learning objective: Recognize and identify the literary figures present in various poems read to enrich her understanding. The classroom team asks the students to create a PowerPoint presentation, which displays the lyrics of various songs, accompanied by the corresponding music, which is listened to and read by everyone, so that Martina can also participate both in the preparation and in the presentation.  Why does the strategy used by the classroom team favor the achievement of the learning objective proposed with Martina and her class?
options: It allows students to access information through alternative modes, which may include auditory, visual information and the combination of these
It allows students independent access, through modifications in the way the schedule or time is structured to develop the classes or assessments
It allows students independent access, through adjustments in the spaces, locations, and the conditions under which the task, activity, or assessment is developed
It allows students to perform activities, tasks, and assessments through different forms and various devices or technical and technological aids designed specifically

question: Read the following situation:  Sebastian, a 2nd-grade student who has an intellectual disability, receives support from the Special Education Needs department of his institution. He stands out for his skills in sports, as he is fast and agile, showing a special interest in Physical Education. In relation to his challenges, he shows a need for support in verbal comprehension and following instructions when these involve tasks of two or more steps. To address his support needs, the special education teacher proposes to consider the student's interest in Physical Education to reinforce the skills that are lacking within the context of a motor circuit class.  Based on the previous situation, which of the following strategies enhances Sebastian's understanding of instructions in the context of Physical Education class?
options: Ask Sebastian and his peers to paraphrase the instruction in their own words after listening to the explanation of the stages of the motor circuit, expressing what they understood they need to do
Allow Sebastian to perform the stages of the circuit independently, repeating each one several times, to then carry out the complete motor circuit that allows him to integrate all the stages
Simplify the instructions for each of the stages of the motor circuit, by using short words and phrases, accompanying each instruction with a modeling done by the teacher
Organize support tutorials with peers, so that the classmates themselves measure the learning experiences and explain the stages of the motor circuit with a friendly language

question: Read the following situation:  In the 1st grade class of a school with Special Educational Needs, Veronica, a student with Autism Spectrum Disorder, attends. She is characterized by being very explorative with objects and enjoys working with didactic materials; however, she displays impulsive behaviors when she wants to obtain items that her classmates are using, as she takes them suddenly. Some of these behaviors are repeated when she needs to go to other classrooms, where it is difficult for her to enter, which is also repeated when she has to re-enter her own classroom after recess, lunch, or the resource room, as she loses her way, entering other rooms.  Which of the following options do you consider appropriate pedagogical actions to address Veronica's support needs?
options: Emphasize the development of thinking skills in the classroom, in which the student engages in metacognition about her actions through various didactic strategies or actions that allow her to observe and gradually modify her own behaviors and compare them with those of her classmates

Encourage continuous interaction between teacher and student, extending it to external spaces outside the classroom. Create systems of turns in the playground and cafeteria, assign hours to teachers to be present, or if necessary, establish a directive that ensures accompanied activity in each space and school activity

Promote more adapted behaviors in the classroom by establishing positive reinforcements that reduce impulsive behaviors and increase the student's ability to function appropriately, and create a 'Guide' support with her classmates, enhancing her social skills and achieving the spatial orientation she needs

Implement the adaptation of visual supports in the school so that the student can anticipate changes, modifying the classroom environment with specific areas of information, and using specific boxes for work materials, complemented by the use of images across different contexts

question: A teacher in 7th grade, in the unit "The civilizations that contributed to the formation of Western culture", designed a class with the purpose of characterizing the Mediterranean Sea as a space of circulation and exchange, aiming to develop the skill of geographical thinking, associated with understanding spatial distributions and patterns linked to territorial dynamics. Considering the design of the class, which of the following activities should be proposed to determine if the students achieved the expected learning outcomes?
options: Represent the location and characteristics of the Mediterranean Sea, using a current political map
Describe the communication routes that are formed around the Mediterranean Sea, using a historical map
Relate various geographical features of the Mediterranean area using a physical map
Illustrate relevant aspects of the geography of the Mediterranean Sea through the creation of a mind map

question: A teacher, in 1st Year, is working with her students on the inferential level of reading. To do this, she applies the strategy of interrogating the following Micro-story, formulating questions that address the inferential level:  We loved to play in the dirt yard. We chased the hens. The Laucha would "hypnotize" them. He would circle them and place them on the ground. With a little wooden stick, he drew their cage. Everything was magic. The mountain in the distance. The sound of the wind. And my mom from the window watched us grow. One Sunday, an unruly dog took my dad away from us. My mom died in life. "She has gone, my daughter," said the little girl, "take care of your mom." I grew up suddenly at twelve years old. Until I arrived in Santiago, I had no idea we were poor.  (Childhood, Pablo Ortiz Tirado. Santiago in A Hundred Words).  Which of the following questions addresses the inferential level of the narrative?
options: Why does the protagonist claim to have grown up suddenly?
What elements of the story connect the protagonist with her childhood?
Would you have felt poor, like the protagonist, when moving from the countryside to the city? Why?
In what way did the Laucha hypnotize the hens?

question: A teacher in a 7th Grade class asks the students to write a news article about the school's anniversary week. The following is a fragment of a draft of the text written by one of the students. The Blue Alliance, after a close result, is the winner of the anniversary week. After a week of intense competitions, the Blue Alliance took first place in the 2017 anniversary. With a difference of only 100 points, the diligent Blue Alliance was crowned champion of the anniversary of the San Patricio school last week. Among the activities won by the Blue Alliance, the most outstanding was the dance of the kings, presenting a great choreography: the most coordinated, creative, and motivating of all. The 4th-year high school students, with great enthusiasm and joy, led the celebration of the victory and invited the other members of the Alliance to dance and sing with them. Considering the characteristics of the news article, what is the difficulty presented by the text written by the student?
options: The description of the facts provides irrelevant data
The headline does not convey the central idea of the news
The ideas presented lack precision and conciseness
The sender of the message does not distance themselves from the facts narrated

question: A preschool educator has worked with her group on several activities aimed at identifying the characteristics of different geometric bodies. For the next activity, she wants the children to discover that there are geometric bodies that have all their faces the same. Which of the following pairs of geometric bodies can the educator select to allow the children to discover this characteristic?
options: Cubes and tetrahedrons
Cubes and triangular prisms
Rectangular prisms and tetrahedrons
Rectangular prisms and triangular prisms

question: At a nursery level, an educator will address the learning objective: “Show interest in textures, responding through various forms, such as babbling, gestures, smiles, among others”, from the Artistic Languages core. To do this, she places various blocks covered with different textures in the room for the children to explore. After a moment, a child explores the resource and begins to react. The educator approaches and gets down to the child's level. Based on the described situation, what intervention by the educator could facilitate achieving this objective?
options: Ask: “What texture are you touching on this block? That one is smooth, and this one is rough. Which one do you want to take?”
Ask: “Did you like the materials I left? Do you want to touch another one? Can you reach it? I will bring it closer to you.”
Comment: “You are touching the blocks with textures, your smile tells me you prefer the smooth texture. This one is rough, do you want to touch it?”
Comment: “You are touching the blocks with textures, do you like them? Do you want to touch another one? Take it with your hands. You babbled, very good!”

question: Which of the following didactic resources is most appropriate to begin working on the representation of two-digit numbers according to their positional value in a 2nd grade class?
options: Base ten blocks
Coins of $10 and $1
Chips of two different colors
Positional chart with removable digits

question: A teacher sets the following learning objective for their class: Characterize the behavior of pH in the neutralization of a strong acid with a weak base. Which of the following options is a pertinent indicator to account for this learning?
options: They construct the titration curve of a strong acid with a weak base
They perform a titration to neutralize a strong acid with a weak base
They quantify the neutralization of a strong acid with a weak base based on the pKb of the base
They determine the concentration of the strong acid based on a known concentration of the weak base

question: A teacher will address with their 7th grade students the unit on The great civilizations of humanity. In the first class, the teacher will teach the students how to select information sources, to work with them on source analysis and to promote the development of historical thinking.  What prior learning do students need to develop in order to appropriately select a historical source?
options: Identify the types of information that the source provides
Recognize the historical agents to which the source alludes
Evaluate the relevance of the source in relation to the topic to be addressed
Distinguish the central arguments about the process present in the source

question: A teacher, in a 4th year secondary class, in the unit of literary avant-gardes, reads and analyzes with the students the Dadaist Manifesto, with the aim of helping them understand the creative process of the Dadaists. Some students indicate that they cannot understand the process of creation. What literary resource could you invite the students to work on, to address the concerns that arose in the class?
options: With an acrostic
With an anti-poem
With a bone-breaker
With an exquisite corpse

question: Read the following case: A teacher asks her students to relate the conception of reality in Plato and Aristotle through ideas or concepts. Which of the analogies posed by the students clearly reflects the difference between these authors?
options: Life and death
The earth and the Christian paradise
Truth and deceit
Magic and science

question: Rodrigo attends a school with special educational needs in preschool. He has congenital blindness and spastic diplegia. He has slowly acquired spatial concepts.  The educator and the special education teacher are working on the area of experiences for learning interaction and understanding of the environment, aiming for students to describe the position of objects and living beings using location and distance concepts such as: inside, outside, on top, underneath, near, and far.  The teachers need to implement a scaffolding activity that allows Rodrigo to participate in the learning experience.  Which of the following activities addresses the understanding of spatial organization in the presented case?
options: Conduct bodily games in pairs that apply the concepts of inside/outside, on top/underneath, near/far, in relation to reference objects and that involve changes in position

Mediating with the hand-over-hand strategy the tactile sweeping of the entire sheet, orally pointing to the names of the animals, then making comparison relationships between them using spatial concepts

Paint on a tactile sheet with paint mixed with semolina and two other textures according to the instructions related to: paint where the fish is, touch the sheet making comparisons, and so with the other animals
Create a model that represents land and water, to place miniature animals in it, establishing comparative relationships through movement and displacement to determine their positions

question: Which of the following activities specifically allows working on grammatical agreement in writing with 2nd Grade students?
options: Play drawing what a sentence says. For example, if the sentence says: “The rose has thorns”, draw a rose with thorns
Play describing features of characters from an image. For example, from the image of a clown, point out the color of their clothes, the makeup on their face, etc
Play identifying mistakes in the language of an alien. For example, from the sentence “The dog drank the milk”, identify the alien's mistakes
Play creating absurd sentences from given words. For example, a student receives a card with the word “bread” and creates a nonsensical sentence, like: “I wore a blue bread.”

question: A teacher is working on the human body unit in a 2nd grade class and is addressing the objective of understanding the function of the heart. When inquiring into the students' prior knowledge, he realizes that some have misconceptions about the subject. Which of the following options presents a misconception about the heart?
options: The heart becomes stronger through sports
A person's heart is the size of their fist
The heart allows blood to carry oxygen to the body
The heart produces the blood that goes to the entire organism

question: In 7th grade, a teacher is working with students on the concept of polysemy. For this, he hands out a guide containing the following exercise: Read the excerpt and pay attention to the underlined word: Studies show that 35% of mercury pollution is caused by batteries that are incinerated with household waste. To get a sense of the pollution problem, it is enough to mention that a single button cell battery (like those used in watches) can contaminate 6.5 million liters of water, the same amount as an Olympic-sized swimming pool. (Diario El Clarín). Then, he presents the following sentences, which contain the term "battery," and asks them to indicate which one contains the same meaning of "battery" as the previous excerpt. 1. The artillery battery allowed the soldiers to strongly resist the combat. 2. The restaurant chef decided to buy a new set of kitchenware, as the one he used was very worn out. 3. A sober show was provided by the band of musicians in their concert, demonstrating a great level in the use of the drum kit. 4. Batteries pose a real danger to children, so toys that use them must have a safety screw. The students answer that the term in sentence 4 has the same meaning as the excerpt. Why is it appropriate to use this strategy for students to understand polysemy?
options: Because it makes it possible to distinguish words according to criteria of synonymy
Because it allows distinguishing the meaning of a term
Because it helps to classify words according to their connotation
Because it allows grouping vocabulary in a semantic field

question: What are the fundamental cognitive skills that underlie the concept of number?
options: Counting, seriation, and comparison
Classification, comparison, and counting
Seriation, classification, and conservation
Sequencing, correspondence, and seriation

question: In the subject of Natural Sciences, a teacher sets the following objective for Grade 1: "Describe what generally happens with fruit trees during the seasons of the year." Which of the following activities is most appropriate to assess students' learning achievement?
options: Provide a blank sheet and ask students to draw their favorite fruit tree in two seasons of the year
Provide various images with different types of trees and ask students to classify them into those that have fruits and those that do not
Provide a sheet with drawings of fruit trees, where the season each one is in is indicated, and ask them to color them
Provide cards with different trees and others with fruits and ask students to associate the tree with the fruit that belongs to it

question: Which of the following topics is a prerequisite for advancing in understanding trigonometric ratios in a right triangle?
options: Scaled figures
Euclid's theorem
Similarity of triangles
Angles in the right triangle

question: Which of the following activities best aligns with the curriculum emphasis on critical thinking in the subject of Technology?
options: The students search for evidence in different sources and social networks about the conspiracy theory, and contrast the doubts it raises with the available scientific information
The students make a list of reasons that might have motivated a possible hoax and analyze it from the social, economic, and technological dimensions

The students design an imaginary interview with an astronaut, with questions intended to elucidate the truth of the conspiracy theory. Then, they investigate the testimonies of astronauts who went to the moon in different sources

The students watch the documentary “Did Man Really Land on the Moon?”, which presents the available evidence regarding the 1969 moon landing. Afterwards, they write an essay with their opinion on the topic

question: During the delivery of the progress report to the family, Soledad's special education teacher, a 2nd-grade student with a reading learning disability, makes the following comment to the guardian:  Among the strengths observed in Soledad during the first semester, her concentration in reading activities, her interest in visual elements, and her effort to learn to read stand out. However, she still faces challenges in activities where she must recognize the syllables that make up a word included in a sentence. She also faces challenges in recognizing words that begin or end with the same sound, naming objects that start with the consonant being worked on, and analyzing and synthesizing a word that contains the specified consonant.  What cognitive process does the special education teacher refer to when describing Soledad's support needs?
options: Alternating attention
Visual discrimination
Phonological awareness
Decoding skill

question: Valentina is in preK and has advanced in her writing skills, managing to connect dots to draw different lines, maintaining continuity in the paths. According to the stages of initiation into writing, what is the next skill that the girl should practice, respecting the level of complexity of these?
options: Copy vowels in cursive
Outline different figures and shapes
Draw free arabesques of different sizes
Trace paths following circuits and mazes

question: Read the following case: A teacher presents some ideas of Jean Paul Sartre about human freedom. A student intervenes to point out that she finds it difficult to understand the author's idea that there is no human nature. Which of the following responses from the teacher can help the student understand Sartre's idea?
options: Unlike human beings, things that have been designed by someone, a craftsman for example, could not have an essence prior to the existence that this grants them when crafting them
Just like with humans, things that have been designed by someone, a craftsman for example, could have a predetermined essence, but nevertheless, for this they must also exist
We humans have not been designed by someone, so there is no essence or human nature prior to the exercise and realization of our freedom of action
Humans have been designed by someone who has given us freedom of action (free will); therefore, we only determine what we are based on our own actions

question: In an 8th Grade, the teacher presents the following learning objective for the clas: compose rotations, translations, and reflections in the Cartesian plane. Then, he organizes the class into groups to go to the computer room where he has installed a geometric processor that the students already know. In that processor, the students must perform the composition of pairs of isometric transformations of the same type (two rotations with the same center, two reflections with respect to the coordinate axes, or two translations) provided by the teacher. On the board, the teacher writes a question to guide the work. With which of the following questions can the teacher guide the students' work to achieve the stated objective?
options: What happens when a composition of transformations is performed?
What difficulty did you find when performing the compositions of transformations?
How can each composition be described using a single transformation?
What are the elements involved in each composition of transformations?

question: A 2nd grade secondary teacher is designing an instrument to evaluate the following learning objective: "Demonstrate that they understand that knowledge of the Universe changes and increases with new evidence."  What evaluation indicator best accounts for students' learning regarding this objective?
options: Explain qualitatively the evolution of the Universe according to the Big Bang theory
Specify the processes of formation of cosmic structures from gravitational collapse
Compare the virtues and limitations of different models of the Universe to explain its evolution
Compare the descriptions made by the geocentric model and the heliocentric model about the solar system

question: A child at an advanced preschool level is playing at a work station with seven cards. All have a red heart drawn on them, but their shades vary from very intense red to less intense red. The child, after observing these cards, arranges them from the card with the most intense red heart to the one with the palest red heart.  What skill underlies the performance demonstrated by the child?
options: Arrange a series
Sequence a pattern
Group into categories
Perform one-to-one correspondence

question: A teacher of a 4th grade class is designing a test to assess the reading comprehension of her students through various questions related to non-literary texts. Among the selected texts, the teacher includes the following:  Bats are the only mammals equipped with wings capable of flying. One of their characteristics is that they have a very limited sense of sight, so they orient themselves and locate obstacles and food using a developed system of high-frequency ultrasounds inaudible to humans.  They are nocturnal animals that feed on insects and small animals. Like bees and other insects, bats fulfill an important function in nature: they are pollinators without which many plant species could not reproduce.  Although bats are commonly associated with being small animals, there are bats of different sizes. The smallest weighs about 1.5 grams, while the largest can weigh 1.2 kilograms and have a wingspan of 1.5 meters.  Bats are present on all continents, except Antarctica. Like mole rats, they are mammals whose body temperature depends on the environment, especially when inactive.  (Adapted from: www.nationalgeographic.com.es/animales/murcielagos).  Which of the following questions allows evaluating the extraction of implicit information from the previous text?
options: Why aren't bats found in Antarctica?
Why are bats commonly associated with being small animals?
What characteristic distinguishes bats from other mammals?
Why can't humans hear the sounds bats make?

question:
A 2nd grade teacher is addressing a unit on analysis of technological solutions, which aims to "Describe the effects and changes that environments have had due to the development of technological innovations." The teacher begins the unit by showing students some images of the public bicycle system that has been implemented in some regions of Chile, and asks them if they know it and have used it.

Which of the following activities would allow the teacher to address the stated objective and respond to the curricular emphasis on critical thinking?
options: Ask students who have used the public bicycle service to share their experience with their classmates, then organize them into small groups to analyze the importance of demonstrating responsible attitudes both in using this type of transport and in the impacts its use can have on the environment and people

Show students a video that shows the misuse of sidewalks by cyclists, crossing roads without respecting signs or traffic lights, and cycling without helmets or lights. Ask them to select one of the observed problems, infer its impacts, and then propose solutions to mitigate these impacts

Take the students to observe in the field the impacts and difficulties arising from the placement of public bicycles in different places, such as sidewalks, parks, and subway exits. Then ask them to systematize this information, so that from it they can propose and generate concrete solutions

Ask the students to survey family members and acquaintances about how they have benefited from or been affected by the public bicycle service. Then, have them systematize the information based on criteria they define in different areas (social, environmental, economic, etc.), determine the impacts, and present their stance on the service to the class with justification

question: During a 7th grade class, in the unit on Photography, a teacher is working with her students on the elements of photographic language. After explaining verbally or through drawings the concepts of frames, angles, and focuses, she asks the students to take pictures in the school yards, applying the different angles reviewed. While monitoring the process, the teacher notices that in many of the photographs, the students only used a variety of frames without applying angles, thus revealing that they confuse the frame with the photographic angle.  What of the following activities can the teacher ask the students to clarify the concept of angles in a practical way?
options: Take photographs of a classmate located at the other end of the room at different distances, until being one meter away from him
Look in a magazine at photographs showing different frames and angles, and then represent them photographing an object from the classroom of personal choice
Take photographs of a pencil case on a glass table, positioning the camera above the object while standing on a chair and then below it while lying on the floor
Take photographs of a backpack on a table that was in the center of the room, from different parts of a drawn circle on the floor around it

question: Read the following situation: Students in the third year of secondary school are preparing a musical presentation for the Spring Concert held by their school. Their teacher monitors the students during the 10 rehearsals available and tracks their progress, considering both their disposition and ability to face the work as well as the development of their interpretative musical skills. Which of the following tools should the teacher select for this task?
options: Rubric
Checklist
Anecdotal record
Audiovisual record

question: Read the following situation: In 8th grade, within the framework of the unit "Visual creation, person and environment", students carry out a group audiovisual project about pollution in their community. To start editing the video, the teacher needs to verify if they have completed the previous activities, such as the creation of the storyboard, the recording of short videos within the community on the topic, and the writing of texts related to the video, which will be included in their audiovisual work. Which of the following assessment instruments is suitable to verify the completion of these activities?
options: Portfolio
Checklist
Analytical rubric
Anecdotal record

question: A secondary teacher, in a 4th year class, is planning an evaluation to conclude the unit on Contemporary Art, where the objective will be to create proposals with visual characteristics typical of the style addressed by contemporary artists in their works. During this unit, the students have learned about different artists from the second half of the 20th century, focusing on the work of pop artist Roy Lichtenstein, so the evaluation will consist of recreating a classical artwork with the pictorial style of this artist.  Which of the following indicators is pertinent to include in the evaluation guideline?
options: They present ironic and humorous elements in their proposal
They apply a wide color palette with small brushstrokes in their proposal
They combine photographic images and objects with painting in the composition of their proposal
They use dots, hatching, and black lines to outline the shapes that make up their proposal

question: Read the following situation: The teacher of a 4th grade elementary class will work with her students on solving one-step equations with additions and subtractions, so she wants to present, at the beginning of the class, an activity that activates the students' prior knowledge related to the topic and helps them better assimilate the new learning. Which of the following activities allows the teacher to achieve the proposed goal?
options: Recognize the inverse relationships between addition and subtraction
Solve additions using the decomposition strategy
Present non-routine problems involving additions and subtractions
Estimate results of additions and subtractions through mental calculation

question: A teacher of a 6th grade will evaluate his students concerning the following learning objective: "Identify characteristic musical traits of a repertoire of traditional Chilean dances and their corresponding belonging to a geographical area of the country".  What performance allows evidence of achievement in learning by the students?
options: They interpret a varied repertoire of traditional dances from different areas of the country, using instruments and their voices
They point out folk and popular music of traditional Chilean dances that they know and classify it according to its predominant rhythm
They create a map of Chile in which they position the native instruments and traditional dances in their respective geographical areas of the country
They listen to various recordings of music from traditional Chilean dances and classify them by geographical area, according to their instrumentation and predominant rhythms

question:
A professor from Middle School, in the unit 'The Meters', selects different auditory examples for his students to learn to identify 2/4, 3/4, and 4/4 meters.
Which of the following concepts should a student master before they can learn to distinguish different types of meters by ear?
options: Rhythm
Form
Accent
Articulation

question: A teacher, in an 8th grade class, during the unit on lyrical genre, will formatively evaluate the following objective: "Understand the formal characteristics of a poem." Which of the following activities will allow for direct evidence of achieving the proposed objective?
options: Present a poem and ask students to identify the symbols of the poem and then meet in small groups to analyze them with their classmates
Present a sonnet and a free verse poem that address the same theme and, starting from the distinction of adjectives and synonymous terms, compare the similarities and differences between both poems
Present a poem and ask students to identify the speaker, motive, and lyrical object, to develop a written personal analysis of the text mentioning the topics present in it

Present a poem and ask students to identify, through a guide of questions, the number of verses, stanzas, presence or absence of rhyme, meter, and characteristics of these, as well as the presence of literary figures

question: Read the following situation:  The classroom team of a 2nd grade must provide educational responses to the support needs presented by two students, stemming from their intellectual disability. Both achieve a good understanding of oral language, manage an adequate incorporation into the course, showing a preference for group work over individual work, and demonstrate motivation in school activities, achieving significant learning when they interact directly with the content to work on. The teacher of the subject of History, Geography, and Social Sciences and the special education teacher seek a strategy to work on the following learning objective: "Read and draw simple plans of the environment, using reference points, categories of relative position, and pictorial symbolism" and thereby offer an educational response tailored to the requirements of both students, within the framework of diversified teaching.  Which of the following strategies diversely favors the learning of students with intellectual disabilities in this case?
options: Incorporate the use of ICT, for which, in the computer lab, students should construct a plan that represents a space in their home, with the support of predetermined templates
Offer multiple forms of expression, from which, individually and using a photograph as support, each student prepares a plan of the chosen image on a white sheet
Provide multiple means of participation, indicating to form pairs to play 'hidden treasure', where students must search for and find objects, following points of spatial comparison and location

Grant multiple forms of representation, from which the teacher shows the class a simple plan of a room that represents, using symbols, elements typical of this; then to ask questions about the spatial location of the objects in the plan

question: Read the following classroom situation: In one of his/her next lessons an English teacher has to cover the following learning indicator: “Paraphrase information from a written text.” To which of the following learning objectives does the previous learning indicator correspond?
options: Locate specific phrases in a text
Infer general and/or specific written information
State the main idea of general and/or specific written information
Show understanding of general and/or specific written information

question: In a preschool, the educator needs to assess the level of achievement of the following indicator of Corporality and Movement: Points out or indicates the location of internal organs such as the heart, lungs, brain. Which of the following evaluation strategies would be most appropriate for the educator's purpose?
options: Ask children to model internal organs of the human body with clay and evaluate their performance using a checklist
Ask each child to select a human body organ and give a presentation about it. Gather evidence through observation records
Ask children to place images of the organs on a silhouette of the human body and record their performance using a rating scale
Ask children to watch a video of the human body and then discuss in groups what they observed. Gather evidence through anecdotal records

question: Read the following situation:  In the 3rd grade of a school with special educational needs, Maite, a student diagnosed with Autism Spectrum Disorder, attends. She has verbal language, achieves conversational fluency taking on communicative roles as both sender and receiver, initiates conversations, and responds to topics, however, she has certain difficulties in aspects of proximate communication, due to her constant motor restlessness, needing the continuous mediation of the teacher to focus her attention on the different demands of the context.  In the Mathematics subject, the teacher is working on the learning objective: "Demonstrate that they understand the concept of angle, identifying acute, obtuse, and right angles".  Considering Maite's educational needs, which of the following diversified strategies is appropriate to achieve the proposed learning objective?
options: The teacher, at the beginning of the class, presents the protractor and its use to the students; subsequently, she shows images of different angles present in everyday contexts, measuring them as the class responds, classifying them according to their measurements. Finally, she invites each student to draw different angles in their notebooks

The teacher presents the use of the protractor to measure angles to the students; subsequently, she invites the group to go outside to look for different angles, which they must measure, later recording their measurement and the specific location where they found them; upon returning to the classroom, the students share the angles they recorded, and then address the audiovisual resource "The angles around me"

The teacher will request the students' protractor at the start of the class; she will draw angles on the board that the students must reproduce in their notebooks and will teach how to measure the angles; then, she will present the audiovisual resource "The angles around me". Finally, she will invite the class to do the activity from the subject's book and will support those who face greater challenges in the activity, redirecting the attention of these students whenever they become distracted by some environmental stimulus
The teacher will ask the students to create an angle card, in which they must draw each angle with its measurements using the protractor;

question: One of the learning objectives of the 2nd Year secondary curriculum is aimed at enabling students to perform calculations involving operations with real numbers. Which of the following assessment indicators directly reflects the achievement of this objective?
options: They differentiate between rational and irrational numbers
They operate with rational and irrational numbers
They solve problems involving roots in different contexts
They recognize numbers whose decimal development is infinite and has no period

question: A teacher, working in the 1st Year of high school on the unit Progress, industrialization and crisis: formation and impact of the new contemporary order in Chile and the world, has defined as one of the evaluation objectives that her students demonstrate their critical thinking through the analysis of the central characteristics of the Chilean economy in the second half of the 19th century.  Which of the following evaluation indicators shows that the students achieved the proposed evaluation objective?
