Students observe that when placing the block on the table and tilting it, at a certain angle, the block begins to slide. Which of the following activities would allow the teacher to evaluate to what extent his students achieve the stated objective?
options: Ask the students to identify the variables of the experiment and determine a method for data recording
Provide the students with a work guide to measure the angle at which each block starts to slide and calculate an average for three measurements per block
Tell the students to place each block in a fixed position and tilt the table until the block slides, and then measure the angle from which it started to slide
Ask the students to determine the steps to establish a possible relationship between the surface of the block and the angle from which it begins to slide

question: Read the following situation: In a sophomore class, the teacher aims to teach the molding process in sculpture. To start the class, the teacher looks for an example that would help relate this sculptural process to everyday life situations of the students. Which of the following situations would allow the teacher to exemplify the molding process?
options: Prepare gelatin
Fill a bottle with water
Put soil in a flowerpot
Remove a nut from its shell

question: In a 7th grade class, during the development of the unit "Creating in the plane the cultural identity", the teacher is addressing the objective "Apply elements and principles of visual language to communicate cultural identity". To do this, she has proposed that the students carry out a project in which, through collage, they design the space of their room. The teacher will assess the products with a rubric. Which of the following performances would best demonstrate the achievement of this learning?
options: They adapt cutouts of selected spaces to their liking to intervene in the room
They create a sample book of different wallpaper designs to decorate the walls of the room
They use the superimposition of fragments of photographs of objects to configure the space of the room
They apply cutting and pasting techniques to cover the various planes that make up the surface of the room

question: Which of the following technological solutions would allow a 5th-grade teacher to directly exemplify how the development of technology meets people's demands?
options: The invention of the ecological battery that recharges indefinitely with movement, responding to the need to save electrical energy
The production of the jacket with solar panels that allows generating electrical power, responding to the need to recharge mobile phones
The creation of the machine that is capable of recycling office paper waste and converting it into toilet paper, responding to the need to use resources efficiently
The development of plants capable of producing light without heat, responding to the need to replace traditional lighting methods with more efficient ones

question: A second-year high school teacher is planning a class where the objective is to "Solve problems using linear inequalities with one unknown". Considering that in previous classes they solved linear inequalities in one variable, which of the following activities would be appropriate to carry out in the development of the class, maintaining consistency with the aforementioned objective?
options: Solve an inequality using the graphical method and analyze its result
Present a problem and, with the students, model an inequality that would allow solving it and represent it graphically
Explain the symbols of inequalities and the interpretation of the possible values that the variable can take, given a context
Pose inequality exercises and solve in plenary those that present difficulty, to activate prior knowledge

question: A group of students complete their weekly work journal. Upon finishing, they submit a Gantt chart to the teacher, in which they establish the tasks assigned to each of the members during the execution of the project. At what stage of their project are the students?
options: Evaluation
Planning
Idea generation
Execution

question: A 3rd year teacher, during the Oral Argumentation unit, develops a discussion activity about energy efficiency. In this instance, one student defends the idea that a call should be made to not "waste" energy. Another student questions this stance, as they consider that it would be very subjective to classify the uses simply as wasting or not wasting. The teacher asks the first student to reformulate their idea.  What strategy would allow the student to reformulate their ideas more precisely within the presented discussion?
options: Use of consecutive sequences to specify the effects of wasting energy
Use of additive sequences to add ideas related to other inefficient ways of using energy
Use of explanatory sequences to determine the concrete meaning of what would constitute energy waste
Use of adversative sequences to establish partial contrasts between different wasteful uses of energy

question: Read the following situation:   Jose is a student with disabilities who is in 4th grade at a regular school with support services, he participates actively in class and in Mathematics he is capable of solving numerical exercises in the range of 1-20, using concrete materials. Currently, the classroom team is working on the learning objective  "demonstrate understanding of fractions with denominators 12, 10, 8, 6, 5, 4, 3, 2" and wants to assess whether the objective is being understood by Jose.  In relation to the previous situation, what evaluation process allows identifying Jose's achievement of the learning objective?
options: Coloring drawings of fractions represented in different geometric shapes that correspond to the whole unit and the divided unit
Answering a guide in groups where the students organize cards that represent fractions on a manipulable number line
Painting the number of pieces of a birthday cake that represents the fraction indicated in a work guide provided by the classroom team
Forming a group of 12 members and giving Jose a toy pizza that he must divide into equal parts to share with his classmates

question: Read the following situation:  A special education teacher is preparing her monthly planning for the students in 1st grade who have multiple disabilities. In this context, she has considered carrying out a series of activities related to spatial awareness through games and songs. Some concepts to recognize are: up, down, in, out, to one side, and to the other. In all activities, the students will use themselves and their peers as references.  Considering the Functional Ecological Approach, which of the following activities aligns with the planning designed for the special education teacher?
options: Listening to the reading of the story 'Up and Down' by Oliver Jeffers, discovering the various spatial locations of the characters
Recognizing the concepts presented in a written sheet where they must mark the position of different drawings in relation to a reference
Integrating spatial concepts into everyday actions, such as recognizing which classmate is to the left or right, organizing their backpack daily only with what they will use, among others
Incorporating graphic-plastic activities in which students can represent and describe their peers, family, and friends in different situations and locations in a model

question: Which of the following musical concepts is the most complex to tackle in initial auditory musical practice in elementary education?
options: Pitch variation
Timbre contrast
Change of meter
Change of tempo

question: As part of the unit "My Planet", an educator must explain to the children what elements make up the weather. Which of the following elements should the educator consider in their explanation?
options: Climate, precipitation, and seasons of the year
Wind, seasons of the year, and temperature
Precipitation, wind, and temperature
Climate, wind, and precipitation

question: A teacher is working with his students on the concept of symbolic color. During the class, one of his students asks him: "In everyday life, when is a symbolic color used?" Which of the following options contains a concrete example that the teacher could give his student?
options: In the colors used in the textile craft, which resemble the colors of a landscape
When I paint and represent my emotional state through a color
When I create an illustration for a book
In the color that unifies and makes, for example, a bus line recognizable

question: A teacher plans a class to work with her 8th grade students on the following objective: "Identify the dependent and independent variables in the factors that influence urine production".  What activity, proposed by the teacher, addresses the objective of the class?
options: Compare the composition of urine in a healthy person and a diabetic person
Build a chart on the effect of alcohol consumption on urine volume
Complete a table that records the color of urine at various times of the day
Complete a table with the concentration of different compounds in the various segments of the renal tubule

question: Read the following situation: In an 6th grade class, in the unit 'Visual creation, person and nature', the teacher addresses the following objective: 'Create a visual work based on the personal imaginations of the students, using sustainable materials'. Which of the following activities allows working towards achieving the objective set by the teacher?
options: Create a green engraving that illustrates your personal tastes
Make a woodcut that represents your most relevant personal characteristic
Build a sculpture with papier-mâché based on the concept of pollution
Design a poster with recycled materials that promotes recycling at school

question: A child, walking with his father in the rain, looks up at the sky and points to the clouds saying: 'The clouds are sad, that's why they are crying.' According to Jean Piaget's cognitive theory, what characteristic of thought is reflected in the child's statement?
options: Animism
Syncretism
Artificialism
Phenomenism

question: According to the following objective: "Read simple plans of your environment, using reference points and categories of relative position," what activities are most appropriate to evaluate it in Year 2?
options: Draw a plan of the classroom
Interpret the symbols of a city plan
Describe the location of places on a given map
Determine the coordinates of given points on a grid

question: A teacher, in the unit “The political organization of Chile”, is planning a class in which she will work on the principle of representativeness and will provide some examples of elected positions by popular vote, with the aim that her 6th or 7th-grade students understand “The importance of popular election for the democratic organization of the State of Chile”. Considering the objective of the class, which of the following indicators is pertinent for evaluating the students at the end of that class?
options: They identify who the political authorities elected by suffrage are, to represent the citizens
They explain the importance of authorities being elected for making decisions of public interest
They describe the main functions of the authorities that represent the citizens after an electoral process
They exemplify different forms of democratic participation that occur in society and in their close environment

question: Read the following case: In a class on Plato's theory of ideas, the professor explains the main characteristics of Platonic ideas, among which he points out that 'each idea is unique, being the representation of what is essential to a class of things'. A student, puzzled by the statement, responds that this could not be and gives as an example the case that an idea is thought by two people, a situation that contradicts the supposed unity of the idea. Bearing in mind Plato's theory of ideas, which option accounts for the concept underlying the student's error?
options: The student assumes that ideas arise from dialogue
The student understands that ideas are a product of the intellect
The student considers that ideas are relative
The student thinks that ideas are part of the intelligible world

question: Read the following situation: Marcelo, a 7th-grade student, has an intellectual disability condition, for which he receives support from the school's integration program. Over the years, he has formed friendships with several of his classmates, which has given him the confidence needed to participate in presentations in front of his peers. However, in situations where he faces strangers, he may appear shy and even somewhat evasive. As part of celebrating Book Day, his teacher planned a dramatization for the Language subject in front of other school courses. To carry out the activity, she chose the text 'The Little Prince,' as it is one of Marcelo and his classmates' favorite books. Considering Marcelo's characteristics and the activity planned by the teacher, in which of the following adaptations are the principles of accessibility and universal design applied best?
options: The teacher designs the activity considering two forms of participation: pre-recorded voices and live dramatizations, and asks the students to organize and sign up for one of the two tasks

The teacher plans an alternative activity for Marcelo and offers him to prepare a brief presentation on the text 'The Little Prince,' which he can present in the classroom context, in front of his classmates

The Language teacher assigns Marcelo the task of designing and creating, together with some friends, the scenery that will be used to support the dramatizations, thereby accommodating the student's interests and skills

The teacher assigns a peer tutor, ideally a close friend, to provide support and accompany Marcelo during the activities carried out in front of other courses, as this way, the student will feel more secure

question: Which of the following learning experiences allows children to develop an estimation problem?
options: Group buttons according to their size and color, and then find a new way to group them
Observe the attendance board, and then write in a numerical field how many children attended classes
Estimate the number of steps they believe are between their chair and the door, and then verify by walking to it
Order a series of wooden blocks from tallest to shortest, and then verify by measuring them with a ruler

question: In a kindergarten, a teacher will use observation records as an instrument to evaluate the learning of young children according to the following indicators of the Sociocultural Environment Understanding nucleus: Communicates habitual activities of their community. Describes their participation in habitual activities of their community. What learning experience would be relevant to observe to verify the achievements of the children in relation to the indicated indicators?
options: Draw activities that take place in the educational community
Share in the greeting circle what they did over the weekend
Gathered in small groups, imitate everyday actions they do at home
In groups, discuss the roles family members play in celebrations

question: According to Bowlby's theory, which of the following statements corresponds to a characteristic of attachment?
options: It is a process that lasts until the early years of life
It is a bond that must be stable and continuous to ensure proper development
It is a bond that determines all affective relationships in life and development
It is the first relationship of the newborn with its mother and cannot be replaced by another person

question: According to the postulates of Vygotsky's sociocultural theory, which of the following statements alludes to the role the teacher plays inside the classroom?
options: Plans and organizes experiences that allow students to stimulate their cognitive competences and develop strategic skills
Develops joint activities in which he/she acts as an essential mediator between the students' knowledge and their appropriation processes
Transmits to students the planned knowledge and develops his/her practices around a series of reinforcements and stimulus control
Organizes systematic and sequenced tasks in advance that allow him/her to impact on the students' cognitive processes and their modifiability

question: A teacher in a 7th grade class, in the unit on argumentation, will evaluate the following learning objective: "To value the diversity of perspectives, beliefs, and cultures present in their environment", through conducting a debate. Which of the following options presents the evaluation indicator that best responds to the stated learning objective?
options: They ask others about their position or opinion on a topic
They counter-argue using solid data based on reliable evidence
They ask questions and make comments, out of their own motivation, to delve deeper into a topic
They intervene with respect towards the heterogeneous opinions expressed by their peers

question: A 7th grade teacher, in the unit Visual Creation, person and nature, is working with her students on the central characteristics of land art, based on the observation and analysis of works by representative artists. Then, she proposes to apply what they have learned in an intervention project in a park close to the school and to take photographic records of the artwork. The teacher will evaluate the results obtained through an appreciation scale.  To collect evidence about her students' learning achievement, what indicator could the teacher consider including in the evaluation instrument?
options: They create a photographic record that evidences the process of making the artwork and its final result
They select natural or organic materials for their project, in accordance with the works of land art
They establish the relationship between the landscape where they install their work and the expressive purpose they want to communicate
They identify visual elements in representative works of land art to create their sketches prior to the production of the work

question: In a preschool, the educator tells the boys and girls that they will conduct an experiment, which involves placing an ice cube in the sun. She tells them that, in silence, each one should draw what they think will happen to the ice cube. Then they go out to the courtyard and she gives each one an ice cube to put in the sun. Back in the classroom, she asks them to draw next to the initial drawing what they actually observed happened to the ice cube. To finish, she asks them to explain how the two drawings are alike and different, and whether what they thought would happen actually happened. Which of the following scientific skills is reflected in the conclusion of this experience?
options: Recording
Prediction
Comparison
Experimentation

question: Which of the following instruments is the most appropriate to assess the level of communicative competencies of deaf students?
options: I.D.E.A
TEVI-R Test
Kendall Scale
Functional Assessment

question: A group of students is planning the design of a technological object that requires a mixed electrical circuit for its operation. Which of the options presents a group of tools necessary for the execution of their project?
options: Soldering iron, pliers, and saw
Electrical tape, cables, and tweezers
Cables, pliers, and soldering iron
Pliers, soldering iron, and wire stripper

question: In a 5th grade class, a teacher is working with her students on the following learning objective: "Investigate, describe, and locate the natural hazards that affect the locality to which they belong, such as earthquakes, tsunamis, floods, landslides, and volcanism." Along with this, the aim is to strengthen the development of geographical thinking, that is, the ability to observe and identify the multiple relationships established between humans and the environment, promoting an active attitude of environmental protection among students.  Which of the following activities would enable the promotion of geographical thinking and address the proposed objective?
options: Students classify the different types of natural hazards present in their region, according to the area they affect, determining common patterns
Students make a map of their region locating the natural disasters that have occurred in their region in recent years
Students describe a natural hazard that has occurred in their region, explaining its causes, its effects on the community, and what actions can be taken to reduce the risks
Students research a natural hazard present in their locality, indicating what type it is, what its main characteristics are, and how often it has occurred in the last century

question: Read the following 3rd Grade Learning Objective: "Demonstrate understanding of the concept of angle: identifying examples of angles in the environment, estimating the measure of angles, using 45° and 90° angles as references". Which of the following activities is most appropriate to assess their understanding?
options: Classify different types of angles
Create angles of specific measures
Recognize different angles in a drawing
Measure different angles using a protractor

question: A third grade teacher is working on the characteristics of 3D figures with his students, and has designed an activity to help them get familiar with the concepts of faces, vertices, and edges. After explaining what each of the new concepts consists of, he presents the following 3D figures:  [Pictures of a pyramid, a prism, and a cube]  The teacher asks them: "Which of the following geometric solids has 6 vertices in total?", to which some students respond: "The cube".  Which of the following activities would help the students understand the new concepts better?
options: Associate 3D figures with real objects
Build different 3D figures from their nets
Identify 2D figures and their equivalent in 3D figures
Differentiate the elements of different 3D figures made from concrete material

question: Read the following situation:  Nahuel is a 2nd-grade student characterized by preferring activities that require manipulating objects and moving around, as he likes to wander around the classroom permanently. The area where he faces the greatest challenges is with his limited vocabulary, even to name everyday items such as school supplies. Given the low repertoire of words he manages, he requires support to classify a group of elements according to a given criterion.  Based on the above, which of the following strategies supports the development of the classification skill in the student?
options: Carry out different classification activities, taking advantage of all the contents from the subjects with which categorization is possible. Ask him to name each element and request a general category by asking: What do all these together call?

Take advantage of different everyday instances in the classroom, for example, for organizing, where the student must classify objects, asking him to name each of them according to their utility, to later place them on shelves or where they belong

Designate an active role within the classroom that allows him to engage in activities involving manipulation of objects, such as, for example, distributing materials. At that moment, ask the student to help, requesting him to mention what elements he is distributing and what they are for

Use work cards that contain different everyday items and ask the student to name each of them to confirm his knowledge of the objects and the words to name them. Then, ask him to circle or color all the objects according to the designated classification criterion

question: A teacher has worked with their students on the quadratic function and wants to assess if they are capable of relating this function to everyday contexts. Which of the following activities will best enable them to achieve this goal?
options: Students solve various problems that involve quadratic functions
Students determine quadratic functions that model various situations
Students identify from the graph maximum values, minimum values, and roots of quadratic functions
Students graph quadratic functions that represent everyday situations and analyze their parameters

question: In the subject of Science for a 6th grade class, the teacher will present a video to review the learning about the states of water and how they are observed in nature. This class includes students with moderate hearing disabilities who have reached a good level of literacy development. Therefore, the teacher decides to implement a strategy that allows these students to access the information she will provide.  Considering the above, which of the following strategies is relevant for the students with profound deafness in this class to access the content worked on by the teacher?
options: Incorporate subtitles into the video that will be presented in class
Provide a summary text with the main ideas and a glossary of terms
Offer a signed presentation by a student with hearing disability
Group the class to create a synthesis of relevant concepts related to the states of water

question: Which of the following performances corresponds to that of a boy or girl who is in the syllabic-alphabetic stage of writing?
options: Copies their name
Writes familiar words
Writes separate letters they know
Copies simple phrases to communicate something

question: A high school teacher aims for her students to be able to distinguish between autonomous and heteronomous morality in different ethical systems. What activity would be most appropriate for her students to achieve the proposed learning objective?
options: The teacher creates a chart on the board with the main ideas of the existentialist ethical system of Sartre and the Kantian system
The teacher designs and explains to the students a conceptual map that includes the ideas of Kantian and hedonistic ethics, based on the source of origin or prescription of norms for the individual
The teacher asks the students to make a summary with the central ideas of the ethical proposals of Aristotle and Kant, considering the source of origin of norms for the individual
The teacher, together with her students, develops a comparative chart based on the goals pursued by the moral systems proposed by hedonists and Stoics

question: A physics teacher, to conclude the unit "Light", proposes the following evaluative activity to her first-year high school students: Below are two situations in which bulbs of different sizes illuminate a screen. a) Compare the type of shadow that will result on the screen in situations 1 and 2. b) Justify your answer using the corpuscular model of light and geometric optics. What scientific skill can be assessed through this activity?
options: Represent information through scientific models
Interpret data from scientific investigations
Formulate explanations based on scientific theories and concepts
Identify the limitations that scientific models and theories present

question: Read the following situation: An educator is starting to work on identifying numbers to develop quantification skills in preschoolers. As a first activity, she wants the boys and girls to be able to identify the number 1. Given her knowledge of the level, she knows that Vicente is an active child, who learns much better by using his body, so she will ask him to imitate a number 1. On the other hand, she also knows that Martina and Clemente have many musical skills and learn quickly through songs, so she will include some in the experience. Which pedagogical principle of Early Childhood Education is mainly highlighted in the planning of this educator?
options: Empowerment
Uniqueness
Relationship
Play

question: Read the following situation:  Pedro is eleven years old, has an intellectual disability, and is in 4th grade at a special school. He shows a preference for materials he can manipulate, especially if it allows him to classify under some criteria. When faced with tasks or activities that interest him, he can maintain his attention until he completes them, and if he encounters difficulties, he is able to ask his teacher for help. His greatest challenges arise in his autonomy to carry out daily routines, such as what he needs to do in the morning upon arriving at school and during meals or personal hygiene, because his mother tends to do things for him. Furthermore, he still needs to learn to write and read, as he only manages at a functional level, and expand the numerical range he currently dominates up to 100. For this reason, he is being supported from the dimensions: Context and Adaptive Behavior.  Based on the above situation and considering the Multidimensional Approach to intellectual disability, what is the reason that justifies the support provided in these dimensions?
options: Because support is needed for the transformation of attitudes and beliefs coming from his microsystem, as well as to promote the development of practical and conceptual skills
Because participation is fundamental for the development of individuals in their contexts; moreover, it is necessary to develop practical skills such as writing, reading, and handling numbers
Because skills should be promoted that allow his development in different areas of social life, in addition to coordinating support with the family regarding priorities for his learning

Because it is necessary to have family support to encourage his autonomy and participation in various areas; furthermore, the development of his cognitive ability will allow for greater and better adaptation

question: Read the following situation:  A young girl expresses her interest in participating in a sports activity to her family and teachers. Together, they analyze the possibility of engaging in an adapted sport, where the girl feels comfortable and meets her current interests. She has a disability characterized by severe motor impairment (tetraparesis). She moves her arms as a block forward, backward, upward, and downward; understands and follows semi-complex verbal instructions and demonstrates social skills related to empathy and responsibility.  Considering the above situation, which of the following adapted sports is suitable for the girl's motor characteristics?
options: Bocce
Basketball
Handball
Table Tennis

question: Which of the following options shows a common misconception about changes in matter that students in 7th/8th grade usually have?
options: Chemical changes can be reversible or irreversible
Boiling water corresponds to an irreversible change
The temperature remains constant when water changes from solid to liquid
The mass of a substance remains constant when it undergoes a chemical change

question: After studying with her high school sophomores the concept of elastic constant, a teacher proposes the following teaching-learning activity: Students organize themselves into small teams and experimentally determine the elastic constant of a spring, recording their results in a table. Similarly, they determine for which ranges of force the spring obeys Hooke's law. To conclude, they prepare a report in the form of a poster, which is subsequently presented to their classmates. Which of the following scientific research skills can be directly developed through this activity?
options: Evaluate scientific research in order to improve it, considering the validity and reliability of the results
Formulate questions and/or problems, based on scientific knowledge, that can be solved through scientific research
Explain and argue with evidence from scientific investigations, both orally and in writing, including tables, graphs, models, and ICT

Plan various experimental research designs that answer a question and/or problem based on various sources of scientific information, considering the appropriate use of instruments and materials to ensure the collection of reliable data

question:
For a 12th grade class, during the unit "Reality and Freedom", a teacher, in order to develop an interpretative analysis of a poetic text from a formal level, gives her students the following poem to read:
"I would be less punished if your complexion were not nardo for my sight, nardo, your skin thistle for my touch, thistle, your voice your era for my ear, your era. Your era is your voice for my ear, your era, and I burn in your voice and around you I burn, and I take time to burn what I take time to offer you my era, my voice for yours my era. Your hand is a thorn if I feel it, thorn, wave your body if I reach it, wave, close once, but a thousand not close. Heron is my sorrow, slender and sad heron, alone like a sigh and an I, alone, stubborn in her error and in her disgrace stubborn." From The Unceasing Ray, by Miguel Hernández.
Which of the following questions asked by the teacher to the students is related to an interpretative analysis from the formal level of the text?
options: To whom is the lyrical speaker addressing, and what characteristics do they present?
To what traditional strophic form does the structure of the poem correspond?
What meaning does the heron acquire in the last stanza, in relation to the lyrical speaker?
What is the relationship between what the speaker says and the sounds that are repeated throughout the poem?

question: A teacher sets the following learning objective: "Understand oral texts to obtain information and develop curiosity about the world." Which of the following planned activities to work on this objective considers the students' context?
options: Follow oral instructions to build a model of a school
Listen to anecdotes about the experiences of their peers during the holidays
Participate in group discussions about the characteristics of the characters in a read story
Move around the room blindfolded following aloud instructions given by a peer

question: A nursery teacher in the Younger Nursery Class level is planning learning experiences to jointly work on the following objectives:  - Mathematical Thinking Core: “To acquire the notion of permanence of objects and significant people, through games with various everyday objects.”  - Corporality and Movement Core: “To coordinate manipulation movements by placing objects in relation to their own body, such as bringing small objects closer using the index finger and thumb in opposition.”  Which of the following learning experiences allows the educator to address the stated objectives?
options: Hide objects of various characteristics around the room and invite the children to explore the space, discovering the hidden materials
Offer baskets full of everyday objects and other empty baskets, and motivate the children to move the objects and exchange them
Distribute throughout the room containers with objects of different characteristics, and invite the children to manipulate, combine, and group them
Present closed boxes with holes and small objects that can be manually inserted and retrieved by the children

question: A teacher presents the following newspaper headline to his 6th-grade students: 'Environmental pollution in Chile causes more than four thousand premature deaths due to cardiopulmonary diseases.' Based on this newspaper headline, the teacher asks his students to prepare and conduct a debate on preventive measures to solve this problem. Which of the following learning indicators is consistent with the activity proposed by this teacher?
options: They analyze graphs related to premature deaths from cardiovascular diseases in the last ten years
They compare measures to reduce environmental pollution rates applied in other parts of the world
They argue about the advantages and disadvantages of possible solutions that could be implemented to reduce environmental pollution
They investigate the reasons why pollution rates increase in winter and the measures that have been taken to reduce these rates

question: A teacher aims for his students to identify the purpose of using dialogical sequences in different narratives. To do this, he proposes reviewing different texts, including reading the following fragment: Also the poet, if he is a true poet, must perpetually repeat "I don't know". With each verse he tries to answer, but at the moment he puts the final period, doubts assail him and he begins to notice that his answer is temporary and in no way satisfactory. Then he tries again and again, so that the successive signs of his dissatisfaction with himself are held together with a huge clip by literary historians to call them "The Work." Sometimes I fantasize about improbable situations. I imagine, for example, in my audacity, that I have the opportunity to talk with Ecclesiastes, the author of a shocking lament about the vanity of all human enterprises. I would have bowed very deeply before him, as he is -at least for me- one of the most important poets. But then I would have taken him by the hand: "There is nothing new under the sun," you wrote, Ecclesiastes. Nevertheless, You yourself were born new under the sun. And the poem you created is also new under the sun, as before You nobody had written it. [...] And also I would like to ask you, Ecclesiastes, what would you like to write now, again under the sun? Something with which to complete your ideas, or maybe you're tempted to deny some of them? [...] For you will not say "I have already written everything, I have nothing to add." No poet can say this, much less one as great as You. (I don't know, Wislawa Szymborska.) Of the following questions posed by the teacher, which one enables students to recognize the purpose of the dialogical sequence in the previous fragment?
options: What register of speech does the broadcaster use to address Ecclesiastes?
What are the central themes addressed in Ecclesiastes' book according to the broadcaster?
What is the contrast that can be drawn between literary historians and Ecclesiastes?
What is the relationship between Ecclesiastes' reference and the idea of the transient nature of the poet's certainties?

question: An educator intends to address the following learning objective of the Sociocultural Environment Core: 'Understand the roles played by members of their family and community and their contribution to the common well-being.' What experience is most suitable to develop this learning objective?
options: Take a walk through the nearest open market and ask the children to buy fruits and vegetables while wandering freely
Arrange for the visit of a parent who will tell the story of how the neighborhood was formed and how it has changed over time
Visit local shops and ask the children to interview, with prepared questions, the people who work there
Take a tour around the vicinity of the establishment and, at certain points, ask the children to draw the people who catch their attention

question: A teacher in the 2nd year secondary class will work with her students on the unit Social Problems in the Visual Arts, developing the objective: "Make critical judgments of works that present social problems, based on the analysis of the symbolic elements and contexts of production of the works." To address this objective, she will ask them to create a sculpture based on this theme.  What sequence of activities allows to address the proposed learning objective?
options: Analyze a contemporary sculptural work, identify the key concepts, and design a sculpture based on one of those concepts
Design a sculpture based on social problems, evaluate the proposal with a peer, and create the sculpture based on that evaluation
Write an essay on the representation of social problems in the visual arts, synthesize relevant ideas from the text, and create a sculpture based on the essay
Research sculptures that address the theme, establish the link between the elements of visual language and the expressive purpose of these works, and design a sculpture based on the research

question: Read the following activity: A Music teacher conducts a listening session in class of a concert for two soloists and orchestra. During the audition, he invites students to distinguish the melodic lines of each soloist and imagine that both instruments relate to each other as if they were holding a conversation. What is the main learning objective of the previous activity?
options: Exercise internal audition
Encourage aesthetic appreciation
Perform a structural analysis
Develop body expression

question: A 4th-grade teacher who worked with their students on the relationship between mass and volume of objects ends the class by presenting four balls of equal size but different materials: rubber, clay, glass, and steel. Then they ask: “Based on what we have seen, what is the relationship between mass and volume of these objects?” Most students answer that since all balls are of the same size, they have the same volume, so the mass should be the same. Which of the following activities could the students do in a next class to correctly understand the relationship between mass and volume of objects?
options: Submerge one of the balls in a flask with water and note the value obtained. Then do the same with the other balls. Finally, compare the results and relate them to the materials of each one

Weigh the 4 balls on a digital scale and note the results. Then compare the results and contrast them with the materials of which the balls are made. Finally, conclude about the relationship between the results and the volume of the balls

Submerge the balls one by one, successively, in an ungraded cup and mark the water displacement reached by each with a marker on the cup. Finally, compare the results and relate them to the mass of each ball

Place one of the balls in one pan of a beam balance and the other 3 in the other pan and record what happens. Then, place 2 balls in one pan and the other 2 in the other, and record the result, and so on. Finally, compare what is recorded each time with the material of each ball and relate it to the mass of each one

question: A secondary teacher in the 1st Year, in the unit "Development and Implementation of a Digital Service", has asked the students to develop projects that address identified needs in the school. To start the project, the students defined the need to know what materials are stored in the Art Workshop, in order to discard those that were not used because they were kept and therefore expired. Then, guided by the professor, they discussed possible solutions to avoid the problem of wasting materials, and decided to carry out a digital inventory. After that, to design their own solution, they analyzed different applications that allow creating an inventory.  To present the design proposal for the service using ICT tools, what prior knowledge is necessary for the students to have acquired?
options: To know how to link the digital solution with the identified problem
To manage digital resources for editing images and text
To know the basic programming language to develop the application
To know how to use design software to create technical drawings in 2D and 3D

question: A preschool educator will evaluate the following indicator from the Core Understanding of the Socio-Cultural Environment: Uses household utensils and technological objects in daily situations and games.  Which of the following evaluation strategies allows collecting evidence of this indicator?
options: In the snack area, place cutlery and napkins in separate containers; and at meal time, record when the children are asked to distribute these items
In the construction area, provide boxes and disused containers of different sizes; and during playtime, record the types of structures the children build with these materials
In the home area, incorporate new materials such as handbags, briefcases, and office supplies; and during playtime, record the activities the children develop with these items

In the playground area, provide different materials in the sandbox like shovels, buckets, and rakes; and during playtime, record the children's performance when they are named a material and asked to store it in a specific container

question: In relation to the evolution of the telephone as a technological solution, which of these questions could the teacher ask to generate cognitive conflict in their students?
options: What advanced functions does a smartphone offer compared to previous phones?
How has the evolution of the telephone been throughout history?
Are smartphones an advance or a setback from a social point of view?
What types of human needs are satisfied with the appearance of smartphones?

question: Why is the globe useful as a teaching resource?
options: Because it allows observing the shape of the Earth in real terms
Because it helps to recognize the entirety of the hemispheres at once
Because it serves to study the latitude of the Earth in more detail
Because it allows accurately locating any place on Earth

question:
Read the following situation:
Felix is in 3rd grade and receives support from the school's integration program, as he has support needs due to his intellectual disability. One of Felix's favorite activities is drawing, so he can often be seen during recess making illustrations of everything that catches his attention. In the subject of Natural Sciences, the teacher aims to address the learning objective: "Observe and describe, through experimental research, the needs of plants and their relationship with the root, stem, and leaves," using the school's garden as a supporting resource, which the students will visit weekly.
Considering Felix's characteristics and the principles of authentic assessment, which of the following assessment procedures is appropriate to evaluate the stated objective?
options: Presentation of a poster that includes illustrations and relevant information about what was observed in the garden
Presentation of a drawing made from the observation of a selected plant in the school's garden
Portfolio with weekly field journals that include drawings and brief descriptions of what was observed in the garden
Presentation about a selected plant with illustrations and/or a PowerPoint that includes photographs and brief descriptions

question: A teacher will introduce the topic of patterns with her class and wants to propose a starting activity that is motivating and allows students to access the content. Which of the following examples is suitable to start teaching this content?
options: A figure made from a Tangram
A simple tiling of paving stones
A board game with dice
A symmetrical image

question: Read the following situation:  The first grade students of a special school are working on the subject of Natural Sciences, specifically, the focus on living beings and ecosystems. The classroom team aims to develop skills such as: observing, describing, classifying, and recognizing through exploration. For this, they must consider that some students have auditory and visual remnants, reduced mobility, and cognitive disabilities to varying degrees.  Based on the Natural Sciences lesson being developed, which of the following pedagogical actions promotes the inclusion of authentic learning situations?
options: Bring some domestic animals to the classroom that they can look at, touch, smell, or listen to according to their preferences
Organize outdoor games of haptic exploration, in order to discover elements of the everyday ecosystem
Show videos of domestic animals, in order to react to their sounds, shapes, colors, and movements
Provide natural elements from the schoolyard, for them to compare according to shapes, color, weight, and size

question: During the unit related to drugs, a teacher aims for her students to develop the ability to determine the variables of a study. What evaluative activity would allow this teacher to measure the achievement of the stated objective?
options: Construction of a cigarette filter to determine its toxic substances
Tabulation of results from a survey constructed by the students on factors influencing early alcohol consumption
Construction of a theoretical framework on a research related to the causes of marijuana consumption in adolescents
Construction of models built by the students on the mechanisms of action of stimulant drugs at the level of chemical synapses

question: Read the following situation: A teacher with their first year high school students, at the end of the unit 'Mural Painting', wants their students to perform a self-evaluation of the following objective: 'Evaluate their murals, considering the relationship between expressive purpose, materiality, and visual language'. What activity would allow measuring the achievement of the aforementioned objective?
options: They appreciate the elements of visual language used in their mural projects
They analyze their murals considering the theme, sustainable materials, color, shape, and lines inscribed in the composition
They experiment with different materialities according to the theme of the project
They establish relationships between the sustainable materialities of the project with the proposed shapes, lines, colors, and textures

question: What prior learning is necessary for students to develop in order to understand the concept of radioactive series?
options: The structure of the atomic nucleus
The atomic constitution of matter
The electronic structure of atoms
The isotopic composition of an element

question: In a class of the "Fundamentals of Morality" unit, the teacher is working on the concept of human nature in Hobbes and Rousseau. A student suggests that the goodness or badness of human beings is determined by the people around them. What explanation can the teacher give to address this error?
options: The state of nature is a hypothetical state to criticize the construction of the State based on human nature
In the state of nature, there have to be good and bad people for there to be a contract
The goodness or badness of human beings are essential states that precede the realization of the social contract
The state of nature is a hypothetical state to explain how goodness or badness determines the type of resulting government

question:
Read the following situation:
Gabriela is a 3rd-grade student and has an intellectual disability. Among her characteristics, she needs support to focus her attention (she can maintain it for 3 to 5 minutes), which makes it difficult for her to start and finish a task; she also requires constant help to understand what she must do in class (especially when activities have more than two or three steps) and, on the other hand, she always seeks adult approval due to her insecurity.
Considering Gabriela's characteristics, the classroom team proposes the following work strategy:
- Support focusing attention on the task by clearing her environment of distractions.
- Offer "The task path" so that, autonomously, she can evaluate her process before moving forward.
- Ensure she understood the instructions through verbalizing them and, during the task development, cover what has already been done so that her attention centers on the relevant elements.
Considering the previous context, what is the reason that best justifies the design of the proposed strategy?
options: This strategy will meet the student's needs, as it considers support towards understanding instructions and sequencing of thought that will guide execution in each task or activity that she must perform, encouraging planning of strategies and steps that will lead her to succeed in these

This strategy will meet the student's needs, as it focuses on the attentional processes that are the basis of all cognitive activity: focused and sustained attention. Additionally, it provides concrete and personalized support, taking into consideration her attention spans, which is fundamental support for Gabriela's cognitive characteristics

This strategy will meet the needs of the student described in the context, as it focuses on input cognitive processes, while also guiding the development of greater autonomy and metacognitive habits, which encourage understanding of her own knowledge and how she processes gathered information, which favors behavior self-regulation progressively

This strategy will meet the student's needs, as it will promote key aspects of executive functioning where she needs support. Additionally, it encourages autonomy and behavior self-regulation through promoting cognitive and metacognitive strategies that allow her to monitor her execution according to the goal of each activity or task

question: A teacher of a 3rd grade worked with their students on expressing multiplication as repeated addition and conducted an assessment to monitor their learning. In this assessment, students had to write the multiplication performed, its expression using the addition of equal addends, and the expression in words of the multiplication operation in each solved problem. Upon review, the teacher noticed that one of them presented the following response to one of the problems: Problem: If I have 6 hens and each of them has two legs, how many legs are there in total? Answer: 2·6=6+6=12 "Two times six". Despite the final result being correct, which of the following feedback comments would provide necessary information for the student to correctly interpret the data in this problem?
options: You reached the correct number, but is there another way to represent the problem? Make a drawing of the situation and then compare it with your result. Is there any difference?
Your result is correct, but do you remember that in the multiplication tables 2 times 6 is the same as 6 times 2? They are the same, but they are not the same in this problem. Did you notice?
The result is correct, but do you think you represented the multiplication using all the necessary addends? Notice that you wrote "2 times 6" and not "6 times 2"
The number you calculated is correct, but are you sure you followed all the steps to solve a problem? Review to see what step you missed

question:
In a 3rd year of high school, a teacher begins to work with her students on a painting project from an expressionist approach.
Which of the following activities could the teacher use to approach the project from the approach indicated?
options: Ask the students about ideas or themes they want to paint about, based on their interests and motivations, to work on their projects
Present a pictorial concept as a starting point for the development of the activity, for the students to resolve and apply in their painting projects
Develop a dialogue with the students about painting techniques they could apply in their projects, to promote participation, critical analysis, and communication of their interests
Propose to the students an investigation on a painter of their personal choice, identifying the pictorial characteristics of their work, to then apply them in their painting projects

question: Read the following situation:  Sergio is sixteen years old and is in 3rd year of a technical professional school specializing in Agriculture. He has acquired blindness due to eye trauma a year ago. His rehabilitation process has been complex, both emotionally and adaptively. The classroom team is studying the most suitable technology resources to provide the best support for his learning, since this year he has restarted his studies, considering that this student has not shown motivation for reading and writing in his school history.  Considering Sergio's situation, which of the following technology resources are essential to promote his current academic performance?
options: Cell phone with Voice Over and SIRI
Screen magnifier and OCR
Screen reader JAWS and Braille display
Screen reader NVDA and Perkins machine

question: Read the following situation:  A first grade teacher has planned an expository activity in which the students must provide a brief description of the location and function of the senses. In the class, there are two students diagnosed with specific language disorders who have a limited production of simple syntactic structures, with combinations of two or three words.  Which of the following curricular adaptations is appropriate to meet the support needs of these students?
options: Eliminate the exposition and description of the functions of the senses
Flexibility in the time allocated for the presentation of the information about the senses
Incorporate the theme of care measures for the organs of the senses in the exposition
Prioritize the naming of the senses and their location in the exposition, postponing the description of functions

question: A Language teacher, aiming for the students to identify the orthographic error, shows a PowerPoint with different texts that contain errors. One of the texts is the following: The teacher asks them about the error in the text, and the students have difficulties identifying it. Which of the following actions would allow the students to identify the orthographic error in the example?
options: Emphasize in class the homographs and find in the example the word that has its pair
Emphasize in class the homophones and find in the example the word that has its pair
Emphasize in class the polysemous words and find the meanings of the terms present in the example
Emphasize in class the paronyms and find the etymology of the terms present in the example

question: A high school teacher designs the following sequence of activities for students to develop the skill of making conjectures: - Graph the function ƒ(x) = √x manually and using software. - Identify the domain and range of the function ƒ and its intersections with the axes. - Graph the function g(x) = √x + b for some b ∈ R, b ≠ 0. - Identify the domain and range of the function g and its intersections with the axes. - Graph the function h(x) = √x - a for some a ∈ R+. - Identify the domain and range of the function h and its intersections with the axes. - Graph the functions g and h again for different values of a and b. Which of the following questions, posed by the teacher, would most directly guide the students to achieve the proposed objective?
options: How does the graph of the function ƒ(x) = √x change when the parameters are modified?
What do the intersections of the function with the X-axis and Y-axis depend on?
How do the maximum and minimum values of the function ƒ change when the parameters are modified?
How is the domain and range of the functions ƒ, g, and h determined, respectively?

question: A high school teacher addresses the students with the following learning objective: "Identify possible positive or negative impacts of current technological innovations on the environment and society." After giving the presentation and explaining how technological advances are limited by the low availability of some resources necessary for their development, the teacher realizes that the relationship is still not clear enough and decides to incorporate an example to complement his explanation. Which of the following examples would be appropriate for the teacher to use to illustrate his explanation?
options: The limitations in the expansion purposes of a factory because it does not have the necessary economic resources to do so
The lack of planning and commitment from local municipalities prevents investment and improvement in the development of rainwater collector technology
The construction of a public works project, such as a road, that faces restrictions because the land needed is owned by the private sector
The decision by some automobile companies to stop producing internal combustion vehicles due to the future scarcity of fossil fuels

question: In a 2nd grade class of a special school, seven students with Autism Spectrum Disorder are attending. The teacher will be working on the learning objective: 'Develop curiosity for words or expressions they do not know and acquire the habit of discovering their meaning.' Within the class, there are two learning groups: one consists of five students who have acquired syllable-level reading skills and the other group includes two students who have not yet acquired reading skills. Given this situation, the teacher must prepare a lesson that meets the proposed learning objective, but with activities suitable for each of the two groups of described students.  Which of the following strategies is the most appropriate for the students to expand their vocabulary?
options: The teacher will provide the group with acquired reading skills with worksheets containing word searches, where they will identify words and then look up their meanings in dictionaries; the group without acquired reading skills will work with guides composed of words and images, which they will have to match when they recognize the correspondences

The teacher will present the group without acquired reading skills posters with words to work on matching and selecting drawings of significant words for the students; the other group with acquired reading skills will work on selecting words with posters with which they should form and compose sentences and read them aloud

The teacher will work using operational methodology with all seven students, reading a short story to enhance reading comprehension, aiming for the students to incorporate more vocabulary by replacing words with drawings, allowing them to form increasingly larger sentences

The teacher will show the group without acquired reading skills two letters, P and M, which they will need to associate with vowels to start reading syllables that they will later relate to drawings accompanied by the initial syllable; the group with reading skills will search for words they do not recognize in a guide with a story and will look up their meanings on their tablets

question: To evaluate the different dimensions of the development of a student with Autism Spectrum disorder entering 1st Year in a school with a Special Needs Department, which of the following assessment procedures is relevant?
options: Conduct a psychoeducational assessment, together with the psychologist, incorporating a WAIS instrument, to understand the cognitive level at which the student is
Develop diagnostic tests, considering the individual characteristics of the student and, if necessary, adapt them together with the special education teacher specialist
Gather personal, social, and cultural information with the psychoeducational team, specify the level of communication and autonomy, to design and plan the educational response of the student
Implement a diagnostic assessment process, initially formative until reaching summative, with the teachers of each subject, to define all the adaptations that the student requires

question: A teacher of 2nd grade of secondary school tackles the writing of expository texts. During the activity, he reviews the following statements from texts written by his students: "They have made all efforts to reduce gas emissions, therefore, they have not achieved it" and "No visible changes have been made in the organic waste treatment method, and monetary contributions have arrived to implement them." What is the main difficulty evident in the texts presented by the students?
options: The impossibility of establishing connectors to advance in writing
The lack of connectors in general in their different semantic relations
The lack of knowledge of semantic relations for textual construction
The incorrect use of connectors to establish relations of opposition between ideas

question: During the Poetry unit in a 6th-grade class, in an evaluation context, a teacher asks the students to read the following excerpt from a poem by Nicanor Parra, so they can analyze it, identifying elements of poetic language.  Dreams  I dream of a table and a chair   I dream that I turn around in a car   I dream that I am filming a movie   I dream of a gas station   I dream that I am a luxury tourist   I dream that I am hanging from a cross   I dream that I am eating fish   I dream that I am crossing a bridge   I dream of a neon sign.    Several students comment that the poem makes no sense because it is repetitive and the lines do not connect with each other.  Considering the students' comments, what question from the teacher could guide them to make a correct interpretation of the poem?
options:
How does that repetition and apparent lack of meaning relate to the title of the poem?  
What rhetorical devices does the poem present that allow us to reconstruct the meaning of the lines?  
What feeling does the lyrical speaker want to express through the dreamlike images he constructs?  
What is the name of the rhetorical figure that consists of the repetition of the same phrase in several consecutive lines?

question: In a 2nd grade secondary class, whose objective is for students to understand the ovarian and uterine cycle, a group of students gives a presentation about this cycle. While explaining the process of ovulation, a student says: "Once the follicle matures and the luteinizing hormone increases, it releases from the ovary and stops producing estrogen. Then, a new structure appears in the ovary called the corpus luteum, which begins to produce progesterone." What difficulty in achieving the class objective is evident in the response of this student?
options: Assuming that the absence of one of the ovarian hormones is the trigger for ovulation
Considering that ovulation is independent of the plasma concentrations of gonadotropic hormones
Establishing an erroneous relationship between the stages of the ovarian cycle and the gonadotropic hormones that intervene in them
Assuming that the corpus luteum originates independently in the ovary and not from a preexisting structure in this organ

question: Concerning how knowledge is acquired, how do the behaviorist and sociocultural learning theories differ?
options: Behaviorism states that a child constructs knowledge through trial and error, while the sociocultural theory does so through interactions
Behaviorism proposes that concepts are acquired through cognitive modifiability, while in sociocultural theory, they are acquired through equilibration processes
Behaviorism suggests that knowledge is achieved through reinforcement, whereas sociocultural theory emphasizes mediation by the environment
Behaviorism postulates that understanding of the world is achieved through cognitive processes, while sociocultural theory emphasizes modeling

question: A second-grade teacher is working on the Greek and Roman civilizations with her students and, as part of the class activities, she divides the class into groups, giving each group a card that refers to a representative element of these cultures, such as: theater, law, architecture and art, mythology, philosophy, language, etc. Each group must investigate the element assigned to them using sources provided by the teacher and indicate which aspects of it can be recognized in today's elements and which cannot. On which concept of history didactics might the teacher be focusing this activity with her students?
options: Permanence
Evidentiality
Evolution and progress
Continuity and change

question: In the context of the unit 'The exercise of citizenship and systems of political representation', a fourth-year teacher hands out the following source to their students and allocates a few minutes for them to read it individually: In 2011, law 20500 was enacted, which allows the creation of the Communal Councils of Civil Society (COSOC), which have an advisory nature and can support achieving the objective of influencing the design, execution, and evaluation of communal, sectoral public policies and the municipality's own management. These must meet at least four times a year. [Undersecretariat of Regional and Administrative Development (Adaptation)]. Which of the following learning outcomes can be achieved with the previous source?
options: Identify instances of citizen participation
Explain the principle of political representativeness
Recognize the functions of local authorities
Describe the process of political-administrative decentralization

question: Eighth grade students, after learning about the functionality of software for building apps, decided to design their own for mobile devices to help manage their tasks and educational activities. What criteria should be present in evaluating the design of this solution?
options: Graphic design and sustainability
User security and environmental impact
Planning and impact on the school community
Functionality and impact on the school community

question: Read the following situation:  In a 1st grade class, through a digital resource, the consonant groups BL and BR are worked on. One of the activities prepared for the beginning of the class consists of the students observing a picture that presents various objects, whose names are associated with those combinations, clicking on the objects to hear their names and then classifying them into one group of consonants or another. Subsequently, they perform the analysis and synthesis of the word with the teacher.  Based on the above, what cognitive process predominates in the initial activity?
options: Visual memory
Syllabic awareness
Phonemic awareness
Visual discrimination

question:
A teacher will work with their 7th grade students on the learning objective related to understanding the circle as a geometric place, and calculating its perimeter and area.
Which of the following prior knowledge from the areas of Numbers and Algebra can be connected with the geometric concepts that will be worked on in this objective?
options: Introduction to irrational numbers including the value of π
Inverse proportionality relating the area and diameter of a circle
Operations with decimal numbers for understanding measurement units
Direct proportionality relating the perimeter and diameter of a circle

question: Read the following situation:   Maria Luisa is in 4th grade at a school with an inclusive education program. She has low vision due to a diagnosis of aniridia and horizontal bilateral nystagmus. In the class of History, Geography, and Social Sciences, the classroom team will work on the following learning objective: Compare the presence of physical and natural characteristics (such as mountains, rivers, deserts, beaches, climate, among others) in different landscapes of her region. To do this, the classroom team needs to plan a strategy that favors Mari­a Luisa's participation.  What strategy facilitates Mari­a Luisa's participation and achievement of the objective?
options: Present, through audible material, the different types of geographical reliefs
Make an infographic with tactile material of the different types of geographical reliefs
Create tactile explanatory cards for each of the different geographical reliefs
Build a model with tactile clues that incorporates the different geographical reliefs

question: A preschool educator in the Minor Transition level, within the Citizenship and Coexistence module, will work on the following learning objective: "Understand that some of their actions and decisions regarding the development of games and collective projects influence those of their peers." To achieve this, she invites the children to participate in a playful experience on the patio, where they will organize into teams to carry out competitive games. At the end of the experience, what intervention by the educator would promote the achievement of the learning objective?
options: What were the rules of the game? What was the most difficult to comply with? Did you feel integrated by your team members?
How did you feel about participating in the game? What did you think about the result of the competition? What would you change next time?
What decisions did you make to organize into teams? Was there any conflict among you? If so, how did you resolve it?
How did you contribute to the game? What would happen if you had to solve the challenges on your own? What would have happened if one of you had not participated?

question: Read the following situation: In a 4th-grade class at a school with an inclusive education program, during a Technology Education lesson, the teacher has asked the students to work in groups to create a simple model of a house. To do this, she provides them with written instructions and the materials they should use. Which of the following activities can the teacher undertake to promote the process of metacognition among the students during the class?
options: Ask the students to make a sketch of the model they will build and distribute the roles each one will play
Ask the students to show their models to another group and describe the layout of the constructed house
Ask the students to watch videos that guide the construction of the model and provide new techniques to make it
Ask the students to list the steps they followed to make the model and highlight the ones they found most difficult and justify why

question: A teacher, during the unit on Chemical Bonding, asks their students to step-by-step develop the following activity: Write the full electronic configurations of the atoms in the water and ammonia molecules. Draw the Lewis structures corresponding to each molecule. Build models of both molecules with clay and matchsticks according to their molecular geometry. Establish similarities and differences between the geometries of the two molecules. Which of the following learning outcomes could be evaluated with the described activity?
options: Predict the bonds of a molecule based on the electronic properties of its atoms
Represent the distribution of electrons within molecules using Lewis structures
Analyze the distribution of the atoms present in molecules according to the valence shell electron pair repulsion (VSEPR) theory
Identify the bonding and antibonding electrons in a molecule based on the electronic configurations of the atoms that compose it

question: A high school teacher is planning a class with the objective of "Describing the kinematics of uniform circular motions." To this end, she will explain that if the angular velocity (ω) of a particle remains constant, the tangential speed (vt) increases as the radius of its path (R) grows, using the equation vt = R·ω. Considering that some students might have difficulties understanding this explanation, the teacher decides to add an alternative formulation of this content to her plan. Given the above, which of the following options would be the most appropriate way to represent the physical phenomenon being described?
options: Apply the equation to solve a contextualized exercise, such as determining the tangential speed of a bicycle wheel
Show a graph of radius versus tangential speed in which the directly proportional relationship between the variables is observed
Demonstrate that when spinning a mass tied to a string, its rotation frequency changes if the length available for the rotation radius is modified
Compare the tangential speed of different points of a truncated cone-shaped glass, when rotated resting on one of its sides on a horizontal surface

question: Read the following activity: In the 'Logical-Mathematical Relationships and Quantification' unit, a preschool educator is working on pattern content. To do this, she will provide the children with material that includes two variable attributes and one stable attribute, so they can create their own patterns. Considering the described situation, which of the following resources is more appropriate to use for the children to create a sequence with the indicated characteristics?
options: Large and small balls of two colors: red and yellow
Large red stars and small green stars
Drink caps of the same size and two colors: white and green
Large and small red forks, large and small blue spoons

question: During the development of the Multimedia Installation unit, in a 2nd year of secondary education, a teacher will work with students on the objective: "Create a multimedia installation in a space of the school to concentrate the school community on climate and environmental issues." The students will work in groups to design the proposal, which will then be presented in a specific space of the educational establishment.  What prior knowledge is necessary for students to satisfactorily address the proposed objective?
options: Methodology for the development of visual projects
Techniques used in multimedia installation works
Expressive elements of the audio and video visual language
Environmental themes in multimedia installation works

question: Read the following situation:  Mauricio attends preschool at a school with a special needs department. He participates in different proposed activities with increasing autonomy each time. His challenges are related to language since, although he manages to establish functional locative acts, these are weak, conversational fluency is scarce, and the abundant use of jargon hinders the understanding of his statements.  The teacher at the level is working, in the core of Verbal Language, on the learning objective: "We talk to our friends clearly and understandably." In order to facilitate communicative exchanges and promote the development of oral language in Mauricio, the teacher decides to implement a bimodal communication system in her strategy that associates the signed and spoken modalities, simultaneously using the oral-auditory form and the visual-gestural modality. She will provide the student with base language messages that will outline the order of the sentence that will determine the syntax of his productions, along with visual and gestural signs.  Why is the implementation of a bimodal communication system by the teacher suitable for addressing Mauricio's support needs?
options: Because it enables and stimulates the student's communication with his environment by using different avenues that increase verbal language
Because it allows the student to acquire the reading and writing process more easily, enhancing his academic projection
Because it enhances the student's self-esteem by facilitating his responses to scheduled evaluative instances efficiently and clearly
Because it provides the student with better tools to express his preferences and interests in play and interaction with his peers

question: A professor who is working on the characteristics of contemporary narrative wants to check the learning of narrative techniques that affect the time of the story. To do this, he proposes his students read the following narrative fragment: As for me, I will be shot as a torturer and murderer. The court has proceeded with rectitude; from the beginning, I have declared myself guilty. Tomorrow, when the prison clock strikes nine, I will have entered death. (Deutsches requiem, Borges, J.L.). Regarding the treatment of time in the previous fragment, the teacher asks his students what feature they visualize and they mostly respond with an analepsis. What pedagogical action by the teacher would allow the students to get out of this confusion?
options: Provide examples with short stories of the differences between flashback and racconto
Practice recognizing cataphoric textual relationships in texts
Provide examples with contemporary narrative fragments of the characteristics of historical referential time
Reinforce through a presentation the characteristics that distinguish anticipation narration

question: A 7th grade teacher has set the following objective for their class: “Explain the behavior of gases in relation to Boyle's law.” What prior knowledge must students have mastered in order to understand this content?
options: The properties of matter
The kinetic energy of gases
