The corpuscular theory of matter
The kinetic-molecular theory of gases

question: For pre-kindergarten children, an educator invites them to the patio to conduct a learning experience about states of matter. This involves leaving a candle in the sun to see what happens. Previously, the children make predictions and draw them in a journal. In the described experience, what change of state of matter can the children of this level observe?
options: Fusion
Vaporization
Solidification
Condensation

question: To address the learning objective "Relate the temperature difference to the transfer of thermal energy between different substances," a teacher has his students perform the following activities using two substances. 1. Calculate the heat absorbed by a substance. 2. Measure and compare the temperature of the two substances. 3. Determine the direction of heat flow between the two substances. 4. Calculate the heat capacity of the substances. Which of the following options presents a progressive sequence, starting from everyday concepts to more abstract ones?
A group of students conducts surveys and interviews for the first time with a certain number of users to understand their needs and desires. What stage of the project does this action correspond to?
options: Understand the user
Characterize the user
Confirm objectives
Evaluate the project

question: A teacher, in a 3rd Year Middle School class, will evaluate, through a written test, the following objective: "Understand the role that various characters play in the conflict of the story, based on the reading of a tale." Which of the following indicators accounts for the achievement of the evaluated learning?
options: They identify the types of relationships between the characters and their impact on the story
They evaluate the reactions of each character throughout the tale and how these trigger the outcome
They psychologically characterize the characters of the tale and how these characteristics influence the development of the story
They classify each character according to their degree of importance in the story, indicating how they determine the development of events

question: What skills are mainly developed in children through structured play?
options: The symbolization of real-life situations
Empathy and conflict resolution
Socializing elements of agreements and turns
Learning of practices and roles established in social settings

question: The first-year secondary students have made their graphic design proposals for apps for various content in hand-drawn paper models, like sketches. They are going to transfer these designs to a digital medium that allows them to work with fonts, paragraphs of text, adjust sizes and positions of images, colors in letters, backgrounds and shapes, insert downloaded images and visual effects with animations.  What ICT tool allows students to perform the task?
options: A presentation software that allows displaying text and images on different slides
A word processing software in which text and images can be organized on different pages
An online image editing platform that allows the insertion of images and texts and is freely accessible
An online design platform that allows developing and editing images and texts based on different templates

question: Read the following situation:  During the collaborative work of a classroom team in 2nd grade, it is established that the learning of reading and writing will be worked on using various teaching methods to respond to the challenges that the students have presented in the decoding process. To this end, they agree that the special education teacher will look for strategies based on the analytical method.  According to the previous situation, which of the following strategies could the special education teacher use to address the challenges of the students?
options: Identify, recognize, and name letters progressively, initially focusing on vowels before moving to consonants
Learn the sounds of letters and syllables, progressively diversifying the combinations of sounds that can be made by combining letters
Recognize and name words to then decompose them, first into their direct syllables, second into complex ones, and later into their letters
Use syllables as basic units of pronunciation and comprehension, starting with direct syllables and increasing the difficulty to complex syllables

question: An educator decides to assign to the children tasks related to the care of the plants in the room. She asks them to comment on what they would like to do. The children say: “I like to draw; I will make a picture of each plant,” “I love taking care of others, at home I take care of my puppy... I will water them!” Which of the following aspects of identity development do the children demonstrate in their comments?
options: Self-efficacy
Self-concept
Self-regulation
Self-esteem

question: A teacher takes their students to a museum to a contemporary landscape exhibition with the goal of appreciating the aesthetic value of the exhibited works. During the visit, the teacher provides them with a written guide to mediate individual aesthetic appreciation; they must choose a work that catches their attention and then answer some questions. What questions should this guide contain to enable the achievement of the goal?
options: What is the theme of the work and within which movement is it framed? What is your appreciation of it and why?
Who is the author of the work and what are their most important biographical milestones? Why did you choose the work? How does it relate to your biography?
In which movement is the work framed? Does it use the technical procedures specific to that movement?
What are the visual characteristics that stand out in the work you chose? How do those characteristics relate to the expressive purpose of the work?

question: During the reading of the text Essay Concerning Human Understanding by John Locke, the teacher proposes to delve into the notion of sensitive qualities of the theory of knowledge to facilitate its understanding and strengthen this knowledge. Which of the following strategies would be appropriate to make a connection with other concepts previously seen in the subject and allow the student to understand this content?
options: Invite the student to review sensory memory as part of the cognitive processes and relate this to Locke's postulates, comparing the basic notions of both proposals
Suggest the student to compare Locke's notion with Descartes' adventitious ideas using a concept map
Instruct the student to review the notions of sensation and perception from cognitive Psychology and compare them with Locke's notion, establishing similarities, differences, and questions
Point out to the student to establish a comparison between Aristotle's notion of essence about knowledge construction and Locke's notion based on the elemental distinctions of each postulate

question: Read the following situation:  The classroom team of a 3rd grade in the subject of Language and Communication carries out an activity to strengthen the overall understanding of a text in the students, especially in those who present Learning Difficulties in reading. The activity consists of selecting a narrative text and, before reading it, sharing in work groups the answers to the following questions:  - Why did I choose this text?  - What is my purpose for reading this text?  - What do I look for when I want to know what a text like the one I chose will be about?  - Based on the previous question, what will the selected text be about?  What option justifies the strategy implemented by the special education teacher before the students read the chosen text?
options: Gives meaning to the reading, using the prior knowledge and experiences that the students possess
Allows students to read with a specific purpose and develop conjectures about what they will read
Facilitates students' identification of the structural elements of the text and gives meaning to the reading
Enables students to make conjectures based on what they are reading and allows the development of inferences

question: A 1st Year secondary teacher, in the unit of Matter and energy in the ecosystem, will formatively evaluate the achievement of the following learning objective: "Understand the relationship between the processes of photosynthesis and cellular respiration". Which of the following activities allows verifying the achievement of this learning?
options: Complete a comparison chart with the main differences between the processes of photosynthesis and cellular respiration
Write an explanation that accounts for the importance of the processes of photosynthesis and cellular respiration in the balance of the ecosystem
Make a poster with the chemical equations of photosynthesis and cellular respiration, in which the interdependence of both processes is determined
Prepare a diagram of the cellular structures where photosynthesis and cellular respiration occur, specifying the compounds involved in each process

question: Which of the following options explains language acquisition from the behaviorist perspective?
options: The child is born with an internal construct for speech, a specifically human innate scheme that is genetically inherited, allowing them to learn and assimilate communicative and linguistic structures
The child has implicit knowledge of their own language that allows them to understand and produce sentences according to the form of their linguistic code
The child accesses language through imitation, the need to satisfy their needs, and reinforcement provided by their parents and the linguistic model of their environment
The child has language development linked with their cognitive development, so they will only learn to speak when they cognitively reach a certain level of that development

question: Read the following situation:  A 1st grade student in a school with a Special Educational Needs Program attends named Sebastian, who communicates in Sign Language. Regarding writing development, the student has faced challenges, as he tends to confuse letters and does not recognize all the consonants that have been worked on. To support the student in developing writing skills, the head teacher, together with the special education teacher, have decided to write the names of different elements present in the classroom, incorporating the written name and the fingerspelling alphabet. Additionally, when new vocabulary appears in class, the special education teacher writes it on the board and the sign language interpreter presents the sign, along with manually spelling it out.  According to the situation presented, which of the following options justifies the strategy implemented by the teacher?
options: Favors a phonological-visual connection
Increases students' vocabulary
Explicitly addresses morphological aspects of words
Contributes to the fluent use of Sign Language in the classroom

question: Consider the following activity: The class is divided into groups of three students. Each group must construct a cone and a cylinder, both with the same radius and height. Then, they must fill them with rice and compare the amounts used in both bodies. Finally, a plenary session is held in which each group presents their results and conclusions are drawn. What is the main objective of the described activity?
options: Construct bodies with the same base and height to compare and estimate their volumes
Identify the relationship between the volumes of cones and cylinders with the same radius and height
Identify the relationship between the total areas of cones and cylinders with the same radius and height
Formulate conjectures based on learning experiences developed using concrete materials

question: A teacher of 8th Basic has worked with their students on different representations and applications of linear and affine functions, and at the end of this unit, they will carry out a summative evaluation of these learnings. In the evaluation, the teacher will present the following problem: In a bookstore, Marta bought 4 erasers for $3160, and each eraser had the same price. If one of the evaluation indicators of this instrument addresses the relationship between direct proportionality and the linear function, which of the following sequences of exercises would provide more comprehensive evidence regarding the achievement of this indicator?
options: 1) Determine the expression representing the amount of money to pay when n amount of erasers are bought. 2) What type of proportionality relates the variables price and quantity of erasers?

1) Create a table of values with the amount of money to pay when buying 2, 3, 4, 5, and 6 erasers, respectively, with these variables being directly proportional. 2) What type of function, linear or affine, relates the variables of the previous table?

1) Determine the algebraic expression representing the function that relates the number of erasers bought with the amount of money paid for them. 2) What element of the previous algebraic function corresponds to the constant of proportionality that relates both variables?

1) Graphically represent the function that relates the number of erasers bought with the amount of money paid for them. 2) What is the constant of proportionality that relates the variables price and quantity of erasers?

question: Language is not innate, it is learned and depends on the relationships that the individual establishes with people and, in general, with the surrounding culture. Therefore, inheritance is not determinant. It develops in stages, from birth to school age. To which author does this conception of language development correspond?
options: H. Maturana
D. Ausubel
L. Vygotsky
J. Piaget

question: In a handball class, two students fiercely contest the ball; one pushes the other to keep the ball, and the other responds with insults. The teacher calls both students to clarify what happened, and after hearing them, applies the established rule: a verbal warning to both. What conflict resolution method did the teacher apply?
options: Mediation
Negotiation
Arbitration
Consensual agreement

question: A teacher, when introducing the concepts of morals and ethics, alludes to the fact that what is good for some is not necessarily good for others. In response to this, a student comments: "These are subjective matters and ultimately everything is subjective, which is why some things are correct for some and not for others." What difficulty can be recognized in the student's comment?
options: Understanding the notion of subjectivity as if it were moral relativism
Deducing relativism of moral judgments based on multiple subjectivisms
Seeing subjectivity as a precursor to the development of a particular moral
Inferring that the relativism of a moral conception is a consequence of subjectivity

question: A middle school teacher has already taught their students about direct proportionality and now needs to start teaching inverse proportionality. Considering the stage of the learning process the students are in, which of the following common mistakes related to this content should the teacher consider in order to anticipate and address it adequately?
options: Errors in multiplying fractions and decimals
Confusion in analyzing the behavior of the variables involved
Confusion in distinguishing between relevant and irrelevant information in a problem related to direct and inverse proportions
Errors in establishing the relationship between direct and inverse proportionality and their various representations (graphical, symbolic, value table)

question: Which of the options presents an expected manifestation of cognitive development in boys and girls who are beginning the pre-operational stage?
options: Use of symbolic thought
Drawing abstract conclusions
Application of deductive reasoning
Interaction with the environment through experimental games

question: The teacher of the Natural Sciences subject for a 3rd-grade class in a school for deaf children asks the students to work on the following objective: "Describe the components of the Solar System, considering elements such as size, location, appearance, and relative distance to Earth." Considering the above, which of the following strategies is the most appropriate to address the stated goal?
options: Complete a guide on the components of the Solar System with images that represent their most important characteristics with the name written to subsequently compare it with the subject text

Create a model of the Solar System in which its components are incorporated and specify in writing the characteristics of each one in order to later discuss the topic in the class establishing similarities and differences between the elements

Create posters that show the components of the Solar System drawn by the students, which have their respective characteristics written next to them to later conduct a presentation in front of the class comparing the different poster models used

Watch informative videos about the components of the Solar System and their characteristics which include interpretation in LSCh to later discuss them in class and hold group presentations where the students comment on the aspects they found most interesting

question: In a 6th grade class, at the beginning of the "Engraving" unit, the teacher proposes in the first class the following activity to the students: imagine your own engravings and then draw them in your sketchbook. Then, he asks them to bring materials such as glue, a base, recycled fabrics, and different natural and artificial textures for the next class. In the second class, through group work, the students should build a sketch with the requested materials, in order to bring their ideas to life. To which stage of the flexible model of the creative process does the second class correspond?
options: Experiment
Plan
Present and share
Imagine and generate ideas

question: Which of the following learning experiences develops semantic awareness and why?
options: Creating sentences and temporal sequences because they allow for the organization of words
Performing verbal categorizations and analogies because they allow for giving meaning to words
Exercising rhymes and syllabic segmentation because they allow for identifying the sounds of words
Engaging in verb tense games and character descriptions because they allow for organizing words

question: Read the following learning objective: "Explain light phenomena through experimentation and the use of models, considering the particle and wave models of light." Which of the following evaluation indicators would directly assess the mentioned objective?
options: Describe diffraction using the wave model of light
Identify the light phenomena associated with the particle model of light
Explain the importance of the Doppler effect by identifying its applications, based on the wave model
Identify everyday situations where different light phenomena associated with the particle model can be observed

question:
In an 8th Grade class, a teacher will introduce students to the application of different dynamics in the score they are performing.
Which of the following activities should the students have completed beforehand to address the proposed content?
options: Identification of tempo symbols
Reading of phrasing with expression ties
Execution of ritardando, accelerando, and rubato
Sonic exploration of the instrument's dynamics

question: A teacher has decided to evaluate whether his students apply the knowledge about nutrition they have learned to their personal reality. What activities are congruent with this objective?
options: In groups, they create a standard diet for teenagers
Individually, they create a diet based on their weight, height, physical activity, and gender
Individually, they make a list of the most nutritious foods they consume
In groups, they analyze the results of a survey conducted in their school about eating habits

question: An 8th grade student, when calculating the hypotenuse c of a right triangle with legs 5 and 7, writes: c=√(5²+7²), c=5+7, c=12. Which of the following activities allows the student to identify and correct the error made?
options: Construct with ruler and compass the triangle with sides 5, 7, and 12
Evaluate the expression a²+b² for different values of a and b, compare with (a+b)² and then apply the root
Clearly identify legs and hypotenuse in the Pythagorean relation a²+b²=c² through various examples
Using Pythagorean triples, show the correct order of operations and use a calculator to verify the results

question: A teacher is starting the unit on chemical bonding and wants to show students that knowledge in chemistry is a progressive, dynamic construction full of controversies. To achieve this, which of the following activities would be relevant?
options: Debate about atomic models
Request a research report on VSEPR theory
Debate about the demonstration of gas laws
Request a bibliographic research on the history of the concept of bonding

question: A teacher in 2nd Grade, in the unit The Bipolar World, will address the following class objective: "Characterize the political-ideological projects of the United States and the Soviet Union during the Cold War," in coherence with the curricular emphasis of Social Sciences: political and economic perspective.  Considering the context presented, which of the following activities for the students to perform is consistent with the class objective and the proposed curricular emphasis?
options: Research online about the implementation of the economic models of the United States and the Soviet Union and their effects in the political realm, considering their respective spheres of influence during the Cold War, and then write an essay about it

Create a graphic organizer about the most notable historical events in the political and economic fields of the ideological projects of the United States and the Soviet Union, and then describe in a paragraph the evolution of each during the Cold War

Read secondary sources related to the models of the United States and the Soviet Union during the Cold War, then write conclusions about their central political elements and the bases of their economic organization, identifying advantages and disadvantages of each model

Create a comparative table to distinguish similarities and differences between the American and Soviet projects during the Cold War, using as criteria of analysis the military and economic alliances established with other countries and their spheres of influence

question: A 7th-grade teacher is planning a class that will address the objective "Reduce like terms in algebraic expressions". What prior learning should students necessarily have achieved in order to tackle the objective of the class?
options: 1) Solve operations with integers and with positive rational numbers. 2) Identify the components of a term and of an algebraic expression
1) Solve operations with integers and with positive rational numbers. 2) Translate expressions from natural language to algebraic language, and vice versa
1) Identify the components of a term and of an algebraic expression. 2) Translate expressions from natural language to algebraic language, and vice versa
1) Solve operations with integers and with positive rational numbers. 2) Represent simple algebraic expressions concretely, pictorially, and symbolically

question: Read the following situation: In a 7th grade class, in the Writing section, a teacher is working with his students on the following goal: "Understand different mechanisms of textual cohesion." In order to obtain information about the worked objective, an evaluation containing the following exercise is applied: Read the following fragment and replace the repeated term. Finally, you decided and adopted a cute puppy. But, did you take into account all the care you must have so that the adaptation of the puppy to its new environment is as little traumatic as possible? One of the most complicated issues to solve usually is its care during the night. If the puppy is left alone in the place you have assigned it in your house, as many experts usually advise, it will surely cry incessantly with all its might. Which of the following assessment indicators corresponds to the exercise of the evaluation?
options: They distinguish the communicative adequacy presented by the text
They recognize the coherence of a text
They apply synonymy as a coreference resource
They distinguish semantic relations between connectors

question: Read the following situation:  The classroom team of a 2nd grade, in the subject of Language and Communication, will work with their students on the purpose of instructional texts, specifically cooking recipes. Four classes are planned, the first of which presents the following strategy at the beginning of the lesson:  - Activation of prior knowledge regarding the aforementioned text, through questions.    - Shared reading of a recipe, supported by images.    - Review of a video where a person, guided by a recipe, is cooking.  Based on the principles of Universal Design for Learning, what option justifies the strategy used at the beginning of the described lesson?
options: Students differ in the way they perceive and understand the information presented to them, so various sensory modalities, learning styles, interests, and preferences should be considered when presenting the information

Students require that learning objectives be flexible and achievable, so various adjustments should be implemented to facilitate access, retention, and participation of students in the school curriculum
Students engage in a learning situation with an appropriate level of challenge, answering questions that guide them in interactions, and providing alternative strategies to motivate and engage them

Students present a diversity of styles, capabilities, and preferences to operate in a learning environment, to execute and express what they know or have learned, so different ways of expressing what has been learned should be considered

question: A teacher is planning classes in which the following objective will be addressed: "Understand and apply characteristics and properties associated with the circle." Since the students are at an initial stage of learning this objective, the teacher decides to start with a class in which they intuitively estimate the value of π. Which of the following activities to be carried out by the students allows them to address the objective, considering the described pedagogical purpose?
options: They look for information about the number π and its relation to circles and circumferences
They solve introductory problems involving the calculation of the perimeter and area of a circle
They calculate the perimeter and area of circles of different radii, using approximate values of the number π
They measure the perimeter of circles of different radii and determine the relationship between the perimeter and corresponding radius

question: At the end of the unit on Installation and Contemporary Art, a teacher will evaluate her 8th grade students regarding the achievement of the following objective: "To collaboratively create an installation in the classroom based on the reflection of the relationship between people and nature."  Which of the following student works demonstrates the achievement of the objective?
options: The students intervene with paint and recycled materials significant everyday objects brought from their homes and mount them in different places in the classroom
The students place a mirror on the ceiling of one of the corners of the classroom, reflecting the previously intervened floor with grass, leaves, and green area paintings made by the group
The students create costumes from recycled clothing inspired by climate change, which are modeled by them while explaining their creation process
The students make a video with images of the digitally intervened classroom with different colors and figures alluding to nature, changing the atmosphere of the intervened space

question: Read the following situation:   Karina is a 4th-year high school student who has been diagnosed with high myopia since 5th grade, with vision 20/400 on the Snellen scale. She relates adequately with her peers, maintaining affinity bonds. She operates independently and asks for help when needed.  According to the context and from a multidimensional perspective, what is the main implication of Karina's diagnosis for the provision of specialized support?
options: She needs to strengthen her use of the braille system to be able to carry out her school activities
She needs to start learning the Hoover Techniques for her independent mobility
She needs the incorporation of large print, a stand, and double-spaced lines for her school activities
She needs to start learning assistive technology tools to diversify her means of communication

question: After aesthetically appreciating some works of painting from the geometric abstraction movement, reviewing their historical context, and conducting a visual analysis, a teacher gives the following instruction to their students: "Create several sketches, as an idea, in which you propose an abstract composition, based on the choice of a significant object, relating your proposal to one of the reviewed artists." To which stage of the flexible model of the creative process does the teacher's instruction correspond?
options: Pose a creative challenge
Reflect and evaluate the process
Plan an activity
Present a first proposal

question:
A 5th grade teacher is working with his students on exercises involving the addition of fractions with different denominators and observes that several students make the following error:

2/3 + 1/2 = (2+1) /( 3+2) = 4/5 = 3/7

Considering the above situation, which of the following strategies would allow the students to understand and correctly apply a symbolic method of adding fractions with different denominators?
options: Locate both numbers on the number line to determine their distance from zero and, from the sum of these distances, obtain the position of the result on the line. Then, identify the corresponding fraction and solve the exercise

Relate the operation to a concrete example to demonstrate the error. Then, work on its symbolic development by amplifying each of the addends in the exercise to link it to the concept of equivalent fractions and solve it with a common denominator

Explain again the method of adding fractions with different denominators, using examples with other values, so that students understand the procedure through practice. Then, ask them to resume the exercise

Propose a contextualized problem where the addends present in the exercise are used, so that students understand addition from the additive composition in concrete situations. Then, ask them to express the result numerically

question: Read the following case:  Daniela is a five-year-old girl who presents profound and bilateral hearing loss, and has been using hearing aids since her hearing loss was detected at about two years old. She communicates using isolated words and some phrases with poor grammatical usage and has a limited vocabulary.  The specialist teacher begins support with an auditory evaluation which consists of a list of consonant phonemes in direct syllables and consonant phonemes between vowels (only via auditory means) that Daniela must reproduce, as, together with her family, they have decided to enhance her residual hearing.  Monthly, the specialist teacher applies this same evaluation and realizes the progress of the student regarding the work done to enhance her auditory skills.  According to what has been described, what auditory skill is the specialist teacher working on with Daniela?
options: Attention
Memory
Perception
Discrimination

question: Which of the following options corresponds to a common element in the learning theories of Piaget and Vygotsky?
options: The systemic organization of children's thinking
Language as an essential element for cognitive development
The importance of cultural tools in development
Dynamic positioning as a necessary requirement for learning activity

question: An 8th grade teacher is planning a lesson on the structural characteristics of secretory cells and proposes that students relate the significant development of the intracellular membrane system to its function.  Which of the following learning activities will allow students to address the planned objective?
options: Project microphotographs of secretory cells and ask them to identify the function of each of the present organelles
Ask them to draw on a microphotograph of a secretory cell the pathway of proteins through the exocytic route and then explain it
Present a model of a secretory cell and another of a muscle cell, and ask them to compare the functions of the structures that differentiate them
Present a model of a secretory cell and another of a muscle cell, and ask them to determine the function of both cells based on their structures

question: A 4th-year high school teacher has explained Kantian critique of the faculties of knowledge and then asks his students to answer some related questions in their notebooks. Upon review, he discovers that a majority of the class confused Kant's answer about the object of knowledge with the empiricists' point of view. What activity would be appropriate for students to understand and differentiate Kant's thought?
options: Students summarize the main characteristics of empiricist and Kantian thought, noting them in their notebooks
Students conduct research on the thought of Kant and Locke, and then present their main contributions
Students do a comparative analysis of the ideas of empiricists, rationalists, and Kant's ideas, through a conceptual map
Students create a dictionary of Kantian, empiricist, and rationalist terms

question: Within the framework of an evaluation regarding Plato's main ideas, a Philosophy teacher aims to assess the following objective: "Understand the theory of participation of being in Plato". Which evaluation indicator is consistent with the aforementioned objective?
options: Distinguish between the concepts of episteme and doxa
Organize Platonic ideas according to degrees of perfection
Identify the three types of soul in the myth of the chariot
Differentiate the notions of the sensible world and the intelligible world

question: A technology teacher designs the instructions for a research project guide that will be carried out by their first-year secondary students as part of the unit "Evolution and impact of a solution." The project will be based on a service chosen by each group on which they will investigate the evolution it has undergone in recent decades, including a reflection on the factors involved and the impacts of this evolution. To address the curricular emphasis of the subject of promoting critical thinking, which of the following guiding questions should be present in the work guide?
options: What social context variables made the evolution of this production process possible? Identify the effect of each on the evolution of this technological solution
Globally, do you think the evolution of this production process has allowed for a better solution to the need it satisfies? Justify your answer with examples from everyday life

Considering what was investigated in this project, do positive or negative effects predominate in the evolution of this service? Justify your answers by referring to social, environmental, and economic areas
What actions would be advisable at a personal and social level to mitigate the negative impact of this evolution? Propose two actions for each area and identify their effects

question: Read the following situation:  Some students in 4th grade, including those with reading disabilities, only manage to answer comprehension questions associated with first-order skills. To enhance their curricular progress and enable them to develop understanding of higher-order comprehension skills, the classroom team works with strategies like the following at the beginning of classes:  A situation projected on the board (accompanied by images)  Guido placed flour in the bowl and added baking powder. In another container, he mixed sugar with margarine and eggs.  Once he mixed the contents of both containers, he added milk and kept beating.  Question for the students  What is Guido doing? What information from the text allows you to point out?  What did you do to arrive at the answer?    Why does the classroom team use this work strategy with the students?
options: So that they recognize information, compare key elements, and classify according to their level of importance
So that they identify data, relate them to each other, link them with their prior knowledge, and draw conclusions
So that they distinguish main facts, organize them in a logical sequence, relate them to the contents, and establish conjectures
So that they locate explicit information, contrast it with other similar texts, link it with the character(s), and reflect on the overall meaning of the text

question: A third-grade teacher, in the unit about children's rights, selected the following excerpt from the story *The Girl Who Had No Name*, by Saúl Schkolnik, to start working with his students on the right to identity:  Once upon a time, there was a girl who had no name at all. It's not that she had a really strange name, or one that was very difficult to pronounce, or one of those very, very old names, or perhaps too foreign... No! She simply had no name at all. The thing is, when one's name is Maria and someone calls: —Maria! One makes a Maria face and answers: —Who is calling me? But, if one has no name at all, then one will never be able to make the face of someone... And the features will kind of blur until one ends up like having no face.  Which of the following questions could the teacher ask to directly connect what was read with the right that will be studied in class?
options: What information about the girl in the story caught your attention the most and why?
Which part of the story do you think relates to children's rights?
What do you think the consequences of not having a name would be for the girl in the story?
How might the story continue to have a positive ending for the girl in the story?

question: Considering a student in elementary education from a special school who presents a diagnosis of spastic tetraparesis and moderate cognitive disability, which of the following actions facilitates their participation and communication in the classroom?
options: Label all the material and shelves in the classroom
Install a panel of acrylic and pictograms with various vocabulary on the work tables
Implement differentiated corners to develop reading, music, among other activities
Arrange the tables and chairs in semicircles so that everyone can interact

question: Read the following situation:  In 1st Grade of a special needs school, there is Mateo, a student with Autism Spectrum Disorder who has adequate adaptation and functional oral language with echolalic episodes. His reading and writing level allows him to recognize letters and love words with them, and also read and understand words related to his immediate environment. In his communication, he shows preferences for topics and challenges with gender agreement of articles. Mateo's teacher aims to address the following learning objective with the class: Understand texts, applying reading comprehension strategies, for example: visualize what the text describes. Which of the following strategies meets Mateo's support needs?
options: Use words that are meaningful to him, which he executes through phonological awareness, in order to ensure a learning triad: reading, writing, and comprehension

Use short and interesting texts, discussed with the class, so that the student has the opportunity to hear specific language considering that vocabulary is the predictor and conductor of comprehension

Use interest texts with illustrations in which the student highlights relevant information with the help of graphical representations, discarding irrelevant information from the text so as to enhance comprehension

Use manipulable material that includes words and images so that the student can connect with the drawing or concept that they represent, proceeding to form sentences, from the simplest to the most complex, using signs with words

question: An 8th-grade teacher is working on the unit 'The Rise of National Monarchies,' aiming to promote the skill of reading and analyzing sources, specifically the reliability of sources, considering the curricular emphasis on historical thinking formation. To this end, she proposes reading the following source:  No one can deny that the power [...] of the monarchs grew as economic resources and the size of armies increased. Moreover, the law not only protected the subjects but also the legal powers of the princes. Lawyers were one of the main elements of the reform of the monarchies. And this was particularly true in countries where the professionalism of Law [...] had its roots in the laws of ancient Rome [...]  (The Europe of the Reformation. 1517 - 1559, Elton Geoffrey (1999).)  Which of the following activities will directly promote the analysis of the reliability of the source by the students?
options: Determine the type of source, primary or secondary, based on its year of publication
Establish who Elton Geoffrey is and what his intention was when writing the source
Identify the author's historical perspective on national monarchies
Describe the usefulness of the text about the power of the monarch to understand the rise of national monarchies

question: Read the following situation:  Belinda is a student with language challenges who attends the preschool at a special language school. Her mother personally takes her to the establishment every morning because she is worried that every day her daughter cries and presents excessive muscle tension when she leaves her there. This tension and irritability in the student are projected into the school context, where she appears withdrawn and sometimes confused or distressed when relating to other students, whom she rejects even though they insist on playing with her. All these characteristics also affect her development in the educational field, where she generally presents low performance.  Based on the described situation, in which of the following areas does the student require school support to progress in her learning?
options: Family: lack of parental strategies
Social: little interest in socializing with peers
Cognitive: impairment in cognitive functions
Emotional: challenges in her emotional regulation

question: A teacher observed that their students make various errors when calculating the area of rectangles. In order to obtain information that allows them to provide appropriate feedback to their students, they decide to conduct an evaluation. Which of the following evaluative activities allows the teacher to obtain the information they need?
options: Hand out different rectangles with given areas and ask the students to represent them pictorially and symbolically
Ask the students to describe the procedure used to determine the area of given rectangles
Gather the students in pairs and ask them to determine the best way to find the area of given rectangles
Ask the students to represent the areas of different rectangles with grids and discuss how they can express this count

question: A kindergarten teacher requires conducting a diagnostic assessment to explore the lexical skills of children, specifically the ability to define words and recognize which category they belong to.  What educational situation is appropriate to gather evidence regarding these skills?
options: Invite the children to play guessing games. Essential characteristics of different words will be mentioned, without using visual support. Use a checklist to record performance

Present the children with a series of objects and baskets representing different categories, and invite them to select one of the objects, mention its name, and place it in a category. Assess performance using a rubric

Invite the children to observe a double-entry table containing some categories, and provide a series of cards that they must associate with the corresponding category. Use anecdotal records to document performance

Present the children with sheets containing images of different categories, and ask them to select one and describe it without mentioning its name. Peers should describe the word and mention its category. Evaluate performance using a rating scale

question: Read the following situation: Kevin is a student with an intellectual disability. He is in 4th grade in a school with an inclusion program. His support needs are mainly focused on sustained attention, as it lasts between 3 to 5 minutes. At the same time, he needs support regarding working memory and planning. This leads to different challenges, such as solving arithmetic problems. Considering Kevin's characteristics, the integration teacher suggests the following strategies to the classroom teacher: Before starting to solve an arithmetic problem, remind the student of the sequence of steps they must follow to complete the task and check them as the problem is executed; then, together, they should determine short goals based on the proposed steps; during the activity, guide the student to carry out a note-taking system to record the data needed to solve the arithmetic problems. Considering the previous context, which of the following options justifies the implementation of the suggested strategies?
options: These strategies will allow Kevin to start and finish the task, retrieving the necessary information, without losing the data needed to operate, planning its execution, and monitoring progress towards the goal

These strategies will allow the student to focus their attention for a longer period of time, as well as identify relevant data from non-relevant data in the given problems, avoiding delivering answers by trial and error

The proposed strategies will allow Kevin to regulate his impulsive behavior, thus facilitating the information gathering processes needed to solve arithmetic problems, leading him to successfully complete each task

The indicated strategies will support the student in defining possible cognitive and metacognitive strategies for developing each presented problem, establishing a step-by-step that organizes its execution and allows the task to be completed

question: A lower nursery educator plans to gather evidence for the following evaluation indicator of the core area of Corporality and Movement: Identifies some parts of their body by pointing to them when named. To collect such evidence, the educator will use videos and descriptive records.  Which of the following strategies allows evaluating the achievement of the indicator?
options: At free play time in the room, the adult sits next to the mirror and when the child approaches, asks where the different parts of their face are
At variable activity time, the adult asks the child about the different senses and asks them to touch the part of their face associated with each one
At changing time, the adult sings different songs related to the parts of the face, touching them on their own body and observing if the child repeats it
At greeting time, the adult performs actions with different parts of their body, like clapping, moving feet and head; then asks the children to imitate these actions

question: Read the following situation: An educator organizes an activity called 'protagonist of the day', which consists of a child, each day, sharing with their classmates their preferences, interests, and hobbies through photos, memories, toys, and anecdotes. What pedagogical principle is present in the above situation?
options: Meaning
Relationship
Singularity
Well-being

question: The teacher wants to assign a reinforcement task to the 50% of the class that got the lowest scores on a test. Which of the following data allows selecting these students?
options: The mode
The mean
The median
The frequency

question: A kindergarten teacher has planned the following learning objective from the Artistic Languages core: "To plastically represent emotions, ideas, experiences, and interests, through lines, shapes, colors, and textures, with resources and supports in plane and volume." To achieve this, they will develop with their group a project called "Creating a pet", documenting the process carried out by the children through photographs.  Which of the following strategies allows the educator to gather evidence about the achievement of the proposed objective?
options: Present the children with different works of art related to animals and ask them to verbalize the emotions they generate. Then, ask them to choose one to represent
Present the children with three pet options for them to select one and represent it with recyclable materials. Then, ask them to present to their families the techniques they used to create it
Present the children with various materials and ask them to select some to create a representation of a pet. Then, invite them to present to their classmates why this pet is special
Present the children with a video that explains the steps to follow to create a representation of a pet. Then, invite them to organize an art fair where they show their creations to the community

question: The management team of a professional technical high school, along with the teachers, is working on the following processes: Analysis of requirements coming from the Institutional Educational Project. Analysis of requirements from the local productive environment. Analysis of employment dynamics and the socio-cultural characteristics of the students and the educational community. At what stage of curriculum management is this high school working?
options: Relevance of evaluation criteria
Units diagnosis
Resource feasibility
Programs contextualization

question: Consider the following situation: In Technological Education, students work with project methodology to set up the “Sustainable Fair”, open to the community, where each group must set up a booth to present the products or exhibition they have designed and produced, all within the framework of sustainable development. The work process has been evaluated through observations and the submission of two written reports. The teacher designs the final project evaluation, scheduled once the fair with the stands of each group has been set up. What is the most appropriate evaluative procedure for the final project evaluation, considering the nature of the learning involved in this experience?
options: A written test of open-ended questions that evaluates the understanding of the concept of sustainable development involved in the work of the various groups in this project
A final written report by group, gathering information from the different steps implemented in the project
A rubric that evaluates the different dimensions of each group's work at the fair, applied both by the teacher and the students themselves
An individual work form in which each student presents their experience about the group work developed during the project

question:
A preschool educator reads the story 'The Very Hungry Caterpillar' to her group of boys and girls, they discuss it, and then she suggests they make a presentation about healthy and unhealthy foods and their consequences. For this, she divides the class into two small groups: one will make a poster with healthy foods and the other, a poster with unhealthy foods. To conclude, they present together and discuss the experience.
What learning does this experience mainly promote in the children?
options: Making informative posters
Communicating their ideas and decisions to others
Respecting the participation and contributions of others
Identifying situations that allow for self-care

question: During the poetry unit in 8th grade, a teacher has students read the following fragment of a poem so that they can recognize the relationship between the formal aspects and the content of the poem.  Alamedas  The branch of the poplar rows above   the branch rows, we row   beneath the leaves, we fly   up the sap, mast ascended.   The leaves row through the torrent   of the avenue. Come, then, feel   how it dives among the skies   the green arms towards the west. (Rafael Rubio).  Which of the following questions would allow students to relate the predominant figure with the meaning of the poem?
options: What rhetorical figures are used in the poem?
How does the style of versification relate to the title of the poem?
Is there any relationship between the sounds and what happens in the poem?
What function does the meter serve in the distribution and understanding of the poem?

question: A teacher in a secondary class at the end of the Nervous System unit will summatively evaluate the following learning objective: "Explain how the nervous system coordinates the actions of the organism to adapt to environmental stimuli through signals transmitted by neurons throughout the body".  Which of the following indicators accounts for the achievement of the evaluated objective?
options: They analyze different diagrams of simple reflex acts and distinguish their components
They describe the changes observed in some nervous responses of different intensity
They describe the structural properties of neurons that allow for the production of different types of responses
They relate the role of the spinal cord, the brain, sensory elements, and effectors in the formulation of the nervous response

question: A 2nd year secondary teacher, after conducting a written production in which students had to comment on a novel, reviews the following text from one of them:  The novel presents a very complex argument related to the lack of trust between the characters. The novel is structured in three parts, thus the novel is complex to understand. Finally, the novel made me reflect on the lack of empathy in the world we live in, such as within the novel, the character Pedro, who does not help his mother when she needed him.  Which of the following observations, made by the teacher, provides feedback to the student to improve the cohesion of the text?
options: It is necessary that you use coreference to improve your text
To avoid repetition, use equivalent words for: the novel
Avoid lexical repetition: the novel is repeated several times in the paragraph
The text fails in its explicit relationships by incurring in lexical repetition

question: A kindergarten teacher invites the children in his group to engage in an experience to start the unit 'Our Body'. To begin the activity, they will go to the playground to search for treasure with the purpose of discovering what the new unit is about. Organized into small groups supervised by an adult, they will find clues to reach the treasure, and upon discovering it, they will return to the classroom to assemble the puzzle together with the pieces they found.  Which pedagogical principles of Early Childhood Education are primarily evident in the proposed learning experience?
options: Play and relationship
Uniqueness and unity
Meaning and activity
Empowerment and well-being

question: To achieve the objective 'Analyze the poems read to enhance their understanding', a teacher performs an activity where they select poems that present literary figures and ask the students to explain them in their own words. Which of the following evaluation indicators does the preceding activity address?
options: They relate the theme of the poems to other texts read
They recognize the connotative and denotative meaning of the verses read
They describe aspects that express a mood in the poems read
They link the grammatical aspects of the selected poems with their meaning

question: A preschool educator will work on the learning objective: “Interpret songs, by integrating various resources such as voice, body, musical instruments, and objects,” from the Artistic Languages core. What performance by the children would demonstrate the achievement of this learning objective?
options: Perform bodily movements following the speed of the music
Dance by making gestures and movements according to the intensity of the music
Mark the pulse together using different musical instruments
Sing different songs marking the rhythm with clapping and head movements

question: Read the following situation:  A second-grade student in a school with an inclusive education program, Carla, a deaf student who uses Sign Language, attends. The classroom team decides to implement some curricular accommodations because they have observed that this students' written productions are generally brief and underdeveloped. The work begins in Language and Communication: Carla informs the special education teacher in sign language about what she is going to write, proceeds to draft the text, and ends the process with a joint review with the professional to verify that the ideas stated at the beginning have been reflected in the writing.  According to the described situation, what is the basis for the accommodation implemented by the classroom team?
options: Strengthens the use of LSCh across different subjects
Makes explicit the grammatical rules of the second language being worked on
Facilitates a bridge between Sign Language and written language
Promotes the use of LSCh by identifying similarities with written language

question: A fifth-grade teacher is evaluating, through various tasks that include open questions, the following objective: "To critically evaluate the information present in non-literary texts, according to their purposes and characteristics." To do so, she presents her students with the following evaluation task:  Read the following text and then answer the questions.  Cigarettes in the Sand  Mr. Director:  I am 12 years old and, like every summer, I went to the beach with my family. While playing in the sand, I found many cigarettes thrown on the ground. Since I was a child, it has bothered me a lot that the bucket has more cigarettes than sand. So, I thought about writing to the newspapers to ask all the readers who smoke to please try not to smoke at the beach. And if they do, to please not throw the butts in the sand. That way, we kids can play in the sand with sand, and not with cigarettes.  Ignacia Tokman Herrera   www.biohechil.cl    What is the communicative purpose of the letter written by Ignacia Tokman? Why?  One of the students responds as follows: "The purpose of Ignacia is to be able to play with sand when she goes to the beach, because that is what she wants to do according to what she writes in the letter." The teacher tells him: "Yes!, you are right, according to what she writes, Ignacia wishes to be able to play at the beach with sand that does not have butts."  Which of the following questions complements the teacher's comment so that the student can better handle the evaluation objective?
options: But who does she really direct her letter to?
But what change does she want to generate with her letter?
But what does her annoyance explained in the letter consist of?
But why is the issue raised in her letter important for everyone?

question: A teacher in an 8th grade class is working on the following objective: "Reflect and evaluate the feasibility and social and environmental impact of a technological project." During the class, the teacher explains to the students the social impact of some technological solutions, but some students report not understanding the relationship between technological developments and the social field. In response, the teacher decides to use an example. Which of the following examples would allow the teacher to illustrate his explanation?
options: The invention of the telephone, which allowed a different way of communication and enabled the development of new technology in telecommunications
The creation of the Internet, which allowed the generation of new economic pathways that enable different forms of financial and business management
The invention of machines and steam engines, which allowed an increase in industrial production in less time, making it possible to reach a broader market
The invention of vaccines, which has allowed the control of disease outbreaks, improving people's life expectancy and establishing public policies in this area

question: According to J. Piaget, one of the characteristics of early speech is that children overgeneralize the meanings of words. Which of the following options is an example of the characteristic indicated?
options: A child says “ato” to the toy car his dad gave him. When shown another car, he says “no ato”
A child, upon looking at a photo of himself with his mother, repeats along with her: “affectionate, beautiful, proud” and then the child adds “loving”
A child says “tata”, pointing to the image of a gray-haired man in a magazine
A child says “papa” every time he is given his bottle

question: Read the following situation: During corner time in the preschool classroom, Francisca, aged 6, takes Legos of different sizes and colors. She builds a tower with five red blocks and another with three blue blocks. Then, she looks at the red tower and forms two towers, one with small red Legos and another with large red Legos. In the described situation, which of the logical-mathematical reasoning skills is demonstrated by Francisca's performance?
options: Seriation by height
Seriation by size
Classification by one criterion
Classification by two criteria

question: A 1st year high school teacher, in the unit of Inorganic Nomenclature, is conducting a class with the following objective: "To apply the systematic, Stock, and traditional nomenclatures to name metal oxides". When he is explaining how to name these compounds using the Stock nomenclature, he realizes that the students do not understand why they must include the valence of the metal in Roman numerals only in some cases. To solve this problem, the teacher decides to carry out an activity.  Which of the following activities addresses the difficulty that the teacher detects in his students?
options: The teacher writes on the board a series of metal oxides and asks the students to calculate the oxidation state of the metal in each case. Then, they reflect on the different oxidation state values that metals present in each chemical compound

The teacher writes on the board a series of exercises on metal oxides and asks the students to name each one of them, using the rules of Stock nomenclature, then, they answer the question: "Why do the different metal oxides mentioned have different names?"

The teacher writes on the board the formulas of dioxo and copper monoxide, and the students respond to the following reflection question: "How can the two compounds be distinguished in Stock nomenclature if the metal oxide consists of the same combination of chemical elements?"

The teacher writes on the board a series of metallic elements with their respective valences and asks the students to write in their notebooks the types of metal oxides that these metals can form, then, they respond to the question: "Why can some metals combine in different amounts with oxygen, forming different chemical compounds?"

question: In a 1st Year secondary class, students engage in activities related to the process of image formation in flat and curved mirrors. During the activity, a student raises their hand and comments: "Teacher, I still don’t understand the difference between real and virtual images, nor why they are called that." What explanation from the teacher would be the most appropriate to clarify the student’s confusion, responding in a comprehensible way and with conceptual precision to their question?
options: Look at one side and the other of a spoon. The image in which you see yourself the right way is virtual and the image in which you see yourself upside down is real
You should remember that real images are always inverted, from top to bottom, while virtual images are always upright
Virtual images seem to be formed behind the mirror, while real images are formed in front of it and can be projected onto a screen
Virtual images are formed in a virtual focal plane by the extension of the incident rays, while real images are produced by the effective projection of the reflected rays

question: In a 7th grade class, in the unit on the Middle Ages, a teacher sets as an objective: "Develop historical empathy by distinguishing the frontier relations between Christians, Muslims, and Jews in the Iberian Peninsula." To achieve this, she asks the students to construct a narrative that reflects the relations existing in that time period.  While monitoring the activity, she observes that several students do not recognize the existence of frontier relations between Muslims, Christians, and Jews. For example, she finds several works with content similar to the following: "My name is Muhamed, I am a young Muslim living in the Caliphate of Cordoba, my whole family follows the Muslim religion and we sell carpets in the market. We do not associate with Christians or Jews because they are infidels."  Considering the evidence collected, what new activity can the teacher propose to the students to address the proposed objective, focusing on the development of historical empathy?
options: Individually describe how they imagine the peoples who lived on the Iberian Peninsula lived and interacted, based on observing images
In groups, represent the different dynamics of coexistence that occurred within the Iberian Peninsula, based on the recording of testimonies from each of the peoples who inhabited it
Complete a chart about the religious beliefs of each of the peoples who inhabited the Iberian Peninsula, based on reading the guidebook, describing how these influenced their relations

Provide examples of the current relations established between Christians and Muslims based on reading news on the internet, pointing out similarities and differences with what happened on the Iberian Peninsula during the Middle Ages

question: Read the following situation:  Emilio is a 2nd grade student who presents mixed language disorders. Due to his difficulties with uncommon vocabulary and figurative language, he was assigned a tutor partner to support and clarify oral explanations that he does not understand. With his help, Emilio manages to complete activities and respond within the designated time, despite usually being the one who takes the longest to finish.  Considering the change in the student's performance, what was the barrier that limited Emilio's participation and learning?
options: Use a single way to deliver instructions to the student
Use a highly complex language in the presentation of tasks
Determine a specific time for completing the planned task
Provide the student with only one way to demonstrate what he has learned

question: Read the following situation: An educator invites the early years children to do an experiment. In a bottle, she pours water, oil, and glycerin. Then, she shakes the bottle and shows how the oil and glycerin rise, while the water stays at the bottom. What property of matter is the educator demonstrating in the described experience?
options: Density
Solubility
Buoyancy
Divisibility

question: Cristabal, a seven-year-old, participates in preschool and has myelomeningocele and low vision. The classroom team is working in the area of interaction learning and understanding of the environment, in the core exploration of the natural environment, with the following learning objective: "Recognize some representative elements of their natural environment, such as: animals." The team has to select a resource that facilitates Cristabal's access to the activity being carried out in the classroom.  Which of the following resources is most suitable to favor Cristabal's response to the proposed learning challenge?
options: Manual pointer to work on a touch screen
Keyboard grid to point to the images on a computer
Visual scanning to choose the images presented on a tablet
Head pointer to indicate the images presented on a communicator

question:
Read the following situation:
Patricia, an early years educator, invites five children from her class each week to lead small group work.
She tells the children that the leader will suggest a group game and encourage their peers to follow the rules of the proposed game.
According to Erikson's stages of socio-emotional development, which one is reflected in the previous learning experience?
options: Initiative vs. guilt
Trust vs. mistrust
Industry vs. inferiority
Autonomy vs. shame and doubt

question: Read the following situation: In the Language and Communication class, in a 4th grade, a teacher proposes the learning objective "Write informative articles to communicate information about a topic". For this, students choose a topic of their interest and search, read, and record information about it. The teacher gives each student a graphic organizer to synthesize the main ideas of the reviewed texts in it. At what stage of writing does the strategy described in the previous situation fit?
options: Editing
Revising
Planning
Drafting

question: Read the following situation: Rodrigo, a student with congenital blindness, whose degree of vision is seeing lumps of large and nearby objects, enters preschool in a school with an inclusion program, receiving formal education for the first time. His educators notice that the student has a low semantic development as expected for his age. Considering the previous context and Rodrigo's characteristics, what teaching diversification experience is the most appropriate for all students to achieve the expected semantic development for the level?
options: The children relate various stories by performing group dramatizations
The children play to describe and discover unknown words from the stories
The children explore concrete materials, miniatures, modeling and commenting on their characteristics
The children take walks to community places, exploring new objects through touch

question: A secondary teacher in a 3rd year is planning the classes in which she will address the following objective: "Solve problems of Euclidean geometry that involve metric relationships between chords, secants, and tangents of a circle." What of the following contents is necessary for students to master beforehand in order to address this objective?
options: Similarity of triangles
Congruence of triangles
Circular sector and segment
Area and perimeter of a circle

question: A preschool teacher working with older toddlers will implement different experiences during large group time to promote the following learning objective from the Artistic Languages area: “Imitate gestures, movements, and sounds from their meaningful environment, through various means.” Which of the following learning experiences allows addressing the stated objective?
options: Present children with a fable and invite them to create a mask of the character they liked the most
Present children with different animal onomatopoeic sounds and ask them to identify them by naming them
Invite children to play "Simon Says" and indicate, through mime, different objects they must find and bring
Invite children to sing and dance songs with physical responses, such as "To the Sound of the Crocodile" and "The Monster of the Lagoon."

question: A teacher of 8th grade, given the importance of addressing topics related to climate change in the Technology class, proposes an activity for her students to work on the theme "Production processes in Chile and clean production." Which of the following activities promotes the critical analysis of this theme more, in accordance with the curricular emphasis of the subject?
options: The students, divided into groups, visit local companies to learn more about the processes involved in production. Then, they prepare a report with the information obtained from the visits and their opinion about the production processes observed

The students, organized in groups, investigate and create a list of production processes in different economic sectors of the country (agricultural, mining extraction, manufacturing industry, among others), from which they derive causes that explain climate change

The students watch a video describing how Chilean legislation has been updated so that production systems, whether goods or services, move towards clean production processes, achieving the goal of carbon neutrality by the year 2050

The students seek information about some production processes of Chilean companies and compare them with similar companies that have signed clean production agreements. Then, they develop a proposal for the former on how to increase efficiency and reduce environmental risks

question: Through an investigative activity, a teacher wants to address the different techniques for separating mixtures. To this end, she shows her students a jar with water, stones, oil, and salt. The teacher has several jars prepared with the same substances. Which of the following activities would allow students to learn about mixture separation techniques while also developing their skills in evaluating the validity of scientific investigation results?
options: In groups, they determine which technique is most relevant for separating the mixture and then justify their answer
In pairs, they separate the mixture using the techniques they deem suitable, and analyze to what extent it was possible to do so
Individually, they provide their previous ideas on the procedure to follow, define a collective procedure, and then implement it
Individually, they follow a procedure indicated by the teacher to separate the mixture and then identify which separation technique was used in each case

question: In which of the following situations is the pedagogical principle of empowerment favored?
options: Experiences that respond to the interests of children and consider their previous experiences, thus supporting their learning
Experiences that allow the child to learn to be, learn to think, and learn to act
Experiences that consider the styles and rhythms of learning specific to each child
Experiences that allow children to recognize their personal abilities, thus developing self-confidence to face new challenges

question: In a third-year high school class, a teacher is working on the unit of Knowledge, Science, and Truth, particularly Hume's distinction between impression and idea as sources of knowledge. Upon explaining that both types of perceptions originate from experience and constitute the foundation of all knowledge, a student questions: "But in our mind, we have some ideas of fictions that do not exist in reality and that we perceive as vividly as if they were impressions. Therefore, not all knowledge has its origin in experience."  What difficulty in understanding these concepts from Hume's epistemology can be inferred from the student's intervention?
options: He assigns a character of innateness to fictitious ideas
He applies the principles of association to ideas, and not to impressions
He locates the origin of impressions in the mind, and not in experience
He attributes a higher degree of vivacity to ideas than to impressions

question: In the context of teaching polymer chemistry in Middle School, which of the following options would be a sequence that goes from general to more particular?
options: Show examples of monomers, explain how polymers are formed through the corresponding chemical reactions, and then provide examples of natural and artificial polymers used in everyday life

Start with examples of polymers used in everyday life (natural and artificial), then explain the formation of polymers through the corresponding chemical reactions, and finally explain what monomers are

Provide examples of natural and artificial polymers that are used daily and from them identify the monomeric units; then explain the formation of these polymers through the corresponding chemical reactions

Illustrate the chemical reactions that form everyday use polymers and their monomers and then request that, by analogy with the examples, they find other everyday use polymers and develop their chemical reactions

question: Read the following situation:  Rodrigo, a preschool student attending a special language school, is characterized by being participative and restless. It is common for him not to finish tasks during classes, as he gets up from his seat, hides the school supplies of his peers, or starts doing some activity different from what the special education teacher proposed, regularly distracting himself.  Considering the described situation, in which of the following areas should support focus to reduce barriers to learning for the student?
options: Social
Health
Cognitive
Emotional

question: In the Middle School unit: Political Ethics, the teacher is going to work on the statement popularized by T. Hobbes: "homo homini lupus". Which of the following concepts should students be aware of beforehand to understand the statement made?
options: Rule of law
Social contract
Private property
State of nature

question: At the start of the unit "The breakdown of democracy and the period of the dictatorship", a teacher asks her 6th grade students to work in groups to complete a timeline with milestones they have heard that shaped the history of Chile from 1970 to 1990. While monitoring the work, she observes that one of the students is very emphatic in asking the rest of his classmates: "Okay, but then, who is to blame for the military coup?" Considering the student's question, what difficulty in learning historical processes can be anticipated?
options: The limitation of perspective to explanations focused on short duration events
The restriction of causal analysis from the perspective of the present
The lack of ability to determine historical explanations beyond the political realm
The ignorance of the multicausality of historical processes

question: Read the following situation:  Federico is a twelve-year-old student who presents with spastic tetraplegia and a pre-symbolic level of thinking. His response system is through signaling. In the subject of Natural Sciences, the classroom team is working on the following learning objective: "Observe and compare animals according to characteristics such as size, body covering, movement structures, and habitat."  Considering the above context, which of the following activities promotes Federico's communication and learning?
options: Show videos of the life forms of animals and their characteristics
Show a thematic communication board with the drawing and texture of each animal
Use animal miniatures incorporating textures and characteristics into them
Use enlarged photos of the animals in which their characteristics are identified and highlighted

question: In the High School Technology class, the teacher asks a group of students to propose a project to improve the use of water resources in the establishment, with a requirement that the proposed solution be sustainable. Which of the following sets of criteria should the teacher consider in their evaluation rubric?
options: Ergonomic, productive, and socio-environmental
Ethical, technical, and administrative
Ecological, social, and economic
Ethnic, environmental, and energetic
